title: Artificial Intelligence at Cisco – Two Current Use-Cases
summary: Cisco is leveraging artificial intelligence in two key areas: real-time translations for virtual meetings and AI-enhanced intent-based networking. Real-time translations, available in over 100 languages via Webex, aim to overcome language barriers and foster better collaboration. AI-enhanced intent-based networking uses natural language processing, machine learning, and machine reasoning to automate network management, improve reliability, and detect threats.

title: 5G Market To Witness Huge Growth By 2027 | Qualcomm, Verizon Communications, AT&T
summary: A new report by HTF Market Intelligence Consulting Pvt. Ltd. forecasts significant growth in the Global 5G Market by 2027. The comprehensive study analyzes the competitive landscape, market statistics, and qualitative information, providing insights into key business segments, end-use applications, and regional performance. Major players like Qualcomm, Verizon Communications, AT&T, and Ericsson are highlighted in this market analysis.

title: ‘Ruthless’ Vice Society claims responsibility for Spar ransomware attack
summary: The ransomware group Vice Society has claimed responsibility for an attack that disrupted card payment systems in 600 UK Spar branches, which are supplied by James Hall and Company. Known for targeting critical infrastructure like schools and hospitals, Vice Society reportedly exploited the PrintNightmare vulnerability, a common entry method for the group. The appearance of stolen data on the dark web suggests that no ransom was paid in the incident.

title: Collaboration startup Miro joins the unicorn club with $400M funding round and $17.5B valuation
summary: Miro, a collaboration startup known for its digital whiteboard, has achieved unicorn status after closing a $400 million Series C funding round, bringing its valuation to $17.5 billion. The company has seen significant user and customer growth, expanding its paid customer base from 20,000 to 130,000 and focusing on the enterprise market with new offices and increased headcount. This funding will support product development and further cultivation of its enterprise customer base.

title: Miro Raises $400M in Series C Funding Round to Accelerate Innovation Through Visual Collaboration in the New Hybrid Workplace
summary: Miro, an online platform for visual collaboration, has closed a $400M Series C funding round, bringing its total funding to $476M and valuing the company at $17.5B. This investment will fuel product development and global expansion as Miro aims to further support organizations in unlocking creativity and strengthening collaboration in the new hybrid workplace. The company has seen significant growth since its Series B round in April 2020, increasing its user base to 30M and working with 99% of Fortune 100 companies.

title: Have you modernized your critical water infrastructure?
summary: This article discusses the critical need for modernizing water infrastructure in the face of widespread drought and climate change, particularly in the United States. It highlights how smart technologies, like those offered by Cisco, can digitize water management, reduce waste, and provide real-time data for better decision-making, as demonstrated by the success story in Idaho's Water District 63. The author advocates for investments in secure Operational Technology networks to enable a broader digital transformation of public infrastructure.

title: Optical lovebirds eye a $14 billion market opportunity
summary: The merger between ADTRAN and ADVA is nearing completion, with ADTRAN shareholders approving the deal. The combined company anticipates a significant increase in its total addressable market to almost $14 billion, driven by growth opportunities in North America and Europe, and expects to boost sales by 50% in a few years. Despite the challenges of integration, both CEOs are optimistic about building a global leader in fiber networking solutions.

title: The Top Ten Asia-Pacific FCPA Enforcement Actions of 2021
summary: This article reviews the top ten Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) enforcement actions in the Asia-Pacific region during 2021, despite a general decrease in US corporate enforcement actions. It highlights three corporate cases with an Asia-Pacific nexus, several individual enforcement actions, whistleblower awards, new investigations, declinations, and DPA closures. The article also discusses relevant developments in anti-corruption efforts in China and the US, underscoring the ongoing relevance of the Asia-Pacific region in FCPA compliance.

title: Connect and Use Your OT Cybersecurity Insights with APIs
summary: This article announces updates to Cisco Cyber Vision (version 4.0), making it more developer-friendly with an updated API and new sample scripts. The enhancements include a DevNet Cyber Vision Sandbox, new learning labs, a Postman collection for the API, and three new script collections for security integration, automatic grouping, and sensor management with Ansible. These tools aim to help developers leverage industrial cybersecurity data more effectively.

title: BigPanda Raises $190 Million in Funding at $1.2 Billion Valuation
summary: BigPanda, a leader in AIOps Event Correlation and Automation, announced a new funding round of $190 million, valuing the company at $1.2 billion. The funding, led by Advent International and Insight Partners, will be used to innovate AIOps capabilities, scale go-to-market activities, and potentially make strategic acquisitions. The company has seen significant growth, with 2021 sales increasing by 155% year-over-year, reflecting the growing demand for AI-powered IT operations solutions.

title: Ten Leading Universities in China Join Cisco Networking Academy Program
summary: Cisco Systems has expanded its Networking Academy program into China by launching ten new regional academies in cooperation with ten tertiary institutions under CERNET. This initiative aims to address the shortage of skilled IT professionals in China and equip young individuals with valuable networking skills for the Internet Age. The program, known for its vendor-independent and web-based curriculum, is expected to involve over 500 schools and colleges across Asia by the end of 1999.

title: DevOps Tool Market 2022 Outstanding Growth Scope Witnessed
summary: The global DevOps Tool market is projected to reach USD 6881.6 million by 2026, growing from USD 4092.1 million in 2020 at a CAGR of 13.9% over five years. This report by Infinity Business Insights analyzes key players, market trends, and the impact of COVID-19, providing insights for investors and businesses to achieve market growth. The study covers product types like DevOps Ready, Enabled, and Capable, and applications across various sectors including IT, Telecom, BFSI, and Government.

title: Cisco CEO Says U.S. Needs to Find a Way to Coexist With China
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. CEO Chuck Robbins stated that the U.S. and Chinese governments should find a way to coexist. This recommendation comes as President Joe Biden is developing a new strategy for managing relations with China. Robbins emphasized the need to reduce tensions between the two nations.

title: Protecting Secrets / Variables Using HashiCorp Vault Secret Manager
summary: This article explains how to protect sensitive variables and secrets using HashiCorp Vault, specifically in the context of integrating Cisco ASD and Sonatype Nexus Repository. It covers the basic installation and configuration of a Vault Dev Server, demonstrates how to store secrets, and provides a Python script to retrieve them. The author emphasizes that the described method is for development and not recommended for production environments due to the use of a root token.

title: Cisco Expands Enterprise-Grade Switching Capabilities into Industrial Networks to Modernize and Secure Critical Infrastructure
summary: Cisco has announced an expansion of its Catalyst 9000 portfolio, introducing the Catalyst Industrial Ethernet 9300 switch to bring enterprise-grade switching capabilities to industrial environments. This new solution aims to improve reliability, security, and scalability for critical infrastructure sectors such as utilities, oil and gas, and transportation. The Catalyst IE9300 integrates IT and OT networks, offering advanced features like zero-trust security and high port density, enabling modernization and enhanced protection against cyber threats in operational spaces.

title: Cisco Introduces New Catalyst IE9300 switch
summary: Cisco has

title: Will 2022 be the year that SASE comes of age?
summary: The article discusses the evolution and increasing importance of Secure Access Service Edge (SASE) architecture since its inception in 2019. It highlights how SASE, which converges security and WAN infrastructure, is becoming a customer priority, especially for hybrid work environments, due to its ability to secure access to applications from any location and its zero-trust network principles. While SASE is still evolving, companies like Cisco are already offering components of this architecture, leading to measurable threat reduction.

title: Cisco’s Catalyst IE9300 powers next-gen substation automation
summary: Cisco's Catalyst IE9300 Rugged Series Switch is designed to modernize substation automation by addressing critical needs like performance at scale, cyber threat detection, and enterprise-grade management. This high-density fiber switch, along with the Catalyst IR8300 Rugged Series Router, provides a validated architecture that unifies substation LAN and WAN, enhancing performance, security, and scalability for the digitized utility grid. The solution supports industry protocols and application hosting, helping utilities improve operations and reliability while reducing risk.

title: Juniper Networks’ Next-Gen Silicon ‘Not In Same Ballpark’ As Cisco Silicon One
summary: Juniper Networks is introducing new additions to its MX Series routing portfolio, a new networking chipset generation (Trio 6), and the Juniper Express 5 silicon, directly competing with Cisco Silicon One. Speaking about the new releases, Brendan Gibbs, VP of Automated WAN Solutions at Juniper Networks, highlighted the Express 5 silicon's superior throughput of 28.8 terabits compared to Cisco's 19.2 terabits, along with better power efficiency. Gibbs also emphasized the Trio 6's optimized intelligence for multi-service edge usage, positioning Juniper's purpose-built ASICs as superior to competitors' single-chip solutions.

title: Privacy and human rights in an AI world
summary: Cisco's 2022 Data Privacy Benchmark Study reveals that data privacy is a business imperative, with 90% of respondents seeing it as crucial and unwilling to buy from organizations that don't protect data. The study, based on a survey of 4,900 security and IT professionals, also highlights concerns about AI usage, with 56% worried about how businesses use AI for automated decisions. Cisco is addressing these concerns by establishing responsible AI standards guided by principles of transparency, fairness, accountability, privacy, security, and reliability.

title: How the International Youth Foundation and Cisco Networking Academy are providing digital skills development in Mexico
summary: The International Youth Foundation (IYF) and Cisco Networking Academy are collaborating to address the digital skills gap in Mexico, particularly within the IT sector. Through this partnership, they have provided digital skills development to 15,000 students at CONALEP campuses, resulting in a job attainment rate higher than 70% for graduates. This initiative aims to equip young people with relevant skills for future workforces and potentially influence public policy for vocational schools across Mexico.

title: Juniper Guns for Cisco, Nokia With Revamped Routing Silicon
summary: Juniper Networks has announced new routing silicon, Express 5 and Trio 6, claiming superior performance and power efficiency compared to Cisco's and Nokia's offerings. The Express 5 chips, designed for core routing, offer up to 28.8 Tb/s throughput, while the Trio 6, for edge routing, features machine learning capabilities and a 70% improvement in power efficiency. Dell'Oro Group analyst Shin Umeda notes that direct comparison of ASICs is challenging due to varying vendor development cycles and deployment timelines.

title: Super Bowl LVI: SoFi Stadium’s end-to-end Cisco Network
summary: This article details how Cisco's end-to-end network powers SoFi Stadium, the venue for Super Bowl LVI. It highlights the technological innovations implemented by CTO Skarpi Hedinsson, including a hyperconverged network design, extensive virtualization, and robust security, all of which enable features like cashless payments, 100% digital signage, and instant replays for an enhanced fan experience.

title: Tibco, Citrix to merge after Citrix acquired by Vista
summary: Vista Equity Partners and Evergreen Coast Capital Corp. plan to merge Citrix and Tibco after acquiring Citrix for $16.5 billion. This merger will combine Citrix's digital workspace and app delivery platform with Tibco's data management and analytics capabilities. Analysts suggest the primary benefit for Tibco will be access to Citrix's extensive customer base, though integrating the diverse technologies and modernizing Citrix could present challenges.

title: Netgear Orbi Pro Wi-Fi 6 review: Super-fast and super pricey
summary: The Netgear Orbi Pro Wi-Fi 6 offers excellent Wi-Fi 6 performance, good range, and easy meshing for small businesses, but comes with a high price tag. While it includes valuable features like a 5-year Insight Premium subscription and robust security, its cost may deter smaller organizations that could find more affordable alternatives like the Netgear WAX630.

title: SentinelOne vs Palo Alto Networks
summary: This article compares SentinelOne and Palo Alto Networks, highlighting SentinelOne's advantages in deployment, management, AI capabilities, and cloud security. It positions SentinelOne as a leader in endpoint protection and autonomous cybersecurity, emphasizing its unified platform, real-time protection, and AI-powered solutions, contrasting them with Palo Alto Networks' perceived complexity and disjointed offerings.

title: Cisco Silicon One Powers the Next-Generation Enterprise Switches
summary: Cisco's new Catalyst 9500X and 9600X enterprise switches integrate Cisco Silicon One Q200 ASICs, extending the architecture's proven performance, efficiency, and flexibility from service providers and web-scale networks to the enterprise core. This unified architecture simplifies development, qualification, deployment, and troubleshooting, leveraging a flexible Network Processing Unit (NPU) and a shared buffer Traffic Manager to optimize diverse networking roles. The integration marks a significant step towards a converged switching and routing silicon architecture, making the new Catalyst switches powerful, power-efficient, and future-proof.

title: Aiming to power hybrid work, Cisco debuts faster Wi-Fi 6E Secure Access platform
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. has launched several innovations to its Secure Access networking platform, including new Wi-Fi 6E products, a private 5G managed service, and new Catalyst 9000X network switches. These developments are designed to enhance hybrid work environments by providing faster, more reliable, and secure connectivity. The new Wi-Fi 6E access points, like the Catalyst 9136 and Meraki MR57, offer expanded network capacity by utilizing the 6 GHz band, along with features for power management, spectrum optimization, and environmental data collection.

title: Pioneer Appoints New Chief Financial Officer
summary: Pioneer Media Holdings Inc. (CSE: PNER, AQSE: PNER) has appointed Jim MacCallum as its new Chief Financial Officer. MacCallum brings over 20 years of international financial and operational executive experience, having previously held senior finance roles at East Side Games Group, Westport Fuel Systems, Absolute Software, and Cisco Systems, Inc. Andrew Stewart has resigned from the CFO position, and the company thanked him for his service.

title: How Cisco’s Circular Design Principles are helping to minimize environmental impact
summary: Cisco is implementing Circular Design Principles to minimize environmental impact, focusing on product reuse and reduced waste. The company has launched training programs and evaluation tools to integrate these principles into product and packaging design, aiming for 100% integration by 2025. Cisco has already met its 2025 goal of reducing virgin plastic by 20% and is continuing efforts to optimize packaging and reduce virgin materials.

title: WEKA and Cisco funding contribution: Now you see it, now you don’t
summary: WEKA initially announced Cisco as an investor in its $73 million funding round, but Cisco's name was later replaced by Digital Alpha in a corrected release. Digital Alpha, founded by a former Cisco managing director, describes itself as an alternative asset manager with a strategic collaboration agreement with Cisco Systems and preferred access to Cisco’s commercial opportunities. This change highlights the close ties between Digital Alpha and Cisco, effectively positioning Digital Alpha as a Cisco investment vehicle.

title: Pegasystems releases contact center agent-assist tools
summary: Pegasystems has launched new contact center agent-assist tools, Voice AI and Messaging AI, which leverage voice recognition, natural language processing, and AI to help customer service agents respond more quickly. These tools, based on the acquisition of Qurious.io, include automated data entry, contextual workflow launches, and compliance prompts. Experts suggest these features are robust and ready for deployment despite Pegasystems being a later entrant in the agent-assist market.

title: Cisco Systems Inc. stock underperforms Wednesday when compared to competitors despite daily gains
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (CSCO) stock increased by 1.72% on Wednesday, closing at $56.29, despite underperforming compared to some competitors. While the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw gains, Cisco's stock remained $8.00 short of its 52-week high. Competitors such as Microsoft Corp. (MSFT), International Business Machines Corp. (IBM), and VMware Inc. (VMW) all experienced greater percentage gains on the same day.

title: Cisco Has Discussed Deal for Software Company Splunk (Published 2022)
summary: Cisco Systems has held discussions with the software company Splunk about a potential acquisition, though no deal is currently solid. If talks resume, Cisco could acquire Splunk for over $20 billion, significantly higher than Splunk's current market capitalization of around $18 billion. This potential acquisition aligns with Cisco's strategy of growth through mergers and acquisitions, particularly in the security sector.

title: Cisco made $20 billion-plus takeover offer for Splunk -WSJ
summary: Cisco Systems Inc reportedly made a takeover offer worth more than $20 billion for software maker Splunk Inc, according to the Wall Street Journal. The offer was made recently, but the companies are not currently in active talks. Splunk, with a market capitalization of $18.2 billion, recently saw its CEO step down.

title: Cisco Systems makes billion dollar offer to acquire Splunk
summary: Cisco Systems reportedly made a takeover offer worth over $20 billion for software maker Splunk, according to The Wall Street Journal. The companies are currently not in active talks regarding the offer. This news follows a recent change in leadership at Splunk in November.

title: Will Cisco Buy Splunk? Big Tech Bids $20 Billion on Public Company
summary: Cisco Systems has reportedly offered $20 billion to acquire software company Splunk, aiming to expand its presence in the software industry. The potential acquisition faces regulatory hurdles and comes as Splunk navigates a leadership transition and business model shifts. Both companies' stocks reacted differently to the news, with Cisco's stock slightly down and Splunk's significantly up due to investor sentiment regarding financial backing.

title: The key to AI success? Ethics
summary: Cisco has developed a Responsible Artificial Intelligence Framework to ensure ethical AI use, addressing concerns about data privacy, bias, and transparency. This framework, guided by principles of Transparency, Fairness, Accountability, Privacy, Security, and Reliability, aims to mitigate challenges and build trust in AI technologies. The company emphasizes that ethical AI, much like privacy, is becoming a crucial business imperative that will shape industry standards and government regulations.

title: Juniper acquires zero-trust security startup WiteSand
summary: Juniper Networks has acquired WiteSand, a zero-trust security startup, to integrate its cloud-native access control technology into Juniper's Mist network management platform. This acquisition aims to enhance security within Mist by improving device onboarding, access policy enforcement, and threat response. The move also positions Juniper to offer a more complete and automated security architecture, similar to competitors like Cisco, and potentially integrate WiteSand's capabilities into its SASE portfolio.

title: Nano Dimension’s Board of Directors Welcomes Igal Rotem
summary: Nano Dimension Ltd., a leader in Additively Manufactured Electronics (AME) and other printing technologies, has appointed Igal Rotem to its Board of Directors. Rotem brings extensive entrepreneurial experience, including founding PowerDsine and leading Finaro Bank to profitability, and will replace Dr. Eli David, who remains as CTO. Yoav Stern, Nano Dimension’s CEO, expressed confidence that Rotem's diverse background will significantly benefit the company's board and growth strategy.

title: Disaggregating Memory May be the Most Exciting Trend for Computing System Design Right Now
summary: This article discusses the trend of disaggregating memory in computing systems, comparing it to the earlier disaggregation of storage. The author argues that decoupling memory from CPUs can lead to greater efficiency, allowing memory to be pooled and shared across multiple hosts and enabling new innovations like memory tiering. This shift aims to solve current limitations by eliminating pre-sized memory requirements and freeing up space for more processing power, ultimately making systems more compute-intensive and operationally efficient.

title: Cisco Systems Inc. Q2 Profit Increases, beats estimates
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (CSCO) reported a significant increase in its second-quarter profit compared to last year, surpassing Street estimates. The company's earnings and revenue both rose, with adjusted earnings per share exceeding analyst expectations. Cisco also provided optimistic guidance for its next quarter and full-year EPS.

title: IBM exec referred to older workers as ‘dinobabies,’ suit says
summary: A lawsuit against IBM has revealed email communications where a high-level executive referred to older workers as "dinobabies" and expressed intent to usher them out to "accelerate change." The suit claims IBM had a "top-down plan to retain younger workers" to improve its demographic makeup. This incident highlights ongoing ageism concerns within IBM and the broader tech industry.

title: The Great Resignation — calamity or opportunity?
summary: The "Great Resignation" has seen millions of Americans quit their jobs since spring 2021, leading many companies to scramble for talent. Cisco views this phenomenon more as a "Great Migration" or "Great Upgrade," driven by the rise of hybrid work and increased talent mobility. The company is adapting its hiring practices to leverage a wider talent pool, offering flexibility and advanced collaboration tools, and emphasizing its purpose-driven culture to attract and retain employees.

title: Energy Management System Market to register a CAGR of 14.8%
summary: A new report by Future Market Insights projects the global energy management system market to reach a CAGR of 14.8% from 2017 to 2025, valuing at US$ 6340 Mn in 2016. Key drivers include increasing demand for energy management solutions in business sectors, rising energy costs, and growing regulatory commitments. The market is segmented by deployment (on-premise and cloud-based), vertical (oil & gas, building automation, etc.), and software (utility, industrial, enterprise carbon, and residential energy management).

title: Cisco and Raymond James to Host Internet for the Future Tech Talk
summary: Cisco will host a Tech Talk with Raymond James on February 28, 2022, to discuss its Internet for the Future Business and its role in 5G and IoT. Jonathan Davidson, Executive Vice President and General Manager of Cisco’s Mass-Scale Infrastructure Group, will answer questions during the session broadcast from Mobile World Congress, Barcelona. Interested parties can find more information on Cisco's Investor Relations website.

title: These are the best employers offering hybrid work in Toronto
summary: Great Place To Work, in partnership with Microsoft, has released its list of top hybrid employers for 2022, recognizing companies that have successfully adapted to a mix of remote and office work. This article specifically highlights the Toronto-based companies that made this prestigious list. To qualify, employers needed to be Great Place To Work certified within the last year.

title: Former Cisco Engineer Theodore Gates Joins NewWave as Cyber Business Development Director
summary: Theodore Gates, formerly a senior security engineer at Cisco Systems' U.S. public sector segment, has been appointed as NewWave's director of cybersecurity business development. Gates will help NewWave in establishing a zero trust security architectural practice. His experience includes work with the National Institute of Standards and Technology and the Federal Executive CIO Council, and he currently serves as the cybersecurity communications chair of the American Council for Technology and Industry Advisory Council.

title: Threat Advisory: HermeticWiper
summary: Cisco Talos has issued a threat advisory regarding "HermeticWiper," a destructive malware deployed in a second wave of attacks in Ukraine. The wiper, which started deployment on Feb. 23, 2022, shares behavioral characteristics with the WhisperGate attacks and targets both the Master Boot Record and partitions for destruction. Recent updates indicate additional components like HermeticWizard for propagation, IsaacWiper for further destruction, and HermeticRansom as a potential diversionary tactic.

title: Threat Advisory: Cyclops Blink
summary: Cisco Talos has issued a threat advisory regarding Cyclops Blink, a new modular malware family targeting small office/home office (SOHO) devices, similar to VPNFilter. The malware runs on Linux systems, specifically 32-bit PowerPC architecture, and is designed for reconnaissance and espionage, leveraging modules for C2 communication, file transfer, and information extraction. The advisory details the malware's components, its ability to masquerade as a kernel process, and ways Cisco security products can detect and block this threat.

title: Q&A on the MITRE D3FEND Framework
summary: This article features an interview with Pete Kaloroumakis from MITRE, discussing the D3FEND framework, a cybersecurity knowledge base focusing on defensive countermeasures. Kaloroumakis explains how D3FEND complements MITRE's ATT&CK framework by mapping defensive techniques to digital artifacts instead of direct attack-to-defense mappings. He also touches on the framework's development, its beta status, target audience, and how vendors can contribute and leverage it to articulate their product capabilities more clearly.

title: Qualcomm Inc. stock rises Friday, still underperforms market
summary: Qualcomm Inc. (QCOM) stock rose 1.76% on Friday, closing at $170.93, despite underperforming the broader market. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average both saw larger gains. Qualcomm's stock remains below its 52-week high and showed mixed performance compared to competitors like Alphabet Inc. Cl A, NVIDIA Corp., and Cisco Systems Inc.

title: Cisco Helps Usher in New Era for Mass #IoT
summary: Cisco has announced advancements to its Internet of Things (IoT) portfolio, particularly enhancing its IoT Control Center platform, to simplify and manage LPWAN/4G/5G IoT connectivity for new and emerging use cases, especially for Mass IoT devices. This update allows service providers to offer a more profitable and practical solution for connecting low-cost, often-stationary devices at massive scale, supporting a broad range of industries and use cases. The new subscription-based offering helps reduce connectivity management complexity and costs, facilitating new revenue streams for service providers.

title: Cisco Brings the Promise of Private 5G to Enterprises, Together with Global Partners
summary: Cisco announced its go-to-market strategy for Cisco Private 5G, partnering with global service providers and system integrators to offer private 5G as-a-service. This solution aims to reduce technical, financial, and operational risks for enterprises looking to leverage mass-scale IoT adoption. Cisco Private 5G complements existing Wi-Fi infrastructure, uses a pay-as-you-use subscription model, and integrates ORAN technology, partnering with vendors like Airspan and JMA.

title: Top Companies for Customer Satisfaction
summary: Cisco Systems, Agilent Technologies, and IBM consistently rank highest in customer satisfaction according to the Management Top 250 ranking. This ranking, developed by the Drucker Institute, assesses corporate effectiveness across five categories: customer satisfaction, employee engagement and development, innovation, social responsibility, and financial strength. Cisco Systems notably appears in the top 10 for two of these categories and is recognized as an "All-Star" for excelling in all five.

title: Cisco CEO says quarter of staff in Ukraine have left
summary: Cisco Systems Inc's CEO Chuck Robbins stated that approximately a quarter of its employees in Ukraine have left the country with the company's assistance following the Russian invasion. Cisco is also providing support to employees who chose to remain, including help with accessing cash. The company confirmed it would adhere to all sanctions imposed on Russia.

title: Cisco CEO says quarter of staff in Ukraine have left
summary: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins announced that approximately a quarter of the company's employees in Ukraine have left the country since the Russian invasion, with Cisco providing assistance for their departure. The company is actively working to support those who chose to remain and is also ensuring compliance with sanctions imposed by the United States and other nations against Russia. Robbins also expressed confidence in the opportunity in standalone 5G networks, especially for enterprise services.

title: Cisco explores ways to sweeten pay as it hunts for 5,000 more workers
summary: Cisco Systems is exploring ways to increase employee compensation, including enhancing cash pay, as it seeks to hire 5,000 new workers. This move comes as other major tech companies are also re-evaluating their compensation strategies amidst a declining stock market and increased competition for talent. This strategy aims to attract and retain top talent in a competitive tech landscape.

title: Cisco stands on guard with our customers in Ukraine
summary: Cisco is actively protecting its customers in Ukraine against cyberattacks amidst the ongoing invasion. Cisco Talos is providing 24/7 security product operations for critical customers and utilizing open-source intelligence to develop Ukraine-specific protections. The company has extended security licenses for all its Ukrainian customers and is offering advanced threat hunting services to critical organizations at no cost, demonstrating a commitment to supporting the region's digital infrastructure.

title: Cisco stands on guard with our customers in Ukraine
summary: Cisco is actively protecting its customers in Ukraine during the Russia-led invasion by directly operating security products 24/7 for critical clients and leveraging over 500 employees for open-source intelligence collection. The company is developing Ukraine-specific protections and aggressively monitoring threats to safeguard both Ukrainian and global customers. This effort highlights Cisco's rapid and creative response to an unprecedented crisis, emphasizing the dedication of its employees.

title: GLOBALFOUNDRIES ANNOUNCES NEXT GENERATION IN SILICON PHOTONICS SOLUTIONS AND COLLABORATES WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS TO ADVANCE A NEW ERA OF MORE IN THE DATA CENTER
summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) has announced GF Fotonix™, its next-generation silicon photonics platform, aimed at addressing the explosive growth of data and reducing power consumption in data centers. GF is collaborating with industry leaders including Broadcom, Cisco Systems, Inc., Marvell, and NVIDIA, along with photonic innovators like Ayar Labs and PsiQuantum, to deliver innovative and feature-rich solutions. This monolithic platform, combining advanced photonics features and RF-CMOS on a single silicon wafer, is designed to enable faster, more energy-efficient data transmission and supports various high-growth markets.

title: Cloudflare and Akamai to continue operations in Russia
summary: Cloudflare and Akamai have stated their intention to continue operations in Russia despite calls to cease business following the invasion of Ukraine. Both companies argue that providing internet access is crucial for Russian citizens to obtain accurate information, rather than contributing to an information blackout. They have, however, condemned the invasion and ceased business with sanctioned entities or state-majority-owned customers.

title: Cisco Stops Business In Russia, Ups Security Efforts In Ukraine
summary: Cisco Systems has halted all business operations in Russia and Belarus in response to Russia's invasion of Ukraine. The company is also providing security assistance to Ukraine through its Talos threat intelligence team, offering free software auto-renewals, Webex meetings, and calls to the country. This move aligns Cisco with other major tech companies sanctioning Russia, and the company has also established a humanitarian fund for Ukraine.

title: Swap Into Arista Stock From Cisco, Analyst Advises Networking Investors
summary: A Wells Fargo analyst recommended that investors in communications-hardware stocks shift their focus from value to growth. The analyst, Aaron Rakers, upgraded Arista Networks stock while simultaneously downgrading Cisco Systems, suggesting a strategic swap for networking investors.

title: Maximus Hires Liz Anthony as VP of Federal Market & Competition Strategy
summary: Maximus has appointed Liz Anthony as Vice President for Federal Market & Competition Strategy. Anthony, who has nearly two decades of marketing leadership experience with companies like Cisco Systems and NetApp, will focus on driving Maximus's growth through technology, consulting, and depth of expertise. Her appointment is expected to bolster Maximus's standing as a federal technology and consulting leader, reinforcing its commitment to government and citizen relationships.

title: IDC MarketScape positions Cloudflare as a Leader among worldwide Commercial CDN providers
summary: Cloudflare has been recognized as a Leader in the IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Commercial CDN 2022 Vendor Assessment. The report highlights Cloudflare's continuous innovation, integrated SASE offering, and the strength of its Cloudflare Workers platform. The company's global network, including its partnership in mainland China, and unified architectural approach were also key factors in its leadership position.

title: NetApp and Cisco Strengthen Partnership Announcing New FlexPod XCS to Extend Converged Infrastructure into the Hybrid Cloud
summary: NetApp and Cisco have announced FlexPod XCS, an evolution of their converged infrastructure solution, designed to enable hybrid cloud connectivity and automation. This platform is the first and only hybrid cloud solution natively integrated across all three major public cloud providers, offering increased accessibility, scalability, and an optional pay-as-you-grow service model. FlexPod XCS aims to simplify hybrid cloud operations and accelerate the delivery of modern applications and data.

title: NetApp, Cisco Bring FlexPod To Hybrid Cloud, As-A-Service
summary: NetApp and Cisco have significantly enhanced their FlexPod converged technology with the introduction of FlexPod XCS, designed for hybrid clouds and integrated with three major hyperscalers through Cisco Intersight. This new offering also enables solution providers to deliver FlexPod-as-a-Service, catering to customer demand for consumption-based pricing models in converged infrastructure. FlexPod XCS focuses on increased visibility, automation, hybrid cloud services, and a consumption model, building on 11 years of FlexPod success with over $16 billion in sales.

title: Spectrum Enterprise Launches Enterprise Network Edge Solution
summary: Spectrum Enterprise, a division of Charter Communications, announced the launch of Enterprise Network Edge (ENE), a new solution aimed at simplifying the management and migration of complex, large-scale networks for businesses. ENE integrates advanced security and SD-WAN capabilities, leveraging Fortinet Security-driven Networking technologies, to provide flexible and secure WAN and LAN services. The solution is designed to support hybrid WAN configurations and offers features like virtual edge, remote access, and LAN management, along with 24/7 US-based support, to help organizations modernize their networks at their own pace.

title: Nvidia debuts next-generation H100 GPUs, its most powerful chips yet
summary: Nvidia has unveiled its next-generation H100 GPUs, built on the new Hopper architecture, which promise an order of magnitude performance improvement over previous models. These chips feature 80 billion transistors, a Transformer Engine for accelerating AI language models, and HBM3 memory. The H100 GPUs are designed for advanced AI workloads, including real-time inference, and will be available to customers and cloud providers in the third quarter.

title: F5 Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
summary: F5 Inc. (FFIV) stock increased by 1.63% to $209.49 on a strong trading day, outperforming competitors like Cisco Systems Inc. and Juniper Networks Inc. This marked the stock's sixth consecutive day of gains, although its trading volume was below its 50-day average. The broader market also saw an uplift, with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average rising.

title: Transitioning to IPv6 for Simplicity, Efficiency, and Modernization
summary: This article discusses the urgent need for transitioning from IPv4 to IPv6 due to the increasing number of connected devices and the limitations of IPv4's address space. It highlights how techniques like NAT, while extending IPv4's life, introduce complexities in network management and security. The article emphasizes that IPv6 is now a requirement for modern infrastructure and digital transformation, with Cisco itself acting as "Customer Zero" by actively transitioning its internal networks to IPv6-only environments.

title: 10 Servers With AMD EPYC Milan-X New Server CPUs
summary: AMD has launched its third-generation EPYC processors with 3-D V-Cache technology, code-named Milan-X, which is the world's first data center CPU using 3-D die stacking. This innovation allows for 768 MB of L3 cache, significantly enhancing performance for technical computing workloads like FEA, EDA, and CFD. Major server vendors such as Dell Technologies, HPE, Cisco, Lenovo, and Supermicro are rolling out new or upgraded servers supporting these processors, aiming to offer improved performance, lower total cost of ownership, and reduced power consumption.

title: Top 3 Use Cases of AI-Enhanced Networking
summary: This article discusses three key use cases for AI-enhanced networking that can benefit NetOps teams across various industries. These include AI-driven issue identification, improving wireless performance, and enhanced endpoint analytics. The author emphasizes that leveraging AI/ML capabilities helps address the increasing complexity of network operations, reduces human error, and enhances security.

title: Cisco Systems Inc. stock rises Friday, outperforms market
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (CSCO) saw its stock rise by 0.86% to $54.97 on Friday, outperforming the broader market. This marked the second consecutive day of gains for the company, though its stock remained $9.32 short of its 52-week high. Competitors Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) fell, while International Business Machines Corp. (IBM) and VMware Inc. (VMW) also saw gains.

title: Cisco Systems Stock Looks Ready to Bounce Off Bullish Trendline
summary: Cisco Systems (CSCO) stock appears poised for a bounce after cooling off from its all-time high, currently trading around $55.28. The equity has pulled back to a historically bullish trendline, specifically its 320-day moving average, which has previously preceded significant rallies. Additionally, an unwinding of pessimism in the options market, indicated by a high put/call volume ratio and attractively priced premiums, could provide further tailwinds for the stock.

title: Cisco: Careers Review
summary: This article provides an in-depth review of career opportunities at Cisco, a global technology company known for networking and data storage, now focusing on hybrid cloud. It covers Cisco's work culture, employee benefits, commitment to diversity, and various awards and recognitions as an employer. The review also details job openings and salary expectations for different roles within the company.

title: Cisco Systems Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (CSCO) saw its stock rise by 0.68% to $56.05 on a generally down day for the stock market, outperforming the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average. This marked the fifth consecutive day of gains for Cisco, although its stock remained below its 52-week high. The company also outperformed some direct competitors like Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) and VMware Inc. (VMW).

title: Cisco SD-Access in Healthcare: A Comprehensive Secure Access Solution for a Changing Industry
summary: The article discusses how Cisco SD-Access provides a comprehensive secure access solution for the rapidly changing healthcare industry. It addresses challenges like preventing security breaches, protecting patient data, and supporting technological innovations with a resilient, automated, and secure network architecture. Cisco SD-Access simplifies network expansion, ensures high availability, facilitates secure segmentation, and offers rich network services and improved visibility for effective management in healthcare settings.

title: DDoS attacks becoming larger and more complex, finance most targeted sector
summary: A report by F5 Labs indicates that while the overall volume of DDoS attacks decreased slightly in 2021, their size and complexity significantly increased, with the finance sector being the most frequently targeted industry. The mean attack size quadrupled since early 2020, and new records for the largest attacks were set multiple times last year. Multi-vectored attacks and the use of more sophisticated methods highlight a growing challenge for cybersecurity defenders.

title: AMD-Pensando Buy: From AWS Rivalry To Cisco, HPE Links—5 Things To Know
summary: AMD is acquiring edge computing startup Pensando for $1.9 billion to enhance its data center offerings and expand its capabilities for cloud, enterprise, and edge customers. Pensando, known for its programmable processor and software stack, boasts a strong market presence, including partnerships with HPE and Oracle, and aims to challenge AWS's cloud technology. This acquisition is expected to accelerate innovation in distributed services and solidify AMD's position in the next generation of data center infrastructure.

title: AMD Buying John Chambers-Backed Pensando For $1.9B To Boost Cloud, Chip Innovation
summary: AMD announced its acquisition of Pensando, an edge and cloud startup, for $1.9 billion to enhance its data center processors with Pensando's software stack and programmable packet processors. This strategic move aims to expand AMD's offerings for cloud, enterprise, and edge customers by leveraging Pensando's distributed services platform, which has been praised for its performance and efficiency. Pensando's CEO Prem Jain and his team will join AMD's Data Center Solutions Group, continuing to execute their product roadmap with increased scale and market reach.

title: An anthropologist’s eye on the future of work
summary: Lizbet Simmons, a senior anthropological and futurist researcher at Cisco, brings a human-first approach to innovation, focusing on understanding fundamental human needs and values to anticipate future challenges and societal shifts. She emphasizes that successful hybrid work depends on both technological advancements and cultural changes that foster trust, flexibility, and a sense of belonging. Simmons is optimistic about Cisco's ability to power an inclusive future by aligning technology with human values and fostering a conscious company culture.

title: The Wait is Over for Secure Firewall 3100 Series
summary: Cisco has launched its new mid-range Secure Firewall 3100 Series, designed to offer superior performance, especially with encrypted traffic, and improved cost-effectiveness. This new hardware builds on previous software optimizations like Threat Defense 7.0 and features an industry-first in-path crypto acceleration capability and a new patented Flow Offload engine. The article emphasizes its high throughput and stateful scalability, positioning it as a significant upgrade from older models.

title: Martin Nystrom named VP, Security Development at Lumen Technologies
summary: Martin Nystrom, a cybersecurity veteran, has been appointed Vice President of Security Development at Lumen Technologies. In this role, Nystrom will oversee all security-related products and services and lead the Black Lotus Labs threat intelligence team. His extensive experience includes 21 years at Cisco, where he developed web security principles, founded a global security operations center, and launched the SecureX extended detection and response platform.

title: Is QQQ A Good Buy For Dividend Portfolios? Why There Are Better Choices
summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust, Series 1 ETF (QQQ) has seen strong capital appreciation in recent years, largely due to factors like ultra-loose monetary policy and pandemic tailwinds. However, these factors are unlikely to persist, making QQQ less suitable for income-focused dividend portfolios. The author suggests investors might find better income opportunities by selecting individual stocks.

title: Cisco launches circular IT payment solution to support customer sustainability goals
summary: Cisco has launched "Cisco Green Pay," a new payment solution designed to support customers' sustainability goals by facilitating the circular use of its technologies. This program offers a 5% incentive on Cisco hardware, predictable five-year payments, and free product returns, aiming to accelerate Cisco's commitment to 100% product return and net-zero emissions by 2040. The initiative helps customers build sustainable IT strategies and contributes to the circular economy.

title: Arista Networks Inc. stock falls Monday, underperforms market
summary: Arista Networks Inc. (ANET) stock dropped 2.95% on Monday, closing at $127.16, underperforming the broader market. The S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw declines on the same day. This marked the second consecutive day of losses for Arista Networks, bringing its stock $21.41 below its 52-week high.

title: NVIDIA enters Top 5 of Fortune's '100 Best Companies to Work For'
summary: NVIDIA has been ranked #5 on Fortune's 2022 list of the "100 Best Companies to Work For," placing ahead of major financial institutions like Capital One and American Express. Cisco secured the top spot for the second consecutive year, followed by Hilton and Wegmans Food Markets, Inc., with Salesforce coming in fourth. The recognition highlights the importance of strong workplace culture during a period of significant workforce changes.

title: Announcing Cisco Nexus Dashboard Open Ecosystem Expansion with ServiceNow
summary: Cisco has announced new operations tools for its Nexus Dashboard in partnership with ServiceNow, enhancing hybrid-cloud operations. This expansion includes the Nexus Dashboard Connector for ServiceNow and the Cisco Cloud Network Insights App, building on the Nexus Dashboard 2.1's open APIs. These integrations aim to automate IT operations, improve incident management, and facilitate self-healing networks for multi-cloud environments.

title: Cisco to Shift Some Pay From Bonuses to Salaries Amid Inflation Crunch
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is adjusting employee compensation by shifting some pay from bonuses to base salaries, a move driven by employee feedback and soaring inflation. This change, effective at the start of its new fiscal year in August, aims to provide workers with more immediate "cash in hand" to address rising living costs. The company is also exploring a four-day workweek and reducing its office footprint by up to 30%.

title: Oracle Database 19c on Cisco X210c M6 – WOW!
summary: This article details the performance testing of Oracle Database 19c on Cisco X210c M6 compute nodes. Using Oracle SLOB and SwingBench benchmarks, it demonstrates excellent scalability, low latency, and high transaction rates, highlighting the suitability of the Cisco UCS X210c M6 for hosting single-instance Oracle databases. The article also addresses performance impact during disk failure, showing minimal disruption and efficient recovery through Oracle ASM.

title: An always-on strategy to find extraordinary talent for CX Centers Americas
summary: Cisco's CX Centers Americas has implemented a comprehensive, "always-on" strategy to recruit exceptional talent, particularly for its specialized Technical Consulting Engineer (TCE) roles. This strategy involves partnerships with universities and community colleges, offering various programs like the TCE Graduate Program, Co-Op/Internships, and Apprenticeships. The goal is to provide robust training and career paths for emerging talent and experienced professionals alike, ensuring a continuous supply of skilled individuals to deliver extraordinary customer experiences.

title: Cisco and General Motors Combine Forces for the Future of Connectivity in Cars
summary: Cisco and General Motors are collaborating to modernize vehicle performance testing using Cisco’s wireless backhaul technology. GM is the first auto manufacturer to deploy this system, which enables real-time data analysis during pre-production vehicle testing, significantly reducing development time for electric and autonomous vehicles. This innovation allows engineers to instantly monitor hundreds of data channels and adjust tests on the fly, streamlining operations and accelerating time-to-market.

title: Arista Networks Inc. stock falls Wednesday, underperforms market
summary: Arista Networks Inc. (ANET) stock fell 1.30% on Wednesday to $127.16, underperforming the broader market. The stock closed $21.41 below its 52-week high and also trailed competitors like Microsoft, Cisco Systems, and HP Inc., which all saw gains. Trading volume for Arista Networks exceeded its 50-day average.

title: How to make threat hunting feasible, Part I: Detection
summary: This article explains the initial steps of making threat hunting feasible, focusing on detection. It uses a hypothetical scenario of a banking application developer falling victim to a cyberattack to illustrate the "kill chain" model and the importance of early detection. The piece highlights the role of threat intelligence, threat hunting methodologies, and how automation tools like Cisco SecureX can streamline the process of identifying indicators of compromise from various sources, including social media.

title: Cisco’s green buildings: Good for the planet and our well-being
summary: Cisco is focusing on green buildings for environmental sustainability and employee well-being, highlighting features like efficient resource use, renewable energy, and improved indoor air quality. By pursuing WELL Health-Safety Rating, WELL Building Standard, and LEED certifications, Cisco aims to create high-performing, energy-efficient spaces that positively impact its occupants. The company's efforts include notable projects in NYC, Atlanta, and San Jose, reflecting a commitment to expanding its green building portfolio.

title: FlexPod XCS Review
summary: Cisco and NetApp have announced FlexPod XCS, an updated version of their converged infrastructure platform designed for hybrid cloud environments. XCS integrates Cisco’s Intersight Platform with NetApp’s ONTAP software, offering full-stack visibility, end-to-end automation, and native integration with major cloud providers. This refresh aims to better position FlexPod for modern enterprise storage needs, building on its existing capabilities and introducing a pay-as-you-go model.

title: Workforce Transition in the Mining Industry
summary: The mining industry is facing a significant workforce transition due to an aging employee base, with approximately 25% of employees over 55. This shift is being leveraged for growth through increased digitization, big data analytics, and automation, leading to new job roles in areas like process automation and AI. Cisco's technology and communication infrastructure support this transition by facilitating digital knowledge capture, providing modern tools for new employees, and enabling inclusive collaboration across diverse workforces.

title: PAN-OS 10.2 Nebula Is a Monumental Leap Forward
summary: Palo Alto Networks has released PAN-OS 10.2 Nebula, a significant update to its cybersecurity operating system, which introduces inline deep learning for the first time. This new capability aims to help organizations proactively stop highly evasive cyber threats by analyzing live traffic through multi-layer artificial neural networks. The release focuses on advanced machine learning, broad coverage across evasive attack vectors, and automated intelligent management across security tools, signifying a "monumental leap forward" in threat prevention.

title: Nvidia’s CFO on how they retain tech talent —even as the Great Resignation continues
summary: Colette Kress, Nvidia's CFO, discusses the company's strategies for retaining top tech talent amidst the Great Resignation. She emphasizes that beyond competitive salaries, a strong culture focused on continuous learning, challenging work, and an egalitarian leadership approach are key factors. Nvidia provides unique upskilling opportunities, offers benefits like "Free Days" and an employee stock purchase program, and fosters an environment where all employees are valued and have opportunities to lead, regardless of title.

title: Fortinet Inc. stock falls Friday, underperforms market
summary: Fortinet Inc. (FTNT) stock dropped 6.36% on Friday, closing at $289.01, underperforming the broader market. The S&P 500 Index fell 3.63% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 2.77%. Fortinet's stock also underperformed competitors like Cisco Systems Inc. and HP Inc., and closed significantly below its 52-week high.

title: Michael O'Donnell named Chairman & CEO at Relay Robotics, Inc.
summary: Relay Robotics, Inc., formed from the acquisition of Savioke, announced a $10 million Series A financing round and appointed Michael O'Donnell as Chairman and CEO. Savioke Co-Founder Steve Cousins will transition to Chief Technology Officer. O'Donnell brings 25 years of experience in the consumer technology industry and a strong track record of success at early-stage companies.

title: ISE business value and ROI uncovered in Forrester study
summary: A commissioned study by Forrester Consulting, "The Total Economic Impact™ of Cisco Identity Services Engine (ISE)," revealed that Cisco ISE offers a 191% ROI over three years with an 11-month payback period. The study highlights significant business value by improving security, reducing IT costs through automation, and enhancing business outcomes like employee productivity. ISE's benefits stem from decreased security incidents, reduced need for additional IT staff, and faster, more secure access to network resources.

title: New Cisco Tech to Predict Network Issues
summary: Cisco is introducing new predictive technology to help IT teams prevent network issues before they occur. This innovation aims to improve network reliability and performance by using advanced analytics and machine learning to identify and avoid disruptions. Early customer trials have shown high accuracy in predicting problems, leading to significant time and cost savings.

title: Cisco unveils technology to predict network issues
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. has introduced new predictive software designed to prevent network issues before they occur. This technology gathers data to predict hardware problems, bandwidth spikes, and app configuration changes, aiming to improve network reliability. Cisco plans to integrate this predictive engine into a broad range of its products and services over the next few years.

title: Cisco Releases Its Cloud Controls Framework To The Public, Making Security Certification Ever More Accessible
summary: Cisco has publicly released its Cloud Controls Framework (CCF) to help organizations achieve cloud security certifications more efficiently. This framework consolidates international and national security compliance requirements into a "build-once-use-many" approach, significantly streamlining the process and reducing resources needed for certification. The CCF aims to ease compliance challenges, promote scalability for cloud providers, and maintain transparency in security practices.

title: Why I joined Cloudflare in Latin America
summary: Carlos Torales announces his new role as Vice President and Managing Director for Latin America at Cloudflare, detailing his motivations, which include Cloudflare's mission to build a better internet and its significant potential in the region. He emphasizes Latin America's digital transformation, Cloudflare's existing impact, and future growth opportunities, highlighting the company's role in providing security, speed, and reliability to businesses and users across the region. Torales also mentions job openings and opportunities for partnership.

title: Ex-Cisco chief John Chambers says Silicon Valley's in trouble, but it's not too late to fix things
summary: Former Cisco Systems CEO John Chambers believes Silicon Valley and the U.S. are entering one of their most difficult periods in his 46-year tech career. Despite the challenges, Chambers remains optimistic that solutions can be found. He shared these views with the Silicon Valley Business Journal, highlighting concerns for the region and the nation.

title: Cisco rethinks office space design to optimize for hybrid work
summary: Cisco has redesigned its 42,000-square-foot Penn1 office in Manhattan to optimize for hybrid work, shifting from 70% individual work to 70% collaboration space. The new facility, which also serves as a showcase for customers, incorporates sustainability, employee well-being, and digitization, with over 5,000 data points collected from various devices to understand space usage. Cisco also addressed challenges like hot-desking and native interoperability for meeting platforms, acknowledging the need for partners to help tailor solutions for other businesses.

title: Qualcomm Inc. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors despite daily gains
summary: Qualcomm Inc. (QCOM) stock rose 1.40% on Tuesday, closing at $136.81, despite a mixed trading session for the broader market. This gain, however, meant the stock underperformed some competitors like Alphabet Inc. and NVIDIA Corp., and remained significantly below its 52-week high.

title: Network Footprints of Gamaredon Group
summary: This article by Onur Mustafa Erdogan details the network infrastructure and tooling of the Gamaredon Group (also known as Primitive Bear, Shuckworm, and ACTINIUM), an advanced persistent threat (APT) originating from Russia. The research, based on observations from March 2022, analyzes their domain registration patterns, TLD usage, and IP address distribution across various ASNs, primarily REG.RU. It also identifies their preferred malware, Pterodo and Groooboor, typically delivered via macro-enabled office files, and explains how Cisco Global Threat Alerts detects their activity.

title: Zoom to Acquire Solvvy and Advance the Next Frontier of Customer Service
summary: Zoom has announced its agreement to acquire Solvvy, a conversational AI and automation platform for customer support. This acquisition aims to accelerate Zoom's Contact Center roadmap, redefine customer experience by integrating Solvvy's AI and machine learning technology, and provide businesses with scalable self-service and conversational AI to improve customer retention and operational efficiency. The companies expect this collaboration to deliver elevated customer service experiences globally and unlock new opportunities for personalized, video-based service.

title: Cisco PENN1 – Making hybrid work, work for offices
summary: Cisco has transformed its PENN1 office space in New York City into a showcase for hybrid work, utilizing its smart building solutions developed over a decade. By integrating various building systems into a unified network, Cisco has created a collaborative environment designed for flexibility and enhanced user experience. This retrofit, leveraging technologies like Catalyst 9000, Cisco DNA Spaces, and Webex, simplifies management, reduces operational costs, and supports diverse collaboration needs.

title: Here's why Pensando agreed to $1.9B AMD deal, according to Chairman John Chambers
summary: Pensando's acquisition by AMD for $1.9 billion helped solve a significant supply chain issue for the rapidly growing Milpitas-based company, according to its chairman, John Chambers. Chambers, the former CEO of Cisco Systems, indicated that the deal addressed a critical challenge Pensando was facing. This strategic acquisition points to AMD's broader focus on data centers.

title: NEC places itself at the heart of the open ecosystem as network integrator at LATAM Telco Vision Forum
summary: NEC hosted the Latam Telco Vision Forum to address the challenges and opportunities presented by 5G. During the event, NEC highlighted its "Open Networks" strategy, which aims to provide flexible, agile, and cost-optimized solutions for operators by leveraging open architecture, virtualization, and cloud solutions, in collaboration with a diverse ecosystem of partners. The company showcased successful 5G applications in Japan that demonstrate increased productivity, labor optimization, and cost reduction.

title: Cisco Stock Tumbles. What to Know.
summary: Cisco Systems' stock is falling due to disappointing financial results. The company attributes its revenue shortfall to ongoing supply-chain issues, despite claiming strong demand for its networking hardware. This situation highlights a complex scenario within the current tech business landscape.

title: Modernizing Building Management Systems at the University of Notre Dame
summary: The University of Notre Dame partnered with Cisco and Schneider Electric to modernize its building management systems, aiming to reduce its carbon footprint and operating costs by 2030. The project involved upgrading to Schneider Electric’s EcoStruxure™ Building Operation platform with Cisco switches, which enhanced energy efficiency, improved security, and streamlined BMS management through higher levels of IP connectivity and secure data exchange. This collaboration has led to significant operational cost savings and optimized energy consumption for the university.

title: Cisco study finds 92% in Philippines prefer hybrid work arrangements
summary: A Cisco study reveals that 92% of Filipino respondents prefer hybrid work arrangements, citing improved work quality, productivity, and financial well-being, with average annual savings of over $6,552. Despite high preference, only 29% feel their organizations are "very prepared" for this model, highlighting the need for companies to adapt and prioritize integrated security and employee-centric policies. Cisco emphasizes that hybrid work requires leaders to foster inclusive cultures, backed by modern and secure technology infrastructure.

title: Coherent optics move toward interoperability
summary: At OFC 2022, the OIF demonstrated significant advancements in optical networking, including interoperable coherent optics from various vendors, 112 Gb/sec electrical links, flexible Ethernet, and co-packaging techniques. These innovations address the increasing demand for higher data rates and improved efficiency in data centers and network infrastructure. Future developments include an 800G coherent optics spec and research into 224 Gb/sec electrical interfaces for 1.2 Tb/sec and 1.6 Tb/sec links.

title: Cisco Takes Top Spot In Gartner Supply Chain Top 25 Despite Record Backlog
summary: Cisco maintained its leading position in Gartner's Global Supply Chain Top 25 report for the third consecutive year, despite facing record-breaking customer order backlogs due to ongoing supply chain challenges. The tech giant's proactive strategies, including investing $10 billion to secure chip supplies, adapting its operations, and focusing on ESG and circular economy concepts, contributed to its continued success. Cisco is also mitigating disruptions by working closely with suppliers, optimizing logistics, and promoting refurbished equipment through its Cisco Refresh program.

title: Cisco Cloud ACI now utilizing Catalyst 8000v
summary: Cisco Cloud ACI is now integrating Catalyst 8000v for its cloud routing capabilities, replacing CSR 1000v to offer enhanced hybrid and multi-cloud connectivity. This update provides customers with benefits like higher bandwidth tiers, simplified Cisco DNA licensing including a Pay-as-you-Go model, and access to new Catalyst 8000v platform features. The change ensures improved throughput and easier management for Cloud ACI deployments as CSR 1000v is nearing End-of-Life.

title: Perspectives on the Future of Service Provider Networking: 5G and the future of enterprise wireless networks
summary: This article explores the role of 5G in the future of enterprise wireless networks, particularly in the context of Industry 4.0 initiatives. It discusses the challenges enterprises face in adopting 5G and presents three ways service providers can offer private 5G solutions, emphasizing the "as-a-service" model as a compelling alternative to DIY approaches. Cisco's private 5G solution is highlighted as an example of a hybrid "as-a-service" model.

title: Cisco Security Cloud Unveiled, Highly Anticipated Cisco Plus SASE Offering Launches At RSA
summary: Cisco announced its new overarching security strategy at RSA 2022 with the unveiling of Cisco Security Cloud, a unified, open-standards-based platform designed to secure hybrid and multi-cloud environments. The company also launched Cisco Plus Secure Connect Now, a highly anticipated everything-as-a-service (XaaS) SASE offering, along with several other security innovations like Continuous Trusted Access and a unified Secure Client, and is making its Talos threat intelligence available as a bespoke service. These initiatives aim to simplify security management and provide comprehensive protection for partners and enterprises.

title: New report points to cyber risks in TVA control systems
summary: A recent oversight report revealed that the Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) failed to properly implement cybersecurity standards for a control system managing flood and river systems in seven southeastern states. The report identified "inappropriate logical and physical access" and vulnerable operating systems at dams within the system. While the risk of a physical threat was considered low, the lack of clear ownership for the system's maintenance and operation heightened cybersecurity risks, prompting TVA to agree with the findings and commit to updates and clearer responsibilities.

title: How has the HACK ETF averaged annual returns of 19%?
summary: The BetaShares Global Cybersecurity ETF (ASX: HACK) has achieved impressive annual returns averaging 19.02% since its inception in August 2016. This success is attributed to its exclusive focus on the fast-growing global cybersecurity sector and the strong performance of its top holdings, which include companies like CrowdStrike Holdings, Palo Alto Networks, and Zscaler Inc. The article highlights HACK's outperformance compared to other popular ASX ETFs, despite its 0.69% annual management fee.

title: HPE hires Mohammad Alrehaili as managing director for Saudi Arabia and the Gulf
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has appointed Mohammad Alrehaili as the managing director for Saudi Arabia and the Gulf, including Oman, Bahrain, and Kuwait. Alrehaili brings over 15 years of experience in the technology industry and previously served as a senior vice president for Business Sales at Mobily. His appointment underscores HPE's commitment to the region and its Vision 2030 objectives, as he is expected to play a crucial role in accelerating innovation and supporting digital transformation initiatives.

title: WAN Connectivity Matters in Delivering Exceptional Application Experiences
summary: This article highlights the critical importance of WAN connectivity in delivering high-quality application experiences, especially with the shift to hybrid and multicloud architectures. It details the partnership between Cisco and Equinix, offering integrated solutions to optimize WAN architecture by addressing latency, security, compliance, and cost. The collaboration focuses on four core use cases: secure cloud connectivity, distributed WAN architecture, private cloud footprint at the edge, and collaboration services, aiming to improve user experience and operational efficiency.

title: HYCU funding to aid multi-cloud data protection, management
summary: HYCU Inc. secured a $53 million Series B funding round, bringing its total funding to over $140 million. This investment will enable the company to expand its multi-cloud backup and recovery product line, increase its workforce, and develop a new data protection platform. HYCU aims to grow its customer base significantly and continues to pursue plans for an initial public offering, positioning itself as "recession-proof" in the data protection market.

title: Cisco had ‘potential headquarters play’ for Georgia, state officials believed - Atlanta Business Chronicle
summary: Georgia state officials believed there was a "potential headquarters play" for Cisco Systems, which could have significantly boosted Atlanta's tech market standing. The article highlights how landing a major tech company like Cisco's headquarters would be a substantial win for the region. It also references other large companies that Georgia attempted to attract, like Tesla and Rivian.

title: Custom Application Troubleshooting With Cisco IOx
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to troubleshooting custom applications on Cisco IOx, focusing on common challenges encountered when creating Docker images for microservices at the network's edge. It offers practical advice across 17 points, covering topics like image size optimization, storage management, logging practices, resource provisioning, networking configuration, and security best practices. The goal is to help developers create robust and efficiently running applications on Cisco IoT devices.

title: Cisco and Ford CEOs to Discuss the Role of Technology in Transforming the Automotive Industry and How People Use Their Vehicles
summary: Cisco and Ford CEOs Chuck Robbins and Jim Farley will discuss the role of technology, software, and connected vehicles in the future of the automotive industry at Cisco Live. They will cover topics like electrification, network connectivity, and using data to create new customer experiences. This conversation highlights Ford's long-standing relationship with Cisco and their joint efforts in digital transformation, including integrating Webex into Ford's new electric vehicles.

title: Atlanta beat these two states to land a 700-job Cisco innovation center
summary: Atlanta successfully competed against two rival Southern states to secure a 700-job Cisco Systems innovation center. Cisco announced its expansion in Atlanta in October of the previous year. The article highlights Georgia's competitive environment in economic development.

title: Blink Charging Announces the Transformative Acquisition of
summary: Blink Charging Co. announced the acquisition of SemaConnect, Inc. for $200 million, significantly expanding its network and capabilities. This acquisition will add nearly 13,000 EV chargers and 3,800 site host locations, making Blink Charging the only EV charging company with complete vertical integration from R&D to operations. The move also positions Blink to comply with "Buy American" mandates and capitalize on the Biden Administration's EV infrastructure bill.

title: Cisco Launches AppDynamics Cloud to Enable the Delivery of Exceptional Digital Experiences
summary: Cisco has launched AppDynamics Cloud, a cloud-native observability platform designed to help IT teams deliver exceptional digital experiences. This platform maximizes business outcomes by optimizing cloud-native applications, accelerating the detection and resolution of performance issues. It features continuous data integrations with OpenTelemetry™ standards and supports various cloud providers, aiming to improve application performance and security.

title: The role digital transformation is playing in the “re-founding” of Ford Motor Company
summary: Ford Motor Company CEO Jim Farley discussed the transformative role of digital innovation in the auto industry at Cisco Live. He highlighted three major shifts: electrification, partial autonomy, and digitally transformed vehicles, with the latter enabling continuous software updates and new customer experiences. This digital shift, Farley stated, is so fundamental that it's a "re-founding" of Ford, emphasizing the importance of improved connectivity and data security.

title: Network Devices Market Size & Share | Growth, Trends & Industry Report, 2034
summary: The global network devices market is projected to grow from USD 26.4 billion in 2022 to USD 36.4 billion by 2027, at a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 6.6%. This growth is driven by the increasing number of internet users, digital transformation initiatives, and the rise of Industry 4.0 and IIoT. Key market segments include routers, gateways, and access points, with WiFi connectivity and enterprise applications holding significant market shares, and North America leading in market size.

title: Cisco Is ‘Tearing Down Walls’ With Unified Networking, CX Goals
summary: Cisco is focusing on unifying its diverse product portfolio and simplifying customer experience, as discussed at Cisco Live 2022. This initiative aims to break down barriers between business units and product lines, starting with the integration of Catalyst management into the Meraki dashboard. The company and its partners believe this "tearing down walls" strategy will better align with how customers want to buy and manage IT solutions, leading to more seamless and integrated offerings.

title: Axians Teams with Cisco to Help Enterprises in Germany and the Netherlands with Cisco Private 5G
summary: Cisco and Axians are collaborating to provide Cisco Private 5G to enterprises in Germany and the Netherlands, focusing on the manufacturing and logistics industries. Axians, with over 10 years of experience in 4G/5G and IoT, will deliver Cisco's Private 5G solution as-a-service to accelerate digitalization and boost IoT adoption. This partnership aims to enhance digital transformation by offering automation, flexibility, efficiency, and security for business-critical activities.

title: PLDT blocks over 1 billion attempts to access CSAMs
summary: PLDT's AI-powered child protection platform has successfully blocked over one billion attempts to access prohibited online child sexual abuse materials (CSAMs) by Filipino netizens in its first six months of full operations. The platform, deployed in November, has stored nearly 300,000 banned URLs and automatically redirects users trying to access illegal content. While effective, the system currently cannot block livestreamed CSAM content or those using VPNs, prompting PLDT to work continuously to enhance its defenses.

title: Not just fun and games, Hasbro part of growing list of companies who say they're focused on social impact
summary: Hasbro has appointed a chief purpose officer, Kathrin Belliveau, to focus on social and environmental impact, echoing a growing trend among companies. While some experts like George Serafeim argue that purpose and profit are intertwined, others like Ken Pucker are skeptical, viewing much of impact messaging as marketing given the unregulated nature of social impact measurements. The article explores the evolving expectations of employees and investors regarding corporate social responsibility, and the debate around its genuine impact.

title: Autonomous Operations in Mining
summary: This article discusses the increasing maturity and adoption of autonomous operations in the mining industry, highlighting the significant benefits seen in haulage, such as increased productivity and reduced operating costs. It also addresses key challenges, including the need for reliable wireless coverage across the entire mine and robust instrumentation and control software. Cisco's infrastructure solutions are presented as critical components in facilitating successful autonomous mining projects.

title: Remote Operations in Oil & Gas
summary: This article discusses the increasing importance of remote operations in the oil and gas industry, highlighting it as a high ROI investment for leveraging digital technology. It outlines how Integrated Remote Operations Centers (iROCs) have improved efficiency and reduced costs, and explores the emerging concept of hybrid work for oil and gas experts. Cisco's role in providing the necessary infrastructure for data acquisition, secure remote access, and an ecosystem of partners to facilitate these remote and hybrid operational models is also emphasized.

title: Cisco wins reversal of $2.75 bln damages award because judge's wife owned stock
summary: A U.S. appeals court overturned a $2.75 billion patent infringement award against Cisco Systems Inc. The reversal was due to the trial judge's wife owning Cisco stock, which the appeals court ruled should have led to the judge's disqualification.
