The case will be reassigned to another judge to uphold public confidence in the judicial process.

title: Cisco Helps Service Providers Build a Sustainable Internet for the Future
summary: Cisco has announced advancements to its Routed Optical Networking solution, introducing new products and features that can help service providers save up to 45% in power and 70% in real estate/space for equipment. This initiative aims to reduce costs, meet sustainability goals, and redefine the economics of the internet by providing enhanced capabilities for converged network solutions. New Cisco CX services will also support customers in migrating to Routed Optical Networking architectures for greater efficiency and sustainability.

title: Evil Geniuses partners with HP Enterprise for edge in talent discovery
summary: North American esports organization Evil Geniuses has partnered with HP Enterprise for a multi-year deal focused on leveraging cloud computing infrastructure and AI services. This collaboration aims to give Evil Geniuses an edge in talent discovery, game strategy, and player development through data analysis. This marks HPE's first partnership with an esports organization, building on Hewlett Packard's existing involvement in the esports sector.

title: ZuoRAT Malware exclusively designed for hacking ASUS, Cisco, DrayTek, and NETGEAR SOHO routers
summary: A new multi-stage remote access Trojan named ZuoRAT has been discovered, actively targeting Small Office/Home Office (SOHO) routers from brands like ASUS, Cisco, DrayTek, and NETGEAR, primarily in Europe and North America. Believed to have been active since 2020, this sophisticated malware allows attackers to compromise local networks, bypass traditional security measures, and perform man-in-the-middle attacks such as DNS and HTTPS hijacking. The rise in remote work has made SOHO routers a prime target due to their often-lower security and lack of active management.

title: Data center market in Malaysia to observe USD 2.08 Bn growth: Report
summary: The data center market in Malaysia is projected to grow by USD 2.08 billion between 2021 and 2026, exhibiting a CAGR of 15.72%. According to a Technavio report, the market is expected to see 14.86% year-over-year growth in 2022, with data center servers anticipated to generate the most revenue. Key vendors like Cisco Systems Inc. and Huawei Technologies Co. Ltd. are focusing on M&A and R&D to stay competitive in this fragmented market.

title: Evernote, Home Depot outsource network management
summary: Evernote and Home Depot have outsourced their network management to vendors like Meter and Aruba, respectively. This strategy allows their in-house IT staff to focus on business-critical projects rather than the daily tasks of network maintenance. Both companies found that a subscription-based "network as a service" (NaaS) model was more cost-effective and agile for adapting to changing business needs.

title: PTL Ltd attends two international events on Border Security Management
summary: PTL Ltd, a leading system integrator, recently participated in two international events focused on Border Security Management: the 2022 World Border Security Congress in Lisbon and the Border Management & Technologies Summit Middle East 2022 in Dubai. The company's Managing Director, Mr. Pierre Attard, highlighted the opportunity these events provided for gaining insights into new technologies and developments in border control and security. These events fostered discussions among government officials globally on key border issues such as biometrics and seamless traveler programs.

title: Florida man charged with selling fake Cisco equipment in $1 billion scheme
summary: A Florida man, Onur Aksoy, has been charged with importing and reselling counterfeit Cisco networking equipment worth over $1 billion if authentic. Aksoy allegedly operated a scheme from 2013 to 2022, netting $100 million in revenue by selling fake devices through Amazon.com, eBay, and other platforms after acquiring them from suppliers in China and Hong Kong. Cisco Systems Inc. sent multiple cease-and-desist letters, and both Amazon and eBay blocked numerous storefronts related to the counterfeiting.

title: EXCLUSIVE BC Partners explores $4 bln sale of IT firm Presidio -sources
summary: Private equity firm BC Partners Ltd is reportedly exploring the sale of Presidio Inc., a U.S. information technology provider, for over $4 billion, including debt. Presidio, which generated $3.1 billion in revenue in 2021, specializes in secured cloud-based technology for a wide range of organizations and has benefited from increased demand for IT services during the COVID-19 pandemic. This potential sale follows a history of private equity ownership, with BC Partners having taken the company private in 2019 for $2.1 billion.

title: Cisco SD-WAN with AWS Cloud WAN for an on-demand global cloud network
summary: Cisco and AWS are collaborating to integrate Cisco SD-WAN with AWS Cloud WAN, aiming to simplify and accelerate cloud journeys for their joint customers. This integration allows organizations to connect multi-region and cloud-to-on-premises workloads with enhanced automation, unified management, and secure cloud networking. The solution will reduce deployment times, lower TCO, and provide a single portal experience for secure connectivity across AWS cloud regions.

title: Key Takeaways: 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Security Service Edge
summary: This article provides key takeaways from the inaugural 2022 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Security Service Edge (SSE), defining SSE and outlining Gartner's market predictions. It evaluates 11 significant providers, categorizing them into leaders, challengers, visionaries, and niche players based on their ability to execute and completeness of vision. The report highlights the strengths of companies like Zscaler, Netskope, McAfee Enterprise, Palo Alto Networks, Cisco, Bitglass, Lookout, Broadcom, iboss, Forcepoint, and Versa within the SSE market.

title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, Conagra Brands and more
summary: JPMorgan Chase and Goldman Sachs shares dropped following disappointing earnings reports, with JPMorgan missing analyst expectations and suspending share buybacks. Conagra Brands also saw its stock fall after reporting a decline in sales volume. Conversely, First Republic Bank's shares rose after exceeding earnings expectations, and Costco's stock jumped after a Deutsche Bank upgrade.

title: Cisco DNA Center is now FIPS 140-2 certified
summary: Cisco DNA Center, a network controller and management system for Cisco Catalyst networks, has achieved FIPS 140-2 certification. This certification is crucial for government agencies and private entities working with them, as it ensures cryptographic modules meet security requirements for protecting sensitive information. Cisco emphasizes its commitment to FIPS standards and encourages Cisco Prime Infrastructure users to migrate to Cisco DNA Center.

title: Wipro Limited appoints Päivi Rekonen to its Board
summary: Wipro Limited has announced the appointment of Päivi Rekonen to its Board of Directors for a five-year term, effective October 1, 2022. Rekonen will serve as an Independent Director, bringing over 25 years of experience in technology, banking, and services to the company. Her appointment is subject to shareholder approval and is expected to contribute to Wipro's digital and technology transformation, strategy, risk management, and organizational development.

title: Cisco and Celcom enhances Mobile Connectivity Experience for Malaysians
summary: Cisco and Celcom Axiata Berhad have significantly improved mobile connectivity in Malaysia by leveraging their converged Software Defined Network (SDN) transport infrastructure, integrating AI and machine learning. This enhancement has led to faster service provisioning, reduced errors during the pandemic, and enabled a 5G-ready network. The collaboration aims to optimize operational investments, provide end-to-end network slicing, and ensure a reliable network for over 14 million users.

title: How Cisco’s purpose is fueling its innovation engine
summary: Cisco's commitment to "power an inclusive future for all" acts as its strategic and moral compass, guiding its innovation, investments, and actions. This purpose influences cutting-edge technological development, responsible AI practices, and a targeted $300 million investment over five years in social justice initiatives, including the $50 million Aspire Fund for diverse-led startups. Cisco's Country Digital Acceleration initiative further demonstrates this ethos by partnering with governments to bridge the digital divide and contribute to global GDP.

title: Small but mighty: Rice farmers with the best in agritech
summary: Cisco's Country Digital Acceleration (CDA) initiative in Kerala, southern India, is transforming small rice and shrimp farms through the innovative use of IoT sensors, drones, edge networking, and AI. This program, in partnership with the Indian government, aims to boost farmer income and ensure sustainable agricultural practices. By providing real-time data and insights through Village Knowledge Centers, the initiative empowers farmers to optimize crop management, improve soil health, and increase yields.

title: Get Hands-on with the Meraki API in the DevNet Sandbox
summary: This article demonstrates how to use the Meraki API programmatically within the DevNet always-on sandbox lab. It guides users through using Google Collaboratory to make read requests, manipulate data using the Pandas library, and visualize network analytics. The post aims to simplify leveraging Meraki API for network management and automation.

title: Nokia Corp. ADR outperforms market despite losses on the day
summary: Nokia Corp. ADR (NOK) dropped 1.37% to $5.04 on a generally negative trading day, underperforming the NASDAQ Composite and Dow Jones Industrial Average. Despite the loss, it closed $1.36 short of its 52-week high. The ADR also underperformed some competitors like Telefon AB L.M. Ericsson ADR, Cisco Systems Inc., and Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co.

title: More than a VPN: Announcing Cisco Secure Client (formerly AnyConnect)
summary: Cisco has announced Cisco Secure Client, formerly known as AnyConnect, as the new unified security agent for Cisco Secure. This client goes beyond traditional VPN capabilities by integrating Endpoint Detection & Response (EDR) and enabling Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA). It aims to simplify management and increase visibility for endpoint security within the Cisco SecureX platform.

title: Cisco leverages Snort 3 and Talos to manage trust in an evolving cloud-based world
summary: Cisco is adapting to the evolving trust boundary in hybrid and multicloud environments by leveraging Snort 3 for intrusion detection and the Talos Intelligence Group for threat information. This approach provides comprehensive security across virtual environments, integrating seamlessly into cloud components. Eric Kostlan from Cisco Systems Inc. highlighted that while the traditional trust boundary has dramatically transformed, the company's solutions ensure protection by continuously updating threat information and expanding visualization capabilities.

title: Enabling Enterprise Cybersecurity Protection with a DPU-Accelerated, Next-Generation Firewall
summary: NVIDIA and Palo Alto Networks have collaborated to enhance cybersecurity by accelerating VM-Series Next Generation Firewalls (NGFW) using NVIDIA's BlueField Data Processing Unit (DPU). This partnership provides a 5x performance boost and significant CapEx savings by offloading packet inspection from host CPUs to the DPU. The joint solution includes an Intelligent Traffic Offload (ITO) service that optimizes firewall resources, ensuring deep security inspection only for necessary payload flows, thereby improving efficiency and enabling comprehensive zero-trust protection across all hosts.

title: Most Influential Women: Emily Nachlas, Western Alliance Bancorporation
summary: Emily Nachlas, Chief Risk Officer at Western Alliance Bancorporation, has been recognized as one of Arizona's Most Influential Women of 2022 by Az Business and AZRE magazines. The article highlights her extensive experience, her role as a mentor, and her philanthropic efforts in fostering over 50 small dogs. Nachlas also cites Judy Sheindlin (Judge Judy) as a significant source of inspiration.

title: Expanding the Possibilities
summary: GlobalFoundries (GF) is challenging conventional semiconductor manufacturing by focusing on feature-rich process technology for 70% of the market. The company emphasizes innovation in RF communications, power management, and precision analog, working with industry leaders to deliver solutions across various high-growth markets. GF's efforts, including the GF Connex RF portfolio, GF Fotonix silicon photonics platform, and GF Labs, aim to drive product innovation and accelerate time-to-market.

title: Gartner CFO Survey Reveals Real Estate and Finance Functions Most Likely to Face Cuts Within a Year
summary: A July 2022 Gartner survey of over 200 CFOs indicates that real estate/facilities management and finance functions are most vulnerable to budget cuts in the next year due to factors like increased remote work. Conversely, IT and sales functions are expected to see spending increases, with 40% of CFOs planning to boost IT investment, reflecting a focus on digital technology and automation to combat inflation and drive growth. The report suggests that "efficient growth" companies, which strategically invest during economic difficulties, achieve better long-term results.

title: Datacentre sector hits back at claims that West London electricity grid capacity crunch is its fault
summary: The datacentre industry is pushing back against claims that its rapid growth in West London is solely responsible for an electricity grid capacity crunch, potentially impacting new housing developments. While a GLA briefing note suggested datacentres are consuming available electricity capacity, industry sources and local councils argue the issue is being sensationalized and stems more from historical under-investment in the UK's energy infrastructure. The sector highlights its efforts to support grid stability and calls for collaborative solutions rather than being made a scapegoat.

title: Turbocharging Multi-Cloud Security and Application Delivery with VirtIO Offloading
summary: This article details how F5 and NVIDIA are addressing inefficiencies in data centers caused by increased virtualization and software-defined architectures by offloading VirtIO to the NVIDIA BlueField-2 DPU. This approach provides optimal acceleration, domain isolation, and dynamic composability, significantly reducing CPU reliance while maintaining flexibility. A demonstration showcased a 320% improvement in throughput and a 66% reduction in processing time when using hardware acceleration with the NVIDIA BlueField-2 DPU compared to running VirtIO on the host.

title: Modernizing Healthcare Networks for the Connected Ecosystem
summary: This article discusses how healthcare organizations can modernize their networks using Secure Access Service Edge (SASE) to enhance security and efficiency in a digitally transforming environment. It highlights the challenges posed by decentralized resources, remote users, and connected medical devices, explaining how SASE addresses these issues by offering secure access, improved performance, and cost optimization. The author emphasizes SASE's role in supporting various healthcare use cases, including Zero Trust Network Access and securing remote patient monitoring.

title: Cisco Talos shares insights related to recent cyber attack on Cisco
summary: Cisco Talos and Cisco Security Incident Response (CSIRT) have been remediating a cyber attack that began on May 24, 2022. The attack originated from compromised employee credentials saved in a personal Google account, leading to successful MFA push acceptance and VPN access. While the attackers accessed some systems and exfiltrated non-sensitive data from a Box folder and employee authentication data from Active Directory, Cisco states no evidence suggests access to critical internal systems like product development or code signing.

title: Creating 5G massive MIMO: Part 1
summary: This article, Part 1 of a series, introduces 5G massive MIMO technology, explaining its origins with Thomas Marzetta in 2006 and its operational principles. It details how massive MIMO utilizes numerous small antennas to simultaneously and selectively beam information to multiple users, enhancing spectral efficiency and network capacity. The piece also touches on the impact of COVID-19 on its deployment and growth areas.

title: Internal Cisco data stolen after employee hit by voice phishing attack
summary: Internal Cisco data was stolen after an employee fell victim to a voice phishing attack. An initial access broker, linked to cybercrime groups like Lapsus$ and Yanluowang ransomware operators, gained access to Cisco systems for product development and code signing after the employee unwittingly accepted a multifactor authentication request. Cisco states that no products, services, sensitive customer or employee information, intellectual property, or supply chain operations were impacted.

title: Cisco supervisors allegedly denied employee opportunities based on caste system
summary: A California appellate court ruled that the Department of Fair Employment and Housing (DFEH) is not bound by an employee's arbitration agreement when suing an employer like Cisco Systems Inc. The lawsuit stems from an employee's complaint of discrimination and retaliation based on India's traditional caste system, where supervisors allegedly denied him opportunities due to his lowest caste origin. The court affirmed that DFEH acts independently and can pursue remedies beyond what an individual employee could.

title: Cisco beats back $371 mln patent lawsuit after Boston trial
summary: A Boston federal jury ruled that Cisco Systems Inc.'s data-center platforms do not infringe on a patent owned by Egenera Inc., dismissing Egenera's claim for at least $371 million in damages. The lawsuit concerned Egenera's allegation that Cisco copied its technology for Unified Computing System (UCS) after discussing a potential collaboration and hiring its employees. Cisco stated it was "incredibly proud of our engineers and our proven track record of delivering innovation."

title: How NTI and Cisco Networking Academy are preparing Egypt for the IT jobs of the future
summary: This article highlights how the National Telecommunication Institute (NTI) in partnership with Cisco Networking Academy is addressing Egypt's challenge of preparing its large, youthful workforce for future IT jobs. Through various training programs, NTI aims to bridge the gap between university education and market needs, providing students with internationally recognized certifications. These initiatives, funded by the Ministry of Telecommunication, have trained thousands of students, enabling them to secure employment in the IT sector.

title: Cisco beats profit and revenue targets and offers encouraging guidance
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (CSCO) reported stronger-than-expected fiscal fourth-quarter earnings and revenue, providing an optimistic forecast for the new financial year which caused its stock to rise. The company's performance was bolstered by an easing of supply constraints and strong sales growth in Europe, despite challenges in the Americas and Asia Pacific and ongoing supply chain issues for fiscal year 2023. Analysts provided mixed reactions regarding Cisco's pace of transformation and its allocation of funds for stock repurchases versus core business investment.

title: Cisco Systems Announces Hoot and Holler Over IP
summary: Cisco Systems announced its Hoot and Holler over IP solution, designed to eliminate the need for costly circuit-switched dedicated lines traditionally used by brokerage firms for their point-to-multipoint voice applications. This new solution allows financial and trading companies to leverage their IP networks for voice traffic, significantly reducing expenses while preserving existing hardware investments. PaineWebber Incorporated is among the firms testing this new technology, with plans for a pilot rollout in Q4 and general deployment in 2001.

title: NEC and Fortinet enter global agreement to deliver end-to-end, high-performance security for 5G networks
summary: NEC Corporation and Fortinet have partnered to provide end-to-end, high-performance security solutions for 5G networks to communication service providers (CSPs) globally. Fortinet will supply its cybersecurity solutions, including FortiGate next-generation firewalls, while NEC will offer professional services and integration expertise leveraging its global reach. This collaboration aims to address the critical need for comprehensive security in 5G networks, protecting against emerging threats in complex environments like Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC).

title: Arista Stock Is a Cloud Winner. One Analyst Advises Caution.
summary: Arista Networks has demonstrated significant growth, with a 49% revenue increase in the June quarter, driven by strong demand for its networking hardware despite supply chain challenges. The company, a key provider to cloud computing and corporate data centers, has seen its stock perform better than competitors like Juniper Networks and Cisco Systems this year. However, one analyst advises caution regarding its future performance.

title: Boeing Selects Cisco Systems for Agreement Valued at up to $124 Million
summary: Cisco Systems announced that The Boeing Company has selected its switching and routing technologies for its worldwide corporate network in an agreement valued at up to $124 million. This network will support over 225,000 employees globally and enable emerging network-based applications and multimedia support. Cisco's scalable LAN switching and routing solutions were chosen for their reliability and strength in addressing enterprise requirements.

title: Megatrends: Opportunities on the Front Lines of Cybersecurity
summary: The cybersecurity sector is experiencing rapid growth due to increasing cyberattacks, cloud computing, and remote work, presenting compelling long-term investment opportunities. Morgan Stanley Research predicts continued growth in cybersecurity spending, highlighting areas like endpoint security, network security, and integrated defense capabilities. Investors can consider thematic ETFs or single-stock investments while acknowledging current elevated valuations.

title: Exodus Communications and Cisco Team to Offer Advanced End-To-End Networking Solutions
summary: Exodus Communications and Cisco Systems announced a partnership where Exodus' Internet Data Centers will participate in the Cisco Powered Network program. This makes Exodus the first Internet Data Center selected for the program, allowing the companies to offer reliable, secure, and high-performance Internet services utilizing Cisco equipment and software. The collaboration aims to provide enhanced connectivity and network-based applications to customers, emphasizing reliability, performance, and network management for mission-critical business applications.

title: KIVEX.com and Cisco Systems Team on National Expansion of Internet Building™ Program
summary: KIVEX.com and Cisco Systems announced a partnership to nationally expand the KIVEX.com Internet Building Service, providing high-speed internet access to small and medium-sized businesses in multi-tenant office buildings. Leveraging Cisco Powered Network technology, KIVEX.com will deliver reliable and secure internet services using Cisco routers and switches. This collaboration aims to offer cost-effective, high-performance internet solutions demonstrating their shared vision for an IP-based communication infrastructure.

title: Punjab National Bank goes the e-Way
summary: Punjab National Bank (PNB) has signed an MoU with Cisco Systems to upgrade its IT infrastructure, aiming to connect its offices nationwide with a new network backbone. This initiative will enable PNB to offer next-generation banking services, including real-time transactions and internet banking, to its customers across India. The bank plans to extend this network to 300 branches by March 2003 and 1000 branches by 2004, leveraging Cisco's expertise for efficiency and improved customer satisfaction.

title: Cisco CEO John Chambers Redefines New Economy as Internet Economy
summary: In 1998, Cisco Systems CEO John T. Chambers visited Washington D.C. to discuss the profound impact of the Internet on the global economy. He declared the "new economy is the Internet Economy," driven by the globalization of business and information technology networking, leading to a significant economic shift since the Industrial Revolution. Chambers outlined key strategies for companies to succeed in this new landscape, including leveraging the Internet as a competitive advantage, globalizing operations, becoming customer-driven, empowering employees, and embracing change.

title: Cisco Systems (CSCO) : Lessons from the Dot-Com Bubble
summary: This article examines Cisco Systems' (CSCO) stock performance, using its valuation during the dot-com bubble as a cautionary tale against overpaying for growth. Despite a substantial increase in earnings per share since 2000, Cisco's stock has not surpassed its dot-com bubble highs due to its extremely high valuation of 165 times earnings at the time. The author emphasizes Warren Buffett's adage, "Price is what you pay, value is what you get," to highlight the long-term impact of initial valuation on investment returns.

title: Qwest, Cisco, Nortel Networks And Indiana University Partner To Deliver High Performance Internet Research Network
summary: Qwest Communications, Cisco Systems, Nortel Networks, and Indiana University have partnered to launch the Abilene Project, an advanced research and education network in the United States. This network, with an estimated value of $500 million over three years, delivers high-speed services to universities, acting as a test bed for future Internet applications such as distance learning and tele-medicine. Operating at 2.4 gigabits per second, Abilene aims to accelerate the development of advanced Internet capabilities for the academic, research, and corporate communities.

title: Frontier Selects Cisco to Supply High-Speed Internet Equipment
summary: Frontier Telephone of Rochester has selected Cisco Systems Inc. as its primary supplier for digital subscriber line (DSL) equipment. This partnership will enable Frontier to provide high-speed internet services, named LightningLink, to residential customers, offering speeds up to 40 times faster than standard modems over existing telephone lines. The service is currently available in specific New York and Minnesota markets, with plans for expansion.

title: Multi-Cloud Networking Market Size, Drivers, Opportunities & Challenges
summary: The global multi-cloud networking market is projected to grow from USD 2.7 billion in 2022 to USD 7.6 billion by 2027, exhibiting a CAGR of 22.5%. Key drivers include the need for disaster recovery and contingency plans, while challenges include limited technical expertise and complexities in network redesign for cloud environments. North America is expected to hold the largest market share, with the retail and e-commerce sector showing the highest CAGR due to increasing adoption of innovative technologies.

title: Why Allstate's CHRO believes HR needs to run more like a business
summary: Robert Toohey, Allstate's new CHRO, is leveraging his background in tech and leadership roles outside of HR to transform the company's human resources department. He emphasizes the importance of HR operating with a business-first mindset, focusing on data and technology to improve employee experience and contribute directly to business outcomes. Toohey aims to treat HR as a service provider that adds value to the organization by aligning talent management with digital transformation goals.

title: Several Large US Companies Quietly Rolling Back COVID-19 Vaccine Mandate
summary: Several major U.S. companies, including Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan & Chase Co., and Cisco, are discreetly reversing their COVID-19 vaccine mandates. Experts note that the rationale for such mandates has weakened significantly since 2021. This move comes after the Supreme Court blocked the Biden administration's broad mandate for companies, though a limited mandate for healthcare workers remains.

title: Small Business UCaaS M&A: Ooma Acquires OnSip -
summary: Ooma has acquired OnSip, a unified communications service provider specializing in small businesses, for $9.75 million in cash. This acquisition aims to expand Ooma's leadership in serving SMB customers and accelerate growth for Ooma Business. The article also provides context on the broader UCaaS market, noting some signs of consolidation and slowing M&A activity in 2022.

title: Hybrid work schemes raise work quality, study finds
summary: A study by Cisco Systems, Inc. found that a significant majority of Filipino employees perceive an improvement in work quality and well-being under hybrid work arrangements. However, the study also highlighted challenges such as connectivity issues due to inadequate infrastructure and concerns about communication for fully remote workers and increased micromanagement. The findings suggest the importance of robust technological infrastructure and cybersecurity to support effective hybrid work models.

title: Gartner Survey Shows 75% of Organisations Are Pursuing Security Vendor Consolidation in 2022
summary: A Gartner survey reveals that 75% of organizations are pursuing security vendor consolidation in 2022, a significant increase from 29% in 2020. The primary driver for this consolidation is to improve risk posture and reduce complexity, rather than solely to cut costs. Opportunities for consolidation are seen in areas like SASE and XDR, with many organizations planning to adopt these technologies by the end of 2022.

title: Nile, Extreme attack Cisco's enterprise networking biz
summary: Cisco, a dominant player in enterprise networking, is facing increasing competition from both established companies like Extreme Networks and new startups such as Nile. Nile, backed by former Cisco CEO John Chambers, is introducing an "as-a-service" pricing model to challenge Cisco's traditional hardware sales approach. Extreme Networks is also gaining market share by offering streamlined architectural solutions and expanding into the 5G market, directly competing with Cisco by focusing on architectural unity and cloud migration.

title: Cisco faces U.S. Supreme Court appeal after overturning $2.7 bln patent award
summary: Centripetal Networks is appealing to the U.S. Supreme Court after a U.S. appeals court overturned a $2.7 billion patent award against Cisco Systems. The appeals court had cited a perceived conflict of interest involving the federal judge who originally presided over the case and his wife's stockholdings in Cisco. Centripetal argues that the judge's decision to place the stock in a blind trust was appropriate and that the appeals court's ruling creates an "impossible position" for judges.

title: How Cisco's ex-CEO and former engineer chief decided to take on their old company
summary: Former Cisco CEO John Chambers and former Cisco chief engineer Pankaj Patel founded Nile Global to address the complexities and high costs associated with computer networking. The article focuses on Patel's perspective regarding why he and Chambers believe their new venture, Nile Global, offers a desirable solution for networking customers today. It highlights their decision to leverage their past experience to challenge their former company, Cisco.

title: Cisco's ex-CEO and former engineering chief unveil their years-in-the-works networking startup
summary: Former Cisco CEO John Chambers and former engineering chief Pankaj Patel have launched a new networking startup called Nile Global. The company, which they have been working on since 2018, aims to use artificial intelligence to disrupt the networking industry.

title: Fortinet Inc. stock underperforms Friday when compared to competitors
summary: Fortinet Inc. (FTNT) stock dropped 0.79% on Friday, closing at $50.42, underperforming the broader market downturn and some of its competitors. The stock closed significantly below its 52-week high and saw higher trading volume than its 50-day average. Competitors Cisco Systems Inc. and HP Inc. both saw gains on the same day.

title: Cisco Systems, Inc - UCS-SPL-M16G - Cisco SmartPlay Select 16 GB Memory Unit - For Server - 16 GB DDR4 SDRAM - 2133 MHz - Registered - DIMM - Distributors Inventory as of September 19, 2022 -
summary: This article reports on the availability of Cisco SmartPlay Select 16 GB Memory Units (UCS-SPL-M16G) for servers, noting 9999 units in the distribution chain as of September 19, 2022. It highlights the unprecedented supply chain shortages and offers information for businesses and government contractors seeking to purchase these units. Special pricing is mentioned for federal government customers.

title: Harmonic goes network-agnostic with 'Pebble-2' module
summary: Harmonic has introduced its new network-agnostic 'Pebble-2' module for distributed access architecture (DAA), designed to work with both hybrid fiber/coax (HFC) and fiber-based networks. The Pebble-2 supports remote PHY, remote MACPHY, PON, and DOCSIS 4.0, including both Full Duplex DOCSIS (FDX) and Frequency Division Duplexing (FDD). This modular device can be integrated into Harmonic's own nodes or adapted to third-party nodes, and it works with Harmonic's software-based network virtualization platform, CableOS.

title: The Pandemic Spawns Multi-Vendor Networks In Its Wake
summary: The COVID-19 pandemic has significantly changed how companies operate, leading to an accelerated need for multi-vendor network solutions due to supply chain issues and labor shortages. Juniper Networks, through acquisitions like Mist and Apstra, has positioned itself to address these challenges by providing flexible, automated, and intent-based networking that supports diverse hardware environments. This shift allows enterprises to maintain business continuity and innovate without vendor lock-in, learning from hyperscalers' focus on reliability and consistency to achieve speed.

title: Most common SAP vulnerabilities attackers try to exploit
summary: Attackers are consistently targeting SAP applications due to unpatched systems, misconfigurations, and vulnerable custom code, exploiting known critical vulnerabilities often within days of disclosure. Organizations struggle with patching all identified SAP vulnerabilities, leading to exposure to data theft and system disruptions. Key vulnerabilities include specific CVEs like RECON and Invoker Servlet flaws, as well as configuration errors like weak passwords and custom code injection flaws.

title: WFH Stocks Still Have Room to Grow
summary: Despite the cooling market sentiment for work-from-home (WFH) technologies as employees return to offices, Morgan Stanley Research suggests that companies continue to invest significantly in collaboration software, cybersecurity, and virtual-desktop programs for hybrid and remote models. A survey of CIOs indicated that many companies utilize multiple providers for these services and plan to increase spending, suggesting a potentially undervalued investment opportunity in the sector. This continued investment stems from the perceived return on investment, the longevity of hybrid work, and ongoing needs for cloud adoption and cybersecurity refresh cycles.

title: Domain Shadowing: A Stealthy Use of DNS Compromise for Cybercrime
summary: Domain shadowing is a sophisticated form of DNS hijacking where attackers create malicious subdomains under legitimate, compromised domain names without affecting the primary function of the compromised site. This tactic allows cybercriminals to leverage the benign reputation of the compromised domains for nefarious activities like phishing, malware distribution, and C2 operations, making detection difficult for both domain owners and traditional security vendors. Palo Alto Networks has developed an automated system utilizing machine learning and passive DNS logs to detect these shadowed domains more effectively, finding thousands that evade other detection methods, and integrating this capability into their security products.

title: Net zero one year later
summary: Cisco's Chief Sustainability Officer discusses the company's progress one year after setting a goal to reach net zero greenhouse gas emissions across its value chain by 2040. The article highlights the importance of science-based targets, the third-party validation of Cisco's goal by SBTi, and the company's strategy for deep emissions reduction, including renewable energy adoption and helping customers reduce their own emissions.

title: Fortinet, VMware, Cisco Drive SD-WAN Gartner Magic Quadrant
summary: A recent Gartner Magic Quadrant report highlights Fortinet, VMware, and Cisco as leaders in the SD-WAN market, with Cisco significantly improving its position. The report also anticipates a surge in single-vendor SASE solutions, as organizations increasingly seek to combine SD-WAN and Security Service Edge (SSE) capabilities from a single provider to reduce complexity and improve integration. Each leading vendor is innovating with specific focuses, such as Fortinet on application connectivity and SD-Branch, VMware on 5G integration and security services, and Cisco on network visibility and public cloud partnerships.

title: Securing the Internet of Things
summary: Cloudflare is announcing its new Internet of Things (IoT) platform to address the growing security risks associated with the exponential growth of IoT devices. The platform aims to provide a single view for managing and securing IoT devices, offering features like cellular connectivity provisioning, packet inspection, and Zero Trust security integration. By offloading compute and leveraging Cloudflare's global network, the platform helps reduce device costs, enhance security, and simplify development for IoT applications.

title: 12 tips for mitigating cyberattacks
summary: This article by JPMorgan Chase provides 12 recommendations for organizations to mitigate cyberattacks, drawing on best practices from the National Security Agency (NSA). The tips cover areas such as software updates, account access control, disaster recovery planning, network segregation, and leveraging multi-factor authentication. The article emphasizes the importance of continuous review, updates, and proactive defensive strategies in a constantly evolving cyberthreat landscape.

title: Paltalk has built a global video chatting platform that connects people with shared interests and stands the test of time
summary: Paltalk, founded in 1998, has developed a global video chatting platform that connects people with shared interests through over 5,000 chat rooms. The company operates on a stable business model with tiered subscriptions and organic growth, and holds 18 issued patents, including some licensed by major tech firms. CEO Jason Katz emphasizes Paltalk's role in connecting people virtually around common interests, driving its continued relevance and plans for strategic opportunities like the acquisition of ManyCam.

title: Top 6 challenges of a zero-trust security model
summary: The zero-trust security model offers significant advantages in cybersecurity but also presents several challenges that organizations must understand and overcome. These challenges include the risk of creating security gaps with a piecemeal approach, the absence of all-in-one zero-trust products, difficulties in adapting legacy systems, the need for continuous administration and maintenance, potential hindrance to productivity, and inherent security risks within the model itself. Overcoming these involves running trials, starting small, scaling slowly, and fostering a supportive workplace culture.

title: Forbes 50 over 50 features 7 Indian-American women
summary: Forbes' recent 50 Over 50 list highlights seven high-profile Indian American women who are making significant contributions across various fields, including technology, business, and social impact. These women, comprising entrepreneurs, CEOs, and activists, demonstrate that age is no barrier to achieving success and making a difference. The featured individuals showcase their achievements in leading major corporations, founding innovative companies, and advocating for social causes.

title: Cisco partners with Microsoft to add Teams to its meeting devices
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. will integrate Microsoft Corp.'s Teams messaging app into its meeting devices, offering users an alternative to its Webex video conferencing software. Cisco aims to be a hardware platform for various conferencing software, including Microsoft Teams, Zoom, and Google, believing this strategy will enhance customer experience and benefit the company. This move comes as Webex faces stiff competition from newer platforms like Zoom.

title: Cisco partners with Microsoft to add Teams to its meeting devices
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. announced a partnership with Microsoft Corp. to integrate the Teams messaging app into its meeting devices. This move offers an alternative to Cisco's Webex and allows users to access various conferencing platforms on Cisco hardware, reflecting a strategy to become a versatile hardware platform for multiple software providers. Microsoft Teams will be available on Cisco devices starting in the first quarter of next year.

title: Cisco gear is being shipped into Russia from China and other countries, leaked customs database shows
summary: A leaked Russian customs database indicates that approximately 500 shipments of Cisco gear arrived in Russia in August, despite the networking giant having ceased its business operations in the country months prior. The data suggests these products originated from various countries, predominantly China, as well as Vietnam, Switzerland, Mexico, Malaysia, Australia, Thailand, Holland, and the U.S. This information highlights ongoing deliveries of Cisco products into Russia after the company's official withdrawal.

title: Cisco gear is being shipped into Russia from China and other countries, leaked customs database shows
summary: A leaked Russian customs database indicates that around 500 shipments of Cisco gear arrived in Russia in August, despite Cisco Systems Inc. having ended its business operations in the country. The majority of these shipments originated from China, with other countries like Vietnam, Switzerland, and even the U.S. also identified as points of origin. This data suggests ongoing delivery of Cisco products to Russia through various global channels.

title: Security solutions that would make da Vinci proud
summary: Cisco's Cybersecurity Co-Innovation Center in Milan, housed in a Leonardo da Vinci namesake museum, focuses on security, digital privacy, education, and social impact. This center, part of Cisco's Country Digital Acceleration initiative, brings together technology, academia, and government to address rising cyberthreats and the cyberskills gap. The initiative emphasizes collaboration, interdisciplinary approaches, and education through programs like Networking Academy to ensure an inclusive and secure digital future for all.

title: Cisco Networking Academy celebrates 25 years, announces new commitments
summary: Cisco announced a new goal to empower 25 million people with digital and cybersecurity skills over the next 10 years through its Cisco Networking Academy program, celebrating its 25th anniversary. This initiative includes a new partnership with Experis to connect learners with jobs and the launch of "Skills for All," a free mobile-first learning program. The company aims to address the growing demand for tech skills and promote an inclusive future.

title: Managing Risk: Understanding Your Firm’s Obligations Under DORA
summary: This article discusses the European Commission's Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA), which introduces new cybersecurity requirements for financial services firms in the European Union. DORA aims to standardize and strengthen digital operational resilience, focusing on two key pillars: ICT Risk Management and ICT Third-Party Risk Management. The article provides insights into how firms can address these risks and prepare for the expected 2024 implementation deadline.

title: Safeguarding Protected Health Information (PHI): Preparing for the Onslaught
summary: Private equity investment in healthcare has significantly increased, leading to a concurrent rise in data breaches, particularly ransomware attacks. This article provides a risk assessment framework and guidelines for cybersecurity resilience and incident response in the healthcare sector, emphasizing the need for comprehensive strategies both before and after a breach. It also highlights that regulatory compliance alone is insufficient for robust security, advocating for a multidisciplinary, threat-intelligence approach to protect Protected Health Information (PHI).

title: Our Commitment to Social Justice: 2-Years in the Making
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. highlights its two-year progress on a social justice commitment launched in September 2020, backed by a $300 million investment. The article details 12 key actions, including policy change, increased representation, fair pay, supplier diversity, and significant commitments to Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs). It also discusses broader engagements like the Black Economic Alliance Foundation and participation in National HBCU week, underscoring Cisco's dedication to driving systemic change.

title: F5 Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
summary: F5 Inc. (FFIV) stock increased by 0.39% to $142.96 on a day when the broader market, including the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average, experienced declines. The company's stock closed significantly below its 52-week high but outperformed some competitors like Cisco Systems Inc., while Juniper Networks Inc. saw a larger gain. Trading volume for F5 Inc. was higher than its 50-day average.

title: Here Are Cisco’s Most Highly Compensated Executives In 2022
summary: Cisco's 2022 proxy filing revealed the top-compensated executives, highlighting their contributions to the company's business model transformation, particularly its shift towards subscription-based services and Everything-as-a-Service offerings. The list includes CEO Chuck Robbins, CFO Scott Herren, and newly added executives like Chief Legal Officer Dev Stahlkopf and Chief Customer and Partner Officer Jeff Sharritts, alongside veterans like Maria Martinez and Gerri Elliott. The compensation packages, detailed from lowest to highest, reflect a mix of salaries, stock awards, and incentive plans, with notable bonuses for new hires.

title: Saving Water and Improving Sustainability with Cisco and Rockwell Automation
summary: Cisco and Rockwell Automation have partnered to develop innovative water and wastewater solutions aimed at improving sustainability. Their joint Digital Water Solutions leverage near real-time anomaly detection to reduce water loss, save energy, and decrease CO2 emissions in water processing systems. This collaboration, supported by Cisco's Country Digital Acceleration program, seeks to address the significant water and energy waste caused by leaky infrastructure.

title: It’s tough out there for cybersecurity leaders at SMBs. Here’s how to fight.
summary: Cybersecurity leaders at small and mid-sized businesses face significant challenges due to increasingly sophisticated attacks. This article outlines fundamental security practices, emphasizing endpoint security as a critical defense. It also details three main types of cyberattacks—phishing, active exploitation, and leveraging the user—and provides actionable recommendations for mitigating each, including MFA, patching, DNS security, and user education.

title: 6 Top Firewall as a Service Providers: Browse FWaaS Companies
summary: This article identifies and reviews six leading Firewall as a Service (FWaaS) providers, explaining how FWaaS extends next-generation firewall capabilities across modern IT environments. It details key features, differentiators, user reviews, and honors for each vendor, including Cato Networks, Cisco Umbrella, Cloudflare, Palo Alto Networks, Check Point’s SASE, and Zscaler. The analysis highlights the integration of FWaaS within broader secure access service edge (SASE) and zero-trust network architecture (ZTNA) solutions.

title: Charter changes approach for its cable access network – sources
summary: Charter Communications is shifting its distributed access architecture strategy for its hybrid fiber/coax (HFC) network, now favoring remote PHY (R-PHY) with a virtual cable modem termination system (CMTS) over remote MACPHY. This change, which follows an in-depth review, aligns Charter more closely with broader industry trends and aims to create greater economies of scale, particularly benefiting from Comcast's existing R-PHY deployments. While Charter's primary focus for DOCSIS 4.0 remains Extended Spectrum DOCSIS (ESD), the move to R-PHY and vCMTS could open new opportunities for chipmakers and various network equipment vendors.

title: Cisco’s partnership with NCCU sets a new paradigm for learning
summary: Cisco partnered with North Carolina Central University (NCCU) to implement a comprehensive network upgrade, which included integrating classrooms, enhancing security, and enabling widespread connectivity for students. This initiative allowed NCCU to support hybrid learning models, including HyFlex, and facilitated remote education during the COVID-19 pandemic. The partnership also led to the establishment of a cybersecurity certificate program and lab, addressing the growing demand for cyber protection professionals and fostering a talent pipeline.

title: Qualcomm Inc. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors
summary: Shares of Qualcomm Inc. (QCOM) fell 2.89% on Thursday, closing at $114.70, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose and the S&P 500 Index fell. This marked the stock's second consecutive day of losses and positioned it below its 52-week high. Qualcomm underperformed some competitors like NVIDIA Corp. and Cisco Systems Inc., though it fared slightly better than Alphabet Inc. Class A.

title: Connected Justice Helps Courts Work for the People
summary: Cisco and ePlus have partnered to deliver "Connected Justice" solutions, transforming the judicial system by improving accessibility and efficiency. These solutions, including virtual hearings and enhanced security, address pre-existing accessibility issues and challenges exacerbated by the pandemic. The initiative has led to significant improvements, such as a reduction in bench warrants, and is expanding to include virtual visitations and educational opportunities for incarcerated individuals.

title: Cisco Equips Partners to Own New Opportunities Through Programs and Innovation Introduced at 27th Annual Cisco Partner Summit
summary: Cisco is holding its 27th Annual Partner Summit from November 1-3, 2022, in Las Vegas, bringing together thousands of global partners. The event focuses on empowering partners through program updates, new technology solutions in security, applications, customer experience, hybrid work, and cloud-scale networking, and opportunities to scale growth. Cisco emphasizes its partner-led growth strategy and introduces initiatives for partners to drive digital inclusion and economic empowerment.

title: 3 Technology Stocks With 3%+ Yields to Buy Today
summary: This article identifies three technology stocks—Cisco (CSCO), Skyworks Solutions (SWKS), and Texas Instruments (TXN)—that offer dividend yields of over 3% and strong total return potential. Despite a challenging year for the tech sector, these companies are highlighted for their reasonable valuations, robust business models, and prospects for double-digit annual returns through a combination of earnings growth, dividend income, and multiple expansion.

title: Marcus Moffett Promoted to Cisco U.S. Public Sector VP of Engineering
summary: Cisco has announced the promotion of Marcus Moffett to Vice President of U.S. Public Sector Engineering. Moffett, a 23-year Cisco veteran with extensive experience, will lead a team of over 500 engineering leaders, systems engineers, and architects responsible for designing secure, intelligent platforms for public sector customers. His promotion is highlighted as a testament to his technical excellence, leadership, and dedication to the team.

title: Cisco Introduces Six New Partner Solution Specializations to Meet Customer Demands
summary: Cisco has expanded its partner program with six new solution specializations, aiming to help partners meet evolving customer demands and capitalize on market opportunities in areas like hybrid cloud, hybrid work, SASE, and full-stack observability. These specializations are designed to highlight partners' expertise and value to customers, making it easier for clients to identify and work with partners proficient in specific cross-architectural solutions. The initiative emphasizes partner differentiation and the co-innovation happening between Cisco and its partners, demonstrating Cisco's commitment to supporting partner competitiveness.

title: Announcing Cisco’s Partner Summit 2022 Global Award Winners!
summary: Cisco announced the 21 global winners of its Partner Summit 2022 Global Awards, recognizing top-performing partners across various categories. These awards celebrate partners' outstanding achievements in supporting customers and communities, ranging from security and software to enterprise networking and social impact. The winners were honored in person in Las Vegas for their impressive performance amidst challenging times.

title: Still Using Passwords? Get Started with Phishing-Resistant, Passwordless Authentication Now!
summary: Cisco Duo has made its passwordless authentication generally available across all Duo Editions. This solution aims to simplify the passwordless journey for organizations and supports a zero-trust security strategy by integrating with existing SAML identity providers or using Duo SSO. It offers flexible, phishing-resistant authentication options like FIDO2-compliant biometrics and Duo Mobile, enhancing user experience and mitigating credential-based risks.

title: At Cisco Partner Summit, a celebration of shared strength
summary: Cisco's Partner Summit 2022 emphasized the crucial role of its global partner ecosystem in addressing current business and global challenges. CEO Chuck Robbins highlighted the company's shared history and future vision, focusing on co-innovation, market leadership, and customer investment. Executives discussed the shift towards an "age of the partner," requiring flexible technologies and integrated engagement to meet evolving customer experiences and leverage solutions like full-stack observability, simplified networking, and secure hybrid work platforms.

title: Deployment Options Expand with New Cisco DNA Center Virtual Appliance
summary: Cisco has introduced the Cisco DNA Center Virtual Appliance, offering customers new deployment flexibility for their network controller in data centers, public, and private clouds. This virtual appliance provides benefits such as eliminating capital expenditures, supply chain concerns, and offers fast, automated deployment with high availability. It supports deployment in public clouds like AWS (with Azure and GCP to follow) or in virtualized environments like VMware ESXi, while maintaining feature parity with the physical appliance.

title: Bret Hull
summary: This article profiles Bret Hull, the Senior Vice President of Network Platform Engineering at Cisco. It highlights his role in leading a global team in software engineering, security, and product design, his tenure at Cisco since 2010, and his prior research work at MIT. The profile also mentions his educational background from U.C. Berkeley and MIT.

title: What is a Data Lake?
summary: This article defines a data lake as a centralized repository for data stored in its native form, accommodating structured, unstructured, and semi-structured data. It differentiates data lakes from data warehouses, highlighting their utility for unknown future data uses and their scalable architecture. The article also specifically discusses security data lakes and introduces Check Point's Infinity Events as a solution.

title: Unified Communication as a Service (UcaaS) Market | Industry Trends and Forecast 2022-2030
summary: Acumen Research and Consulting has published a report on the Unified Communication as a Service (UCaaS) Market, providing an in-depth study of current industry trends, recent developments, and the competitive landscape. The report covers market segmentation by deployment and industry vertical, as well as a geographic analysis across major regions. It includes competitive analysis of leading players and identifies opportunity areas for investors in the UCaaS market up to 2030.

title: Cisco to open new chip design center in Barcelona, Spain says
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (CSCO.O) is set to open a new semiconductor chip design center in Barcelona, Spain, supported by government subsidies under the PERTE plan. This initiative is part of the European Union's broader strategy to strengthen its chip industry and reduce dependency on foreign supply, aiming for 20% of the global chip market by 2030. The announcement follows a similar large-scale investment by Volkswagen's Spanish unit for electric vehicle and battery production.

title: $1.5 billion firm’s new HQ is 80% smaller than its old space
summary: Kforce, a $1.5 billion technology staffing company, has opened a new 22,232-square-foot hybrid work headquarters in Tampa, which is 82% smaller than its previous 130,000-square-foot campus. This move reflects a growing trend towards hybrid work models, contributing to rising office vacancy rates in the city. The company's new space in Midtown Tampa is designed for collaboration and team-building, emphasizing flexibility and trust for its 2,000 employees nationwide.

title: Cisco to open its first EU chip design center in Barcelona
summary: Cisco Systems announced it will establish its first EU chip design center in Barcelona, Spain, aiming to bolster Europe's semiconductor ecosystem. This initiative aligns with Spain's €12 billion PERTE program and the EU's strategy to reduce dependence on chip production in China. The investment's specific amount was not disclosed, but the project is expected to consolidate Barcelona as a technological hub.

title: 6 Steps to Become an Inventor
summary: Cisco technical leaders Praveen Kumar and Navdeep Sood share their journey of innovation and how they became inventors within the company. They discuss their experience submitting over 100 patents and the importance of a strong support system. The article outlines six steps for aspiring inventors to begin their own innovation process, encouraging them to be brave and start today.

title: Grainger subsidiary decamping Buffalo Grove for West Loop
summary: Zoro, an e-commerce platform and subsidiary of W.W. Grainger, is moving its headquarters from Buffalo Grove to Chicago's West Loop. The company signed an 11-year lease for nearly 42,000 square feet at 500 W. Madison St., consolidating its Buffalo Grove and existing downtown offices. This move reflects a continuing trend of companies relocating to urban centers to attract and retain young talent.

title: Prisma SD-WAN Chatbot for Slack Enables Intelligent Operations
summary: Palo Alto Networks has introduced the Prisma SD-WAN Chatbot for Slack CloudBlade, aiming to enhance collaboration and operational efficiency for organizations using SD-WAN solutions. This chatbot allows IT and non-IT staff to query network and application performance information using natural language in Slack, and receive instant notifications for alerts. It simplifies troubleshooting, reduces context switching, and provides secure, anytime, anywhere visibility into the SD-WAN environment.

title: Xerox onboards John Bruno as President and COO
summary: Xerox Holdings Corporation has announced the appointment of John Bruno as President and Chief Operating Officer (COO), following Steven Bandrowczak's promotion to CEO. Bruno will collaborate closely with the leadership team to shape global strategy, enhance operating models, and oversee various key business operations including global offerings, service delivery, and IT services. His extensive experience in business, technology, and operations at Fortune 500 companies is expected to advance Xerox's client-centric operating model and improve customer experience.

title: Cisco Systems Q1 23 Earnings Conference Call At 4:30 PM ET
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (CSCO) is scheduled to host a conference call on November 16, 2022, at 4:30 PM ET to discuss its Q1 23 earnings results. Interested participants can access the live webcast or listen to the call using provided dial-in numbers. A replay will also be available for those unable to attend live.

title: Cisco Plans Restructuring While Giving Upbeat Forecast
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. announced a restructuring plan, including job cuts and office space reduction, while simultaneously providing an optimistic revenue forecast. The company expects a 4.5% to 6.5% increase in sales for the current quarter and up to 6.5% growth for fiscal 2023, exceeding analyst expectations. This strategic move aims to adapt to evolving business conditions.

title: How Cisco Systems Regained Its Competitive Edge, Avoided IBM's Problem
summary: Cisco Systems (CSCO) has regained its competitive strength and avoided the long-term problems faced by IBM through continuous innovation. After a period of declining revenues and negative Economic Value Added (EVA), Cisco's revenue growth has resumed, driven by strong quarterly earnings and a positive outlook for fiscal 2023. While Wall Street has responded positively to recent financial results, the company still needs to convince markets that this comeback is sustainable.

title: Cisco layoffs won't affect Israeli workforce
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. announced layoffs of 4,000 employees globally, representing 5% of its workforce. However, sources close to Cisco in Israel stated that the company's 800 employees in the country would not be affected by these cuts. This decision comes shortly after Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins praised Israel as a model of innovation and highlighted the company's significant investments and acquisitions there.

title: Ethernet Switch and Router Market Size to Grow by USD 6074.87 million From 2022 to 2027, Assessment on Parent Market, Five Forces Analysis, Market Dynamics & Segmentation - Technavio
summary: The global Ethernet Switch and Router Market is projected to grow by USD 6074.87 million from 2022 to 2027, with a CAGR of 2.67% and a Y-o-Y growth rate of 1.61%. This growth is primarily driven by the increasing demand for colocation data centers among SMEs. North America is expected to contribute 42% to this growth, largely due to the adoption of data centers by various service providers and governments. The market will also see trends like the rising use of open Ethernet switches, despite challenges posed by interoperability issues.

title: New CIO Wants Cisco to Be a Model for Hybrid Work
summary: Cisco Systems Inc.'s new CIO, Fletcher Previn, aims to establish the company as a leader in hybrid work by improving the employee experience for remote workers. He plans to further automate internal processes and refine IT to enhance team agility. Previn emphasized that managing hybrid work presents more complex challenges than solely remote work during the pandemic.

title: DOCSIS 4.0 CPE costs won't break the bank – Liberty Global CTO
summary: Liberty Global CTO Enrique Rodriguez believes that Customer Premises Equipment (CPE) for DOCSIS 4.0 will be comparably priced to current DOCSIS 3.1 gear, despite anticipated higher network upgrade costs. He outlined Liberty Global's varied network evolution strategy across different markets, incorporating both DOCSIS 4.0 and XGS-PON. Rodriguez also acknowledged industry disagreement on CPE pricing and expressed concerns about the bifurcation of DOCSIS 4.0 into FDX and ESD options.

title: British CEO appointed by AI platform Boost.ai
summary: Boost.ai, a Norwegian conversational AI leader, has appointed Briton Jerry Haywood as its new CEO to drive global growth. Haywood, with 20 years of enterprise technology experience from companies like IBM, Cisco, and Salesforce, replaces founder Lars Ropeid Selsås, who will now focus on product development. The company aims to accelerate its growth and become a global category leader in conversational AI under Haywood's leadership.

title: Retail shopping through a new lens
summary: This article showcases the Cisco Store Tech Lab, a 2500-square-foot retail space at Cisco's headquarters that doubles as a testing ground for Cisco and partner retail solutions. It details the use of Meraki MV cameras for security and customer experience insights, highlighting their easy setup, onboard storage, and analytics capabilities. The article also touches on privacy safeguards, future plans for deploying the technology at events like Cisco Live, and integrating solutions with partners like Everyangle for advanced computer vision analytics.

title: Security in Network Design: Key Considerations from a Network Architect’s Perspective
summary: This article emphasizes that security is a collective responsibility in network design, not just for specialists. It delves into key security considerations based on the CCDE v3.0 Written exam blueprint, including segmentation, network access control, visibility, policy enforcement, the CIA triad (Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability), and regulatory compliance. The author, Daniel Dib, stresses that a good network design always meets requirements, and security must be an integral part of the design process from the outset.

title: Nutanix buyout may be on the cards with HPE sniffing around
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is reportedly considering acquiring hyperconverged infrastructure vendor Nutanix, with discussions ongoing for several months. The potential acquisition aligns with HPE's GreenLake everything-as-a-service (XaaS) platform strategy, aiming to enhance its software offerings and attract more customers. Nutanix's recent financial recovery and improved market cap make it an attractive target, especially given HPE's struggles in expanding the GreenLake model to its global partners.

title: U.S. Supreme Court rejects Centripetal appeal in Cisco patent fight
summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has rejected Centripetal Networks Inc's appeal to reinstate a $2.75 billion patent infringement award against Cisco Systems Inc. The award was previously overturned due to a conflict of interest involving the trial judge's wife owning Cisco stock. The case will now return to a lower court for further proceedings.

title: Wi-Fi NOW Awards: List of finalists announced! Read who made it to the final round here.
summary: Wi-Fi NOW has announced the finalists for its annual awards across various categories, including Affordable Connectivity, Best Wi-Fi IoT Product, Enterprise Wi-Fi Solution, and more. The winners will be revealed at the Wi-Fi NOW Awards Gala on Monday, December 12. This year saw nearly double the number of entrants compared to the previous year, indicating increased competition and value for the awards.

title: Manchester City Collaborates with Cisco to Deploy Crowd Intelligence Solution, WaitTime
summary: Manchester City Football Club has partnered with Cisco to deploy WaitTime, a real-time crowd intelligence solution, at Etihad Stadium, making it the first Premier League club to use this technology. WaitTime leverages artificial intelligence and uses existing Cisco Meraki cameras to provide data on crowd density and movement, aiming to enhance the fan experience and operational efficiency. Following a successful pilot, Manchester City plans to expand the deployment within the stadium and integrate live data with digital signage to direct fans to less congested areas.

title: Adobe Eliminates 100 Jobs in Sales, Joins Other Companies in Using Job Cuts to Reduce Expenses
summary: Adobe Inc. has eliminated 100 jobs in its sales division, aligning with other tech companies reducing expenses through workforce adjustments. Despite these cuts, Adobe stated it is not undergoing company-wide layoffs and is still hiring for critical roles, reassigning some affected employees to support "critical initiatives." The company's adjustments are significantly smaller compared to thousands of job cuts reported by other tech giants this year.

title: HPE GreenLake Adds AWS’ Kubernetes Service and Other Hybrid Cloud Capabilities
summary: HPE GreenLake Private Cloud Enterprise has expanded its availability worldwide and is introducing new hybrid cloud capabilities. These enhancements include support for Amazon Web Services’ Kubernetes software and an improved analytics tool for measuring both private and public cloud usage and costs. The updates aim to provide greater flexibility and control for enterprises managing hybrid cloud environments, further solidifying HPE's as-a-service model for on-premises solutions.

title: For HPE, The Cloud Is A Private And A Public Matter
summary: HPE is focusing on its GreenLake utility platform to expand its presence in the hybrid cloud market, emphasizing private cloud offerings. The company aims to make its entire portfolio available as a service and is seeing steady growth in GreenLake's orders and revenue. HPE is enhancing GreenLake with new services like Private Cloud Enterprise and expanding partner offerings, catering to businesses that prefer a mix of public and private cloud solutions, especially for edge computing needs.

title: EVPN Myth Buster Series – To lead or follow, where does Cisco stand?
summary: This article from Cisco Blogs discusses Cisco's leadership in networking technology, specifically focusing on its role in the development and standardization of Ethernet VPN (EVPN). It highlights Cisco's contributions to IETF RFCs for EVPN and related technologies, asserting that Cisco innovates and leads while other vendors often follow and adopt these standards. The article also provides a historical overview of EVPN and its evolution, emphasizing Cisco's long-standing commitment to open standards and interoperability.

title: Fortinet Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
summary: Shares of Fortinet Inc. (FTNT) rose 2.54% on Thursday, outperforming the broader market and some competitors on a positive trading day. The stock closed strong, contributing to its second consecutive day of gains, despite remaining below its 52-week high. Competitors such as Cisco Systems Inc. and CrowdStrike Holdings Inc. also saw gains, while Palo Alto Networks Inc. experienced a slight decline.

title: We were All In at Cisco Connect LatAm!
summary: Cisco recently hosted Cisco Connect LatAm, gathering Latin America's top IT professionals, customers, experts, and partners to discuss technology's future after a two-year hiatus. The event showcased Cisco's latest solutions and trends in Spanish, emphasizing digital transformation, hybrid work, and security. Leaders like Laercio Albuquerque and Rachel Barger highlighted technology as essential for quality of life and business, while the Country Digital Acceleration program's impact was also discussed.

title: OTEGLOBE Strengthens Its European Network to Support the Internet for the Future with Cisco
summary: OTEGLOBE is increasing its network capacity and performance through a collaboration with Cisco, deploying new Cisco 8800 series routers powered by Cisco Silicon One. This upgrade provides an 800G-ready infrastructure, connecting more people and businesses across Europe faster and more efficiently. The transition from 100G to 400G happened rapidly to meet rising bandwidth demands and positions OTEGLOBE for future 800G needs, while also improving sustainability through reduced power and space requirements.

title: [Latest] Global E-Learning Market Size/Share Worth USD 848.1 Billion by 2030 at a 17.53% CAGR: Custom Market Insights (Analysis, Outlook, Leaders, Report, Trends, Forecast, Segmentation, Growth, Growth Rate, Value)
summary: The global E-Learning market, valued at approximately USD 210 Billion in 2021, is projected to reach USD 848.1 Billion by 2030, exhibiting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 17.53% between 2022 and 2030. Key drivers include increased e-learning adoption during the pandemic and technological advancements, while challenges involve data security concerns. The Learning Management System (LMS) technology and North America are noted as dominant segments within the market.

title: Making private 5G interconnect easy to configure, simple to operate, and widely adopted
summary: This article discusses the challenges and potential solutions for scaling private 5G network interconnect. Cisco, in collaboration with the 5G DRIVE project, is exploring how to simplify 5G roaming interconnect with private wireless networks by leveraging the new 5G Security Edge Protection Proxy (SEPP) and adapting N32 transport for adoption by private networks using WebSockets. The piece highlights a proof of concept demonstration that shows how established cellular roaming interfaces can be modified to streamline interconnectivity between private 5G networks and credential holders, mirroring the success of OpenRoaming for Wi-Fi.

title: T-Mobile and Cisco Launch World’s Largest Cloud Native Converged Core Gateway
summary: T-Mobile collaborated with Cisco to launch the world's largest cloud-native converged core gateway, moving all 5G and 4G traffic to it. This initiative has already resulted in over a 10% improvement in speed and latency for customers. The new core gateway simplifies operations and accelerates the deployment of new 5G and IoT applications for T-Mobile.

title: Behind the Logo: The Origin of Cisco
summary: This article details the origins and evolution of Cisco, a major networking hardware and software manufacturer. It explores how the company was founded in 1984 by Leonard Bosack and Sandy Lerner, its early intellectual property disputes with Stanford University, and the symbolic meaning behind its iconic logo, which pays tribute to San Francisco and the Golden Gate Bridge. The article also traces the redesigns of the Cisco logo over the years, reflecting the company's changing focus and growth milestones.

title: Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center
summary: The Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center offers comprehensive, centralized management for multiple firewalls, intrusion prevention, advanced malware detection, and security automation. It provides real-time threat identification and protection across networks. While powerful and feature-rich, its complexity may pose challenges for small businesses without dedicated IT resources.

title: Fortinet FortiGate Firewall
summary: The Fortinet FortiGate Firewall provides robust, unified threat protection for small to mid-sized network systems, offering advanced features like deep packet inspection, intrusion prevention, VPN support, and real-time threat detection. It can be deployed virtually or on-premises, integrates with multi-vendor cloud environments, and is managed through an intuitive web-based interface. While highly scalable, scaling requires hardware replacement, and the GUI can occasionally freeze.

title: Kyndryl Receives Recognitions from ISG and Gartner® for Managed Network Services
summary: Kyndryl announced it has received recognitions from leading analyst firms ISG and Gartner for its managed network services, just one year after becoming an independent company. The company achieved leadership standing in the 2022 ISG Provider Lens™ Network Software Defined Solutions and Services Report for the U.S. and was recognized as a Niche Player in Gartner's Magic Quadrant for Managed Network Services. These recognitions underscore Kyndryl's growth strategy, advanced delivery, and strategic alliances in the network and edge computing space.

title: Microsoft, Cisco Shareholder Votes Demonstrate Increasing Investor Demand for Tax and Offshore Transparency
summary: Shareholders of Microsoft and Cisco, representing hundreds of billions in investments, voted in favor of proposals calling for public country-by-country reporting on offshore operations and tax practices. This trend indicates growing investor demand for transparency regarding potential risks associated with opaque offshore activities. The FACT Coalition highlights that public country-by-country reporting is becoming inevitable due to global momentum and regulatory considerations in jurisdictions like the EU, Australia, and the U.S.

title: Palo Alto Networks, Cisco, Fortinet Led Q3 Security Market
summary: The cybersecurity market experienced significant growth in Q3, increasing by 15.9% to $17.8 billion, led by Palo Alto Networks, Cisco, and Fortinet. Palo Alto Networks held the largest market share at 8.4%, with endpoint security being the fastest-growing category and North America remaining the largest market. The shift to subscription-based models and strong channel sales contributed to this growth, with channel partners optimistic about future opportunities.

title: Philippines’ PLDT Talking to Cisco, Huawei on Budget Overrun
summary: Philippines' largest phone company, PLDT Inc., is in discussions with four major vendors, including Cisco Systems Inc. and Huawei Technologies Co. Ltd., regarding a 48-billion peso ($859 million) budget overrun over four years. The company is seeking to cancel some projects and obtain discounts from suppliers to reduce this overspending.

title: What's next: Predictions, challenges and IT trends for 2023
summary: Despite a challenging economic climate, businesses anticipate increased IT spending in 2023, particularly in cybersecurity, AI, and automation, driven by ongoing skills shortages and growing IT complexity. Organizations that have embraced digital transformation are keen to continue investing, seeing long-term gains even amid economic downturns. This shift also redefines the business-IT relationship, with IT operations becoming central to achieving business success and strategic initiatives.

title: Cisco Systems to eliminate 80 jobs in San Francisco
summary: Cisco Systems is eliminating 80 jobs in San Francisco as part of global job losses announced in November. This information was detailed in letters sent to California employment officials by the company. The layoffs affect the San Francisco office, while the company's headquarters remain in San Jose.

title: Warning over ransomware attacks spreading via Fortinet kit
