summary: Ransomware operators are exploiting a critical authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2022-40684) in Fortinet VPN devices, which was disclosed in October 2022. eSentire's Threat Research Unit has observed threat actors scanning for vulnerable devices and successfully exploiting them in two attacks against customers, with access to compromised devices also being sold on the dark web. The security firm emphasizes the risks posed by misconfigured and third-party managed SSL VPNs, highlighting the need for vigilant monitoring and endpoint security solutions.

title: Fiscalnote Appoints Will Wilkinson as General Manager
summary: FiscalNote Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: NOTE) has appointed Will Wilkinson as General Manager of Geopolitical & Market Intelligence. Wilkinson brings over two decades of international business management expertise, previously holding senior positions at Dow Jones Corp. and The Economist Group. In his new role, he will oversee and enhance FiscalNote's leading solutions like FrontierView, Oxford Analytica, and Predata, focusing on expanding product lines and strengthening customer relationships.

title: Ransomware attack against Yum! Brands follows several incidents targeting restaurant industry
summary: Yum! Brands, the parent company of KFC, Taco Bell, and Pizza Hut, recently disclosed a ransomware attack that resulted in the temporary closure of nearly 300 U.K. restaurants and data exfiltration. The company has initiated an investigation and is working to restore affected systems, though it does not anticipate a material adverse impact on its business. This incident adds to a growing list of cyberattacks targeting the restaurant and food delivery industries, which criminals view as vulnerable to credential stuffing and financial fraud due to potential lax security compared to other sectors.

title: Technology & Innovation Highlights from Q4 2022
summary: Lockheed Martin's Q4 2022 highlights include the successful completion of the Orion spacecraft's NASA mission, a significant F-35 procurement contract with Germany, and advancements in hypersonic flight testing. The company also made strides in sustainable energy storage, optionally piloted Black Hawk helicopters, and secured full-rate production for the CH-53K helicopter. Other notable achievements involve new production sites, space communication technology, inflatable habitats, radar contracts, and the delivery of the Mid-Range Capability battery to the U.S. Army.

title: 2022 SecureIQLab: Command and Control Comparative Report
summary: The 2022 SecureIQLab report evaluates the effectiveness of next-generation firewalls against Command and Control capabilities of the Cobalt Strike attack suite, a tool increasingly used by threat actors for obfuscating network payloads. The study compared Palo Alto Networks' Advanced Threat Prevention against solutions from Check Point, Cisco, and Fortinet across six attack scenarios. The report assesses how well these security solutions block sophisticated evasive techniques used by Red Team tools.

title: Fortinet Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
summary: Fortinet Inc. (FTNT) stock increased by 2.80% to $53.21 on a strong trading day, outperforming the S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average. This marked the stock's sixth consecutive day of gains, closing below its 52-week high. While the stock rose, some competitors like Cisco Systems Inc., CrowdStrike Holdings Inc. Cl A, and Palo Alto Networks Inc. also saw gains.

title: Cisco embarks on digital transformation with Adobe
summary: Cisco is undertaking a three-year digital transformation of Cisco.com, dubbed "Cisco.com Reimagined," in partnership with Adobe. The initiative aims to modernize the website, improve customer experience, and support the company's shift towards a SaaS distribution model. By migrating to Adobe Experience Manager as a Cloud Service and leveraging other Adobe solutions, Cisco has seen significant improvements in website performance, personalization capabilities, and time to market.

title: Cisco Advances the Hybrid Work Experience with Audio and Interoperability Innovations
summary: Cisco showcased new collaboration devices for Microsoft Teams and unveiled the Cisco Table Microphone Pro, along with audio interoperability advancements, at Integrated Systems Europe (ISE) 2023. These innovations aim to enhance the hybrid work experience by offering more inclusivity and choice for meetings, while also improving manageability, configuration, and security for IT. Notably, Cisco is partnering with Microsoft to run Microsoft Teams natively on Cisco collaboration devices, expected for certification in March 2023, and introducing advanced spatial audio and noise cancellation capabilities.

title: AV for startups and SMEs
summary: This article discusses the importance of effective audio-visual (AV) solutions for startups and SMEs in today's hybrid work environment. It highlights how accessible unified communications and conferencing (UCC) technologies have become, emphasizing simplicity, ubiquity, and flexibility through options like "bring your own device" (BYOD) and platform standardization. The piece also covers advanced AV applications such as meeting equity, digital signage, cloud telephony, and the future role of AR/VR and the Metaverse in business communication.

title: Rima Alameddine Joins IonQ to Expand Enterprise Sales
summary: Rima Alameddine, a former NVIDIA sales executive, has joined IonQ as Chief Revenue Officer to expand its commercial business. She will focus on helping enterprise customers identify critical workloads for quantum computers and fostering an ecosystem of partnerships. Alameddine aims to leverage her experience in technology adoption and her passion for diversity to drive quantum enterprise adoption forward, drawing parallels to her previous role at Nvidia.

title: People Behind Purpose at Cisco: How a passion for measuring impact led to a leadership role in ESG reporting
summary: This article highlights Amanda Cumberland, Cisco's Director of ESG Strategy and Reporting, Insights, and Impact, and her role in leading the creation of the company's annual Purpose Report and ESG Reporting Hub. It details her background in research and statistics, her passion for measuring impact, and the extensive process involved in compiling Cisco's ESG reporting with contributions from numerous teams and subject matter experts. The piece emphasizes the importance of transparent ESG reporting for stakeholders and showcases how Cisco's purpose is "personified" through various individuals and initiatives.

title: Cisco secures IoT, keeping security closer to networking
summary: Cisco is addressing the growing challenge of securing IoT and unmanaged devices by integrating security capabilities directly into its networking infrastructure. The article highlights how Cisco's Secure Firewall, operating on Catalyst 9000 Series switches, provides enhanced security through granular segmentation, centralized management for isolated IoT networks, and encryption of IoT traffic. This approach aims to unify IT and IoT by making the network both a sensor and an enforcer for security policies.

title: How Internet Outages Impact Transportation
summary: The transportation industry, including airlines and public transit, is critically reliant on constant connectivity, and system downtime can lead to significant financial losses and reputational damage. Increased adoption of IoT and digital transformation further amplifies this reliance, making proactive management of Internet unpredictability crucial. Cisco's ThousandEyes offers end-to-end visibility into network performance to help transportation companies predict and prevent costly outages, improve operational efficiency, and enhance passenger safety and experience.

title: New Cisco Innovation Helps Organizations Meet Sustainability Targets
summary: Cisco has launched Carbon Emissions Insights in Webex Control Hub, an industry-first tool designed to help organizations track and reduce their carbon footprint. This innovation provides scope 2 emissions reporting and recommendations for optimizing energy consumption from Cisco Webex devices, aligning with Cisco's goal of achieving net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2040. The feature will be available starting in summer and integrates with customer sustainability reporting tools to assist IT administrators in meeting ESG targets.

title: Buildings are a huge source of CO2. Cisco has a solution.
summary: Cisco is addressing the significant carbon emissions from buildings, which account for over 30% of all CO2, through its innovative "energy networking" solution. This approach integrates AI, IoT, network visibility, and low-voltage DC microgrids to optimize energy usage, reduce emissions, cut costs, and securely connect renewable energy sources. Denise Lee, VP for Cisco's Engineering Sustainability Office, highlights the Penn 1 office retrofit as an example, demonstrating over a 40% reduction in energy waste and showcasing the potential for net-positive energy buildings.

title: Transforming IT Modernization
summary: The article discusses the need for government agencies to shift their IT modernization strategy from traditional Development, Modernization, and Enhancement (DME) budgeting to a "New O&M" approach that integrates Optimization and Modernization into Operations and Maintenance budgets. This shift is crucial for accelerating digital transformation, leveraging software-defined infrastructure, AI/ML, and fostering continuous innovation to improve mission and business outcomes. The author highlights the decline in traditional DME spending and emphasizes enterprise-wide, holistic architectural perspectives for modernization.

title: Cisco Live Amsterdam: All in on security, sustainability, and simplicity
summary: Cisco Live Amsterdam focused on security, sustainability, and simplicity, with company leaders discussing platform strategies, full-stack observability, and AI. The event highlighted Cisco's commitment to supporting transformation by addressing customer concerns like IT complexity and emphasizing inclusive future initiatives. Discussions covered innovations in networking, security products like Cisco Security Cloud, and efforts toward Net Zero status by 2040.

title: Inside the Gray Market Keeping Cisco Tech in Stock in Russia
summary: Despite Cisco Systems Inc. stopping all sales to Russia and broad U.S. sanctions after the invasion of Ukraine, Cisco equipment remains readily available in Moscow. This is due to a "gray market" supplied by unauthorized third-party vendors in countries like Turkey and Asia, operating largely beyond the reach of American enforcement.

title: Extreme Networks extends network fabric to the edge
summary: Extreme Networks has extended its software-defined WAN (SD-WAN) into a unified fabric reaching organizational branches, simplifying deployments and enhancing application performance monitoring. This integration unifies wired, wireless, and WAN networking through ExtremeCloud IQ, enabling easier network control and consistent user experiences across locations. The development follows Extreme's 2021 acquisition of Ipanema, strengthening its SD-WAN offering amidst competition.

title: Cisco gear getting into Russia is ‘disappointing’ and ‘frustrating,’ exec says
summary: Cisco CFO Scott Herren expressed frustration and disappointment over the continued flow of Cisco networking gear into Russia through "gray markets," despite the company's official exit from the country. Products are reportedly entering Russia via unauthorized vendors in regions like Turkey and Asia, bypassing U.S. enforcement. This situation highlights the challenges companies face in controlling their products' distribution once they leave official channels, especially in conflict-sensitive areas.

title: Cisco (CSCO) Stock Rallies After Forecast Shows Tech Spending Holding Up
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. saw its stock rally after announcing an upbeat forecast suggesting that demand for tech infrastructure is stronger than anticipated. The company expects sales in the quarter ending in April to rise significantly more than what analysts had predicted, along with higher-than-expected profit per share. This indicates that corporate tech spending is holding up well.

title: 11:11 grows DR portfolio after Sungard buy
summary: 11:11 Systems has significantly expanded its disaster recovery portfolio through seven acquisitions in two years, including key parts of Sungard Availability Services. The company aims to compete with established managed services providers by offering a unified platform for security, connectivity, and storage, and plans to further develop its cyber resiliency offerings. Its strategy focuses on vendor-agnostic solutions and integrated managed services across cloud, connectivity, and security.

title: John Chambers: ‘Breaking away during the tough times is when the next generation of great companies gets formed’
summary: Former Cisco CEO John Chambers discusses the importance of speed and agility during turbulent economic times, emphasizing that historical periods of difficulty have often given rise to the next generation of great companies. He shares insights on reinvention, financial conservatism for his start-ups, navigating geopolitics, and his extensive work in promoting digital transformation in countries like France and India. Chambers also highlights the continuous learning and reinvention necessary for individuals and organizations to thrive in the current environment.

title: What’s next for the share prices of Palantir, Cisco and Twilio?
summary: Following a challenging period for technology companies, Palantir, Cisco, and Twilio all saw their share prices rise this week after beating earnings and revenue expectations. Despite these positive results and signs that inflation may have peaked, tech companies like Twilio are still cutting costs, with Twilio recently announcing a 17% staff reduction. Investors are optimistic about the sector's recovery in 2023, evident in funds like the ProShares Ultra Technology ETF experiencing significant year-to-date gains.

title: NTT named a Juniper Networks Partner of the Year
summary: NTT has been recognized for the second consecutive year as Juniper Networks' Global AI-Driven Enterprise Partner of the Year. This award acknowledges NTT's success in AI-driven enterprise solutions, its commitment to excellent customer experience, and achieving financial goals. The article highlights NTT's multivendor approach in networking, emphasizing their collaboration with partners like Juniper Networks to deliver innovative, AI-powered solutions, especially in AIOps for network management.

title: Cisco and NEC to Bring 5G Connectivity and Automation to Networks Across the Globe
summary: Cisco and NEC have announced an expansion of their collaboration to include system integration solutions and opportunities in 5G xHaul and private 5G to help customers transform their network architectures. This partnership aims to enhance end-to-end automation and routed optical networking for 5G transport networks and develop private 5G solutions, particularly for the Japanese market. The collaboration leverages Cisco's industry-leading solutions and NEC's expertise as a global network integrator.

title: Ronnie Vasishta Author Page
summary: This page profiles Ronnie Vasishta, NVIDIA's Head of Telecom and a key figure in the company's strategy and products for the telecommunications sector. It highlights his extensive background in the semiconductor and networking industries, including his previous roles at Intel and eASIC Corporation. The page also features a collection of articles authored or co-authored by Vasishta, focusing on AI trends in telecom, network optimization, and the adoption of generative AI in the industry.

title: ET Global Indian award: A passion for networking helped this 'Entrepreneur at Heart' make the switch
summary: Jayshree Ullal, CEO of Arista Networks, received the ET Global Indian award for her leadership in transforming Arista into a major player in the computer networking industry. Her move from Cisco to the then-startup Arista in 2008 was considered risky but led to significant growth, increasing the company's market cap from $2.5 billion to $38.52 billion. Ullal emphasizes her passion for networking and engaging with customers, advocating for skill-based valuation regardless of gender in the tech industry.

title: When It Comes To Zero Trust, Nobody Puts Appsec In A Corner
summary: Zero Trust adoption is increasing among large enterprises, but the role of application security (appsec) within this framework is often misunderstood. The article emphasizes that appsec, which protects the entire application stack and manages interactions between components, is a critical and integrated part of a robust Zero Trust architecture. It highlights that applications are a primary cause of breaches and secure DevOps (DevSecOps) is essential for finding and remediating vulnerabilities within a Zero Trust context.

title: HPE Buying Private 5G Network Provider Athonet for Telco, Aruba Enhancements
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is acquiring Italy-based Athonet, a provider of 4G and 5G mobile core software, to strengthen its private 5G network offerings. The acquisition aims to integrate Athonet's technology with HPE's GreenLake cloud services platform and Aruba networking portfolio, allowing enterprises to deploy Wi-Fi and private 5G services via a single subscription. Analysts suggest this move will simplify private 5G deployment for enterprises and combine with HPE's existing Wi-Fi assets for comprehensive network management.

title: OT/IoT Security: 3 Key Takeaways from Gartner Peer Insight’s Survey
summary: A survey by Cisco, Gartner Peer Insights, and Takepoint Research revealed that 77% of industrial organizations are in the early stages of their OT security journey, with none fully secured. The main goals are comprehensive visibility on OT devices, but cost and complexity of building out-of-band networks are significant obstacles. Cisco Cyber Vision offers an alternative by embedding visibility features into network equipment to overcome these challenges.

title: Cisco and NTT Collaborate to Bring Private 5G to Enterprise Customers to Accelerate Industry Transformation
summary: Cisco and NTT Ltd. announced a collaboration to accelerate Private 5G adoption across various enterprise sectors, including Automotive, Logistics, Healthcare, Retail, and Public. They plan to co-innovate and jointly market technology and managed services to help enterprises deploy Private 5G successfully and integrate it into existing infrastructure. This partnership aims to enable critical Industry 4.0 capabilities and reduce the technical and financial risks associated with managing 5G networks, building on their mutual commitment to a simple, managed private network solution.

title: Should You Fund Your 401(k) First or Your IRA?
summary: This article discusses the optimal order for funding retirement accounts, including 401(k)s, IRAs, and HSAs, and also covers recent financial news. It highlights the impending expiration of Vanguard's hybrid ETF/mutual fund patent and its potential impact on the fund market, along with earnings reports from Kraft Heinz, Roku, and Cisco Systems. The experts provide a hierarchy for allocating retirement savings, emphasizing employer match, HSAs, Roth IRAs, and then evaluating company 401(k)s.

title: Ethernet Switch Market Size, Share, Trends, Analysis, Growth Rate, and Forecast 2029
summary: The Ethernet Switch Market was valued at US$ 5.38 billion in 2021 and is projected to reach US$ 7.40 billion by 2029, growing at a CAGR of 4.07%. The market's expansion is primarily driven by increased Gigabit Ethernet usage, the adoption of Power over Ethernet (PoE), the convergence of residential and business networks, and the rising demand for internet-connected devices. Key growth segments include fixed configuration Ethernet switches and the data center sector, with significant contributions from SMEs and cloud adoption in regions like India.

title: Cisco Showcases Simple and Secure Wireless Experiences to Help Businesses Connect at MWC 23
summary: Cisco is showcasing new wireless, cloud, and IoT solutions at MWC 23, including its Mobility Services platform, Meraki 5G Gateways, and Wi-Fi 6E products, to simplify and secure connectivity for businesses. The company is partnering with global service providers and integrating Webex into Mercedes-Benz vehicles, while also announcing collaborations with Intel, NEC, NTT, and Qualcomm to advance 5G and IoT adoption. Cisco aims to help businesses navigate the digital era by providing innovative 5G and Wi-Fi connectivity solutions.

title: Cisco to acquire Valtix to enhance its Security Cloud portfolio
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. announced its plan to acquire cloud-native network security startup Valtix Inc. to bolster its Security Cloud portfolio. Valtix offers a multicloud network security platform known for its ease of deployment and comprehensive security coverage across major public cloud environments. This acquisition is expected to accelerate Cisco's goal of providing seamless workload security in multi-cloud settings.

title: Co-selling: It’s a BIG Deal!
summary: Co-selling is highlighted as a crucial engagement strategy for IT vendors to connect with line of business executives and drive new growth. The article defines co-selling as a multi-partner collaborative sales approach focused on delivering differentiated joint customer outcomes. It also outlines key elements for a successful co-sell model, including identifying the right partners, shifting sales paradigms, implementing new analytics, and digitizing collaboration.

title: Cisco works with Mercedes-Benz to create mobile office
summary: Cisco is partnering with Mercedes-Benz to integrate its Webex conferencing tools directly into the dashboards of the 2024 E-Class vehicles, transforming cars into mobile offices. While driving, users are limited to audio, but when parked, they can access all Webex features including video meetings, content sharing, and automatic transcription. This move allows Cisco to expand its collaboration tools into new markets, following a boost in video-conferencing platforms during the pandemic.

title: Racing to the Future: How Autonomous Vehicle Networks are Revolutionizing Connectivity
summary: This article discusses how autonomous vehicle networks are revolutionizing connectivity, focusing on the Indy Autonomous Challenge (IAC) as a test case. It highlights the critical need for ultra-reliable and high-speed wireless connectivity for true Level 4 & 5 autonomous vehicles, emphasizing how current networks are insufficient. Cisco's Ultra-Reliable Wireless Backhaul (CURWB) is presented as a solution enabling fiber-like performance and seamless connection handoffs, paving the way for future autonomous vehicle deployment.

title: Hitachi Vantara Names Sheila Rohra as Chief Business Strategy Officer
summary: Hitachi Vantara has appointed Sheila Rohra as its new Chief Business Strategy Officer. In this role, Rohra will be responsible for developing and executing transformative strategies, leveraging her extensive experience from Hewlett Packard Enterprise, NetApp, and Cisco Systems. Her focus will be on helping customers manage and utilize their data amidst complex hybrid and multicloud environments and global macroeconomic uncertainties.

title: CEO Antonio Neri On HPE’s Supercomputing ChatGPT Advantage, Axis Security And GreenLake Momentum
summary: HPE CEO Antonio Neri discusses the company's strong position to capitalize on the ChatGPT frenzy through its Supercomputing-as-a-Service offering, driven by its acquisition of Cray Supercomputer. He also highlights HPE's expanded edge portfolio with the acquisition of Axis Security, integrating it into the GreenLake platform for a comprehensive SASE model. Neri emphasizes the significant growth in HPE GreenLake's annualized revenue run rate and total contract value, underscoring the success of its as-a-service strategy and partner adoption.

title: NeutraDC, NAVER Cloud, dan Cisco Jalin Kolaborasi Peningkatan Adopsi Cloud Demi Mendorong Transformasi Digital
summary: NeutraDC, a subsidiary of PT Telkom Indonesia, has partnered with NAVER Cloud and Cisco to accelerate digital transformation in Indonesia's business sector. This collaboration aims to provide a reliable and secure cloud computing system, leveraging local knowledge and data center infrastructure. The partnership will also foster knowledge-sharing and talent development in cloud computing, offering Indonesian businesses increased agility, scalability, and cost efficiency.

title: Cisco Demonstrates Co-packaged Optics (CPO) System at OFC 2023
summary: Cisco demonstrated its Co-packaged Optics (CPO) system at OFC 2023, showcasing significant power efficiency improvements crucial for scaling data centers amid increasing bandwidth demands. The system leverages innovations like the removal of DSPs, a remote light source, and a production-proven Silicon Photonics platform to achieve substantial power savings. Cisco anticipates trial deployments with the 51.2Tb switch cycle and larger-scale adoption during the 101.2Tb switch cycle, highlighting its expertise in systems, ASICs, and optics for this technological transition.

title: Cybersecurity firm Palo Alto Networks names new CMO
summary: Palo Alto Networks has appointed KP Unnikrishnan as its new chief marketing officer, effective March 4. He will lead the global marketing team from the Santa Clara headquarters, reporting to chairman and CEO Nikesh Arora. Unnikrishnan, who has nearly a decade of experience with the company, previously served as vice president of marketing for Asia Pacific and Japan.

title: Cisco was recognized as a Visionary in the 2022 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Endpoint Protection Platforms
summary: Cisco has been recognized as a Visionary in the 2022 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Endpoint Protection Platforms, based on their Ability to Execute and Completeness of Vision. The company emphasizes its commitment to protecting organizations against evolving threats, especially for hybrid workforces, through solutions like Cisco Secure Endpoint and integrations with threat intelligence from Talos. Cisco aims to provide comprehensive security with greater visibility, faster detection, and simplified response across its security portfolio.

title: Taiwan blames Chinese ships for cut internet cables
summary: Taiwan has accused Chinese ships of cutting two internet cables connecting its outlying Matsu islands, severely disrupting daily life for residents. While Taiwan's government stopped short of calling it a deliberate act, some experts suspect China may have cut the cables as part of its ongoing harassment tactics against the self-ruled island. Repairs are expected by late April, and Taiwan is also exploring satellite internet as a backup, particularly after observing Russia's cyberattacks in Ukraine.

title: FlexPod & AFF C-Series: New Solutions for Infrastructure | NetApp Blog
summary: NetApp has introduced new solutions for its FlexPod converged infrastructure, comparing them to high-quality ingredients for a pizza. These include the AFF C-Series capacity all-flash storage systems for cost-effective, high-density storage, and the NetApp Advance program offering flexible ownership and tech refresh options. Additionally, enhancements in storage efficiency guarantees and cyber resilience features are highlighted as key benefits for FlexPod users.

title: Scality Hires Former HPE Exec Peter Brennan As CRO
summary: Scality has appointed former Hewlett Packard Enterprise executive Peter Brennan as its new CRO, entrusting him with worldwide sales, alliances, channels, and related marketing activities. Brennan emphasizes a "channel-first" philosophy, aiming to significantly boost Scality's partnerships and expand the reach of its Artesca technology, particularly in edge data storage. He also plans to strengthen relationships with other key players like Supermicro and Cisco, leveraging his extensive industry connections.

title: Experts call for AI regulation during Senate hearing
summary: During a recent Senate hearing, experts advocated for the immediate implementation of AI regulations by Congress to protect consumers from the potential harms of the technology, such as algorithmic bias and the misuse of deepfakes. Concerns were raised about AI's impact on safety, privacy, economic, and national security, with comparisons drawn to the European Union's regulatory efforts and the White House's Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights. The discussion emphasized the need for transparency, accountability, and safeguards in AI development and deployment,

title: Zscaler appoints Karl Soderlund to drive growth
summary: Zscaler has appointed Karl Soderlund as their Senior Vice President, Worldwide Partners, and Alliances. In this role, Soderlund will be responsible for enhancing and modernizing the Zscaler partner program and leading its channel team. He brings extensive experience from previous senior roles at Palo Alto Networks, Aruba Networks, and Cisco Systems, among others.

title: Federal Circuit Allows Apple’s Fintiv Challenge To Proceed
summary: The Federal Circuit Court of Appeals has revived Apple's challenge to the PTAB's Fintiv practice of discretionary denials, allowing it to proceed to the district court. This decision questions the USPTO's ability to issue rules through precedential decisions rather than traditional notice-and-comment rulemaking. The resolution of this case could significantly impact how the USPTO operates regarding Inter Partes Review practices.

title: Talos uncovers espionage campaigns targeting CIS countries, embassies and EU health care agency
summary: Cisco Talos has identified a new threat actor, YoroTrooper, conducting espionage campaigns since June 2022, primarily targeting government and energy organizations in CIS countries like Azerbaijan, Tajikistan, and Kyrgyzstan. YoroTrooper also compromised accounts from a critical EU health care agency and the World Intellectual Property Organization, as well as European embassies. The actor uses Python-based custom and open-source information stealers, along with commodity malware like AveMaria/Warzone RAT and LodaRAT, delivered through malicious shortcut files and decoy documents in phishing campaigns.

title: Companies say they want diversity. So why are Latinos left off corporate boards?
summary: Despite being the nation's largest ethnic minority and a significant economic force, Latinos are severely underrepresented on corporate boards of Fortune 100 and 1000 companies. Advocacy groups like the Latino Corporate Directors Assn. and Latino Donor Collaborative highlight this disparity, stating many major companies lack Latino directors even while benefiting from the Latino market. While some diversity initiatives have boosted board representation for other minority groups, Latinos continue to face barriers, often being overlooked or stereotyped, leading to calls for increased pressure on corporations and investors to address this gap.

title: The top 10 biggest technology companies in the US in 2023
summary: This article lists the top 10 biggest technology companies in the US in 2023 by market capitalization. It provides details on each company's headquarters, CEO, market cap as of January 2023, and a brief overview of their primary products and services. The list includes giants like Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon, highlighting their significant contributions and innovations in the technology sector.

title: Saving SecOps Ryan: Is Cyberwar Real or Something From a Movie?
summary: This article discusses the reality of cyberwarfare, moving beyond cinematic portrayals to highlight its devastating impact on critical infrastructure and national security. Author Oscar Montes uses the Russia-Ukraine conflict as a key example to illustrate how cyberattacks disrupt essential services, spread misinformation, and are increasingly fueled by hacktivist groups like Killnet. The piece emphasizes the urgent need for increased societal awareness, updated cybersecurity policies, and stronger international cooperation to combat this evolving global threat.

title: Cisco Powers Unrivaled Hybrid Work Experiences with Purpose-Built AI Innovations in Webex
summary: Cisco has introduced new AI-powered Webex capabilities aimed at enhancing hybrid work and customer experiences. These innovations include cinematic meetings and meeting zones for devices, alongside improvements in Webex Suite like Super Resolution and smart re-lighting. Additionally, new AI features for Webex Contact Center and Webex Connect focus on actionable insights, agent coaching, automated summaries, and rapid customization of customer journeys.

title: Why SASE will benefit from faster consolidation of networking and security
summary: Secure Access Service Edge (SASE) is seeing significant growth as 75% of enterprises pursue vendor consolidation, up from 29% three years ago. SASE, which incorporates Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA), helps improve enterprise security postures and streamlines consolidation plans. Leading cybersecurity providers are integrating SASE through product roadmaps and strategic acquisitions to meet the rising demand for consolidated and secure network solutions.

title: Cisco Announces its Intent to Acquire Cloud Security Software Company Lightspin
summary: Cisco has announced its intent to acquire Lightspin Technologies Ltd., a privately-held cloud security software company specializing in cloud security posture management (CSPM) using graph-based technology. This acquisition aims to enhance Cisco's cloud-native technologies portfolio by allowing customers to identify, prioritize, and remediate critical cloud security risks. The Lightspin team will join Cisco’s Emerging Technologies & Incubation business to accelerate the delivery of solutions for prioritizing and remediating cloud vulnerabilities.

title: New Cisco 800G Innovations Help to Supercharge the Internet for the Future
summary: Cisco has announced new 800G innovations designed to transform internet economics and sustainability for service and cloud providers. The new 28.8Tbps / 36 x 800G line card, powered by Cisco Silicon One P100 ASIC, offers up to 6x more space efficiency and significantly reduces power consumption compared to current 400G solutions. These advancements aim to support high-bandwidth demands from IoT, AI/ML workloads, and help connect underserved populations while improving operational efficiency and sustainability.

title: Cisco 800G Routing Silicon Gets Market Jump
summary: Cisco has introduced 800-gigabit (800G) performance to its 8000 Series routers, utilizing its new Silicon One P100 ASIC. This move gives Cisco a market lead over rivals, with the company noting its product's availability and ongoing trials. The updated routers offer significant capacity, space efficiency, and power savings, targeting hyperscalers, service providers, and enterprises for high-bandwidth applications.

title: Good Friends Say Goodbye as Prime Infrastructure Sunsets
summary: Cisco is announcing the End of Life (EoL) for Prime Infrastructure, a network management tool that has served customers for over a decade. The new generation platform, Cisco DNA Center, offers enhanced capabilities driven by Intent-Based Networking, automation, and AI/ML. Existing Prime Infrastructure customers are encouraged to migrate to Cisco DNA Center or the Cisco Meraki Dashboard using provided tools and services to leverage these advanced features.

title: Enterprise Connect 2023 Expo Hall: RingCentral, VMware, Five9, Cisco, More
summary: The Enterprise Connect 2023 conference showcased over 170 organizations at the Gaylord Palms Resort in Orlando, Florida. A major highlight was the significant advancement of artificial intelligence in UCaaS and CCaaS, with tech giants like Google Cloud, Microsoft, and Zoom unveiling new AI offerings. The article includes a slideshow featuring photos from the expo floor, highlighting the innovation and booth creativity of participating companies.

title: Cisco Is #1 on Fortune’s 100 Best Companies to Work For® In the U.S. List — Again!
summary: Cisco has been named the #1 best workplace in the U.S. for the third consecutive year by Fortune. The article highlights testimonials from various Cisco employees who praise the company's culture, work-life balance, inclusive environment, and commitment to social impact through programs like Time2Give. These firsthand accounts illustrate why employees feel supported, empowered, and proud to work at Cisco.

title: Municipal broadband and why communities should be interested
summary: This article discusses the benefits of municipal broadband for communities, highlighting its role in providing equitable access to connectivity, enhancing public services, and driving economic growth. It emphasizes job creation, increased competition, improved education and healthcare access, and the reduction of the digital divide. The City of Fort Worth is presented as a successful case study in implementing municipal broadband to serve lower-income households.

title: Future-proofing Observability with OpenTelemetry
summary: OpenTelemetry (OTel) is an open-source framework that standardizes the collection of observability data (metrics, traces, logs) in modern, complex IT systems. It provides vendor-agnostic tools for instrumentation, data processing, and export, addressing the challenges of diverse data conventions and proprietary solutions. OTel helps organizations achieve unified system observability, reduce vendor lock-in, and streamline data collection efforts, enabling them to focus on improving service quality.

title: Cisco’s new Atlanta offices aimed at addressing the workforce of the future
summary: Cisco has opened new offices in Atlanta designed for hybrid work and talent attraction, particularly targeting local talent near Georgia Tech. The office design incorporates lessons from their New York Penn1 location, emphasizing collaborative spaces and leveraging Power-over-Ethernet for efficiency. Cisco aims to use this location as a showcase for customers on how to make offices a magnet, rather than a mandate, for employees.

title: California scraps caste bias case against Cisco engineers; company still being sued
summary: The California Civil Rights Department has dismissed its caste discrimination case against two Cisco engineers, Sundar Iyer and Ramana Kompella, but is continuing its lawsuit against Cisco as a whole. The original lawsuit alleged that a Dalit employee faced discrimination in pay and opportunities and retaliation for opposing traditional caste practices within the company. This development has sparked mixed reactions among South Asian community groups regarding the necessity and implications of caste-based anti-discrimination policies.

title: Pulse & Cisco Collaboration Enables New ICM Manufacturing Facility in India
summary: Pulse Electronics has successfully launched a new manufacturing facility in Tiruchirappalli, India, dedicated to producing Integrated Connector Modules (ICMs) for Cisco Systems, Inc. This facility marks Pulse's first in India capable of manufacturing ICMs and is the result of a multi-year collaborative effort between Pulse and Cisco's supply chain and engineering teams. ICMs are vital components used in switching, routing, and computing products.

title: Cisco to offer Webex air-gapped cloud system for security, defense work
summary: Cisco plans to introduce an air-gapped, cloud-based Webex collaboration system in 2024, specifically designed for companies involved in US national security and defense work. This system will offer enhanced network and data handling security by isolating it from public networks, addressing the stringent requirements of the US government for sensitive data collaboration. Cisco has also committed to aligning its on-premises solutions with FedRAMP requirements, ensuring high security standards for government use.

title: Best Workplaces in Canada 2023: 1000+ employees
summary: This article lists the Best Workplaces in Canada for 2023 in the 1000+ employees category. It highlights various companies and the unique employee benefits and initiatives that earned them this recognition, focusing on aspects like well-being, flexibility, career development, and social impact. The featured companies span diverse sectors, including technology, finance, professional services, and manufacturing, showcasing a wide range of employee-centric practices.

title: State-sponsored campaigns target global network infrastructure
summary: Cisco Talos has observed a significant increase in sophisticated, state-sponsored attacks targeting network infrastructure globally, including routers and firewalls. These attacks aim for espionage or to pre-position for destructive operations, often exploiting out-of-date software and poor security practices. The article provides recommendations such as using strong credentials, multi-factor authentication, keeping hardware and software updated, and actively monitoring network configurations to enhance resilience against these threats.

title: Tap collaboration devices for employee experience data
summary: This article explores how collaboration devices can significantly enhance employee experience. It details four key strategies: leveraging device data to understand employee behavior and engagement, simplifying the process of starting in-room meetings with one-click-to-join systems, ensuring equity between in-office and remote participants through advanced camera setups and touchscreens, and encouraging the use of wellness features embedded in communication software. The piece emphasizes the importance of analyzing collected data and taking action to improve productivity and engagement based on these insights.

title: 2023 SecureIQLab: Command and Control Comparative Report
summary: This report, commissioned by Palo Alto Networks, details SecureIQLab's comparative analysis of cloud-delivered firewalls against Cobalt Strike command-and-control capabilities. The study tested Palo Alto Networks Prisma Access Enterprise, Cisco Umbrella SIG Essentials, and Zscaler ZIA Transformation bundle to assess their efficacy in blocking sophisticated red team tool attacks. The initiative was prompted by a significant increase in the use of such tools by threat actors, as reported by Unit 42.

title: Vulnerability Spotlight: Vulnerabilities in IBM AIX could lead to command injection with elevated privileges
summary: Cisco Talos has discovered two vulnerabilities in IBM AIX Unix platforms (TALOS-2023-1690 and TALOS-2023-1691) that could enable command injection with elevated privileges. One significant vulnerability is in AIX’s errlog() syscall functionality, allowing arbitrary log generation and malicious command execution as root, or out-of-bounds memory access. Users are strongly advised to update IBM AIX version 7.2 as soon as possible to mitigate these risks.

title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights Runway Growth Finance, Cisco Systems, Kimberly Clark, NiSource and Omnicom
summary: Amid an economic downturn and tightening credit conditions, this Zacks Analyst Blog highlights five ultra-safe stocks with low beta and attractive dividend yields. The article recommends Runway Growth Finance Corp. (RWAY), Cisco Systems (CSCO), Kimberly Clark (KMB), NiSource (NI), and Omnicom Group (OMC) for investors seeking stability and income. These companies are identified as having strong business models and favorable Zacks Ranks.

title: MSP M&A: ePlus Acquires Network Solutions Group From CCI Systems
summary: ePlus has acquired Network Solutions Group (NSG), a Cisco master services provider and a business unit of CCI Systems. While financial terms were not disclosed, this acquisition aims to expand ePlus' national presence in the service provider market and leverage federal broadband funding initiatives. This deal is part of ePlus' strategy of active acquisitions in the MSP and IT solutions provider market.

title: Cisco Business Model
summary: This article details Cisco's business model, which has evolved from primarily selling networking hardware to offering high-technology services and software. It covers the company's financial performance, revenue streams from hardware sales, licensing, and services, and strategic initiatives like acquisitions. The article also provides a SWOT analysis, outlines key competitors, and discusses Cisco's customer segments and value propositions.

title: Why Cisco partners are more important than ever
summary: Cisco emphasizes the increasing importance of its partners in helping businesses navigate complex, dynamic, and ever-changing technology landscapes. The company is launching a new initiative to help organizations understand how different types of Cisco partners, from solution integrators to managed service providers, can deliver value and accelerate digital transformation. This initiative will demystify partner selection, highlight specialized expertise, and explain how partners contribute to business outcomes and sustainability goals.

title: Cisco to Manufacture in India as it Targets over $1 Billion in Combined Exports and Domestic Production
summary: Cisco announced a significant investment in manufacturing capabilities in India, aiming to produce over $1 billion in combined domestic production and exports in the coming years. This strategic move strengthens its global supply chain and supports India's vision of becoming a manufacturing hub. The new facility will cater to growing demand and build advanced technology for flexible, cost-effective services.

title: FlexPod powers Cisco Live
summary: FlexPod, a converged infrastructure solution combining Cisco and NetApp components, is powering the mission-critical Network Operations Center (NOC) for Cisco Live Las Vegas from June 4-9, 2023. This marks FlexPod's role as the NOC provider for the tenth consecutive in-person Cisco Live event, highlighting Cisco's trust in its reliability. Attendees can learn more about FlexPod's capabilities, including its use with Aston Martin's racing team, by visiting the Cisco NOC, the NetApp booth, or attending specific sessions at the conference.

title: "No matter how you cut it, you've got to own Cisco" (2000) - The Big Picture
summary: The article reflects on a 2000 Fortune magazine cover story that enthusiastically recommended Cisco stock, predicting it would be the first trillion-dollar company. Author Barry Ritholtz uses this as a case study to highlight the inherent unpredictability of the future in investing. He demonstrates how Cisco's stock plummeted shortly after the article's publication and has significantly underperformed benchmarks like the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 over the subsequent 23 years.

title: Juniper Networks launches R&D initiative to help to shape next-generation networks
summary: Juniper Networks has launched Juniper Beyond Labs, an R&D initiative to drive innovation in networking and IT through research and experimental technology projects. This initiative will focus on various networking domains including AI, 5G/6G, and quantum computing, with a strong emphasis on sustainability. It aims to collaborate with academic and industry partners, offering customers and partners an opportunity to influence research directions and access new innovations.

title: Saudi Telecom Creates Cybersecurity Center of Excellence
summary: Saudi Telecom has partnered with Cisco to transform its Cyber Defense Center into a Cybersecurity Center of Excellence, aligning with "Saudi Vision 2030." This collaboration significantly enhanced Saudi Telecom's cybersecurity capabilities, including reducing mean time to detect and respond to cyberthreats. The initiative involved advanced monitoring, automation, and a vendor-agnostic security approach, leading to critical accreditations and improved cybersecurity maturity.

title: 3 steps to prevent a case of compromised credentials
summary: This article by Katherine Yang discusses three crucial steps to prevent and respond to compromised credentials, emphasizing the inadequacy of passwords alone. It highlights the importance of immediate notification to IT, upgrading to stronger authentication methods like biometrics, and especially enabling multi-factor authentication (MFA) to protect against modern cyber threats. The piece also delves into advanced MFA techniques like Verified Duo Push and Risk-Based Authentication for enhanced security postures.

title: Resurgent Amdocs expands into banking market
summary: Amdocs, an Israeli business software giant, is expanding into the banking and finance market, leveraging its experience from the telecom sector. The company's share price is nearing its all-time high, with a market cap of $11.4 billion. Amdocs sees the banking industry as a long-term growth engine, aiming to help banks modernize their systems during a period of disruption, similar to its role in the telecom industry.

title: Wesco Expands Offerings, Support and Opens New Innovation Center
summary: Wesco International has expanded its services by forming an Innovation Partner Ecosystem, enhancing its IoT Advisory Services, and opening a new solutions innovation center in Glenview, Illinois. These initiatives aim to help customers, suppliers, and partners navigate market complexities, technology convergence, and sustainability demands. The expanded portfolio leverages the synergy from Wesco's combination with Anixter to provide tech-enabled supply chain solutions and drive innovation across its ecosystem.

title: Physical Security Global Market Report 2023: Players Include ADT, Cisco Systems, Honeywell International, Johnson Controls and Anixter
summary: The global physical security market is projected to grow from $109.96 billion in 2022 to $152.73 billion in 2027, driven by technological advancements and increasing incidents of terrorist attacks. Key players in this market include ADT Inc., Cisco Systems Inc., and Honeywell International Inc. North America was the largest regional market in 2022.

title: NVIDIA Grace Hopper Superchips Designed for Accelerated Generative AI Enter Full Production
summary: NVIDIA announced that its GH200 Grace Hopper Superchip is now in full production, intended to power systems globally for complex AI and HPC workloads. This superchip combines the Arm-based NVIDIA Grace CPU and Hopper GPU architectures using NVLink-C2C interconnect technology, offering significantly higher bandwidth for demanding generative AI applications. Global hyperscalers, supercomputing centers, and numerous system manufacturers are deploying GH200-powered systems, which are expected to be available later this year.

title: Black Hat Asia 2023 NOC: XDR (eXtended Detection and Response) in Action
summary: This article details the successful implementation of Cisco's eXtended Detection and Response (XDR) security solutions at Black Hat Asia 2023. The Network Operations Center (NOC) showcased how integrated security technologies, such as Cisco XDR, Umbrella, Secure Malware Analytics, and Secure Cloud Analytics, provided robust threat detection, visibility, and automated responses to various security incidents. The collaboration between Cisco and other NOC partners facilitated quick identification and resolution of issues ranging from phishing attempts to insider threats and policy violations.

title: What’s lurking in your email?
summary: Email remains the primary threat vector for cyberattacks, complicated by hybrid work and advanced AI-enabled threats. Cisco Secure Email offers a multi-layered defense, integrating with other security solutions like Cisco Secure Endpoint and Cisco Umbrella, to protect organizations from phishing, ransomware, and other email-based attacks. The solution also includes Cisco Secure Email Threat Defense for Microsoft 365 and will be a key component of the forthcoming Cisco Security Cloud, aiming to simplify security operations and provide comprehensive protection.

title: Cisco buys Armorblox to beef up cybersecurity with generative AI
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. announced its acquisition of cybersecurity startup Armorblox Inc. to strengthen its security offerings with generative AI. Armorblox specializes in using natural language processing and large language models to protect against email and cloud-based threats like business email compromise and phishing attacks. This acquisition will integrate Armorblox's AI capabilities into Cisco's security portfolio to enhance threat prediction, detection, and policy enforcement.

title: Cisco needs to simplify. Here’s how.
summary: This article analyzes Cisco Systems Inc.'s business, highlighting its strong financial performance but also customer complaints about product complexity. It outlines how Cisco can simplify its offerings in security, networking, and observability to integrate its diverse portfolio and improve customer experiences, drawing on market data and insights from analyst Zeus Kerravala. The report also previews what to expect from Cisco Live regarding AI, security strategy, collaboration, and environmental, social, and governance initiatives.

title: Celebrating Excellence: Our 2023 CX Customer Hero Award Winners
summary: Cisco recognized eight customers with the 2023 CX Customer Hero Award for their exceptional innovation and business transformation in partnership with Cisco CX. The awards spanned categories such as Digital Transformation, Cybersecurity Excellence, and Innovation, celebrating companies like NYC Health + Hospitals, Nationwide, Princess Cruises, and Honeywell International. These organizations demonstrated success in areas including secure networks, enhanced customer experiences, IT automation, and data protection, showcasing the impact of their collaboration with Cisco.

title: PTC Names Janesh Moorjani to Board of Directors
summary: PTC (NASDAQ: PTC) has appointed Janesh Moorjani to its Board of Directors, effective June 7, 2023. Moorjani, currently the CFO and COO of Elastic NV, brings over two decades of experience in finance and operations within the technology sector. His expertise in financial and operational management will be an asset as PTC continues its evolution and embrace of SaaS, expanding the board to ten members.

title: Cisco Showcases Vision for Cisco Networking Cloud
summary: Cisco has unveiled its vision for Cisco Networking Cloud, an integrated management platform designed to simplify IT by providing a unified experience for managing all Cisco networking products from a single location. This initiative addresses fragmentation and complexity, offering innovations such as single sign-on, API key exchange, and expanded network assurance with Cisco ThousandEyes. The goal is to enhance operational simplicity, efficiency, and reliability across on-prem and cloud operating models.

title: Cisco Shows Breakthrough Innovation Towards AI-First Security Cloud
summary: Cisco is advancing its AI-driven Security Cloud with new innovations designed to simplify cybersecurity operations and enhance security efficacy. Key announcements include the launch of Cisco Secure Access, a new Security Service Edge (SSE) solution for hybrid work, and a preview of generative AI capabilities for threat response and policy management. Additionally, Cisco is bolstering network security with the new Secure Firewall 4200 Series and Multicloud Defense, alongside new cloud-native application security features in Panoptica.

title: Cisco and AT&T Join Forces to Help Businesses Expand Connectivity for a Growing Mobile-First Workforce
summary: Cisco and AT&T have announced new solutions to enhance connectivity and advance calling for hybrid workforces, integrating Webex Calling with AT&T's mobile network for a seamless, mobile-first collaboration experience. The partnership provides businesses with a single mobile identity, cost reduction, and crystal-clear voice communication. Additionally, they are collaborating to offer secure SD-WAN connectivity with add-on services like 5G and broadband, streamlining network deployment and management for businesses of all sizes.

title: Cisco and AT&T Join Forces to Help Businesses Expand Connectivity for a Growing Mobile-First Workforce
summary: Cisco and AT&T have announced new solutions to enhance connectivity and advance collaboration for hybrid workforces, integrating Webex Calling with AT&T's mobile network for a unified experience via a single business mobile number. They are also collaborating to provide SD-WAN connectivity with add-on services like 5G and broadband, catering to businesses of all sizes. Additionally, AT&T is launching a self-service digital purchasing experience for SMBs, bundling fiber and mobility with Cisco Meraki's cloud-managed solution.

title: IT and Networking | Large Enterprise Solutions
summary: Lumen offers large enterprise IT and networking solutions designed to prepare businesses for the AI revolution, addressing the concerns of 86% of CIOs about network readiness. The platform provides secure, scalable, and high-performance connectivity, cybersecurity, and collaboration tools, leveraging a globally peered network and a programmable network fabric for AI workloads. Lumen also provides various infrastructure, connectivity, security, and communication services, featuring customer success stories and extensive resources for intelligent enterprise solutions.

title: Cisco's Jeetu Patel on its new Security Cloud: a generative AI-driven approach to cybersecurity
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. has launched a new cloud-based, generative AI-powered cybersecurity platform called Cisco Security Cloud, aiming to integrate security and networking and combat sophisticated attackers. According to Jeetu Patel, EVP and GM of Security and Collaboration Business Units, the platform leverages Cisco's experience with predictive AI, custom and generic data, and innovative solutions like Cisco Multicloud Defense and Cisco Secure Access, to augment human capabilities and simplify security connectivity.

title: Is Cisco Stock a Buy, Sell, or Fairly Valued After Earnings?
summary: Cisco Systems (CSCO) reported stronger-than-expected fiscal third-quarter results, driven by networking equipment sales, though tempered by slow order growth. Morningstar maintains a 4-star "undervalued" rating with a fair value estimate of $56 per share, attributing Cisco's strong position to its wide economic moat from customer switching costs and comprehensive networking and cybersecurity offerings. Despite short-term demand worries and competitive threats, the company's long-term fundamentals, pricing power, and shift to subscription-based models suggest strong upside potential for investors.

title: Women of Influence 2023: Liz Centoni of Cisco Systems Inc.
summary: Liz Centoni, Executive Vice President, Chief Strategy Officer, and General Manager of Applications at Cisco Systems Inc., has been honored as a 2023 Woman of Influence. This recognition highlights her significant role and leadership within Cisco Systems. The article specifically references her title and the date of her recognition.

title: Cisco Silicon One Breaks the 51.2 Tbps Barrier
summary: Cisco has announced its fourth-generation Silicon One devices, the G200 and G202, three and a half years after the initial launch, demonstrating a rapid pace of innovation. The G200 is a 5 nm, 51.2 Tbps device focusing on enhanced Ethernet-based AI/ML and web-scale spine deployments, while the G202 offers similar benefits at 25.6 Tbps. These new devices leverage Cisco's high-speed ADC-based SerDes technology and a converged architecture to reduce costs, power consumption, and latency in web-scale and AI/ML networks.

title: Micron, Applied Materials make big investments in India
summary: Chipmakers Micron Technology and Applied Materials are making significant investments in India, bolstering the nation's ambition to become a silicon superpower. Micron plans to build an assembly and test facility valued at $825 million, while Applied Materials will establish a $400 million engineering center. These announcements coincide with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's state visit to Washington, highlighting growing US-India collaboration in technology and defense, including jet engine manufacturing and spaceflight partnerships.

title: Top 10 cybersecurity companies in the US
summary: This article lists the top 10 cybersecurity companies in the US, providing a brief overview of the services offered by each. It highlights that as technology advances, the need for robust cybersecurity solutions grows due to increasingly sophisticated cyber threats. The companies featured specialize in protecting sensitive information for individuals, businesses, and governments through various solutions like endpoint protection, cloud security, and threat intelligence.

title: Cisco bats for universal AI regulation
summary: Cisco is advocating for universal AI regulation, emphasizing the need for a global public-private dialogue due to AI's far-reaching impact. The company believes AI should be used responsibly with transparency and collaboration between the public and private sectors to establish regulatory frameworks, including content attribution and copyright. This stance aligns with other tech giants like Microsoft and Google who also support AI regulation, and comes as the EU is actively pursuing legislation in this area.

title: High-severity bug in Cisco data center switches lets attackers break into encrypted traffic
summary: Cisco has issued a warning regarding a high-severity (7.4 CVSS) vulnerability in some of its data center switch models, which could allow attackers to decrypt and modify encrypted traffic. Since no software updates or workarounds are currently available, Cisco has advised customers using the affected ACI Multi-Site CloudSec encryption feature to disable it and contact support for alternative options. The vulnerability highlights a significant security risk for data centers handling sensitive information.

title: 4 Indian American businesswomen on Forbes’ 2023 list
summary: Four Indian American businesswomen — Jayshree Ullal, Neerja Sethi, Neha Narkhede, and Indra Nooyi — have been featured on Forbes' 2023 list of America's 100 most successful women. Jayshree Ullal, CEO of Arista Networks, leads the group with a net worth of $2.2 billion. The article highlights their achievements, wealth, and contributions across various industries.

title: Networking and security for the megastars of music
summary: Cisco and Rock Lititz collaborate to co-innovate cutting-edge networking and security solutions for live music, transforming how major artists rehearse and perform. Located in rural Pennsylvania, Rock Lititz serves as a "living lab" where advanced technologies are designed, tested, and perfected to ensure seamless, secure, and spectacular concert experiences. This partnership focuses on robust, reliable, and innovative solutions to support everything from pyrotechnics and stage lighting to fan connectivity and point-of-sale systems, all while prioritizing cybersecurity.

title: BigPanda automates IT incident analysis and management with generative AI
summary: BigPanda Inc. has launched a new Generative AI for Automated Incident Analysis capability for its AI operations platform. This new tool helps IT operations teams quickly analyze incidents, estimate their impact, and suggest root causes, utilizing large language models to provide clear, natural language summaries. The company claims this leads to faster incident resolution, reduced escalations, and 95% accuracy in root cause determination.

title: Meet Indian-origin women on the 'America's Richest Self-Made
summary: Four Indian-origin women, Jayshree Ullal, Neerja Sethi, Neha Narkhede, and Indra Nooyi, have been named on Forbes' list of America’s 100 richest self-made women, with a combined net worth of $4.06 billion. Each woman has achieved significant success in technology, IT consulting, or consumer goods, showcasing their entrepreneurial prowess and impact in their respective industries. The list calculates net worth using stock prices from May 12, 2023, and evaluations from external experts for private companies.

title: Securing Identity as the New Perimeter: Cisco Announces Intent to Acquire Oort
summary: Cisco announced its intent to acquire Oort Inc., a company specializing in Identity Threat Detection and Response (ITDR) technology. This acquisition aims to strengthen Cisco's AI-driven Security Cloud by integrating Oort's API-driven, cloud-native platform to enhance user context telemetry and improve identity security across its product portfolio, including Duo IAM and XDR. The move addresses the increasing number of security breaches involving compromised identity credentials.

title: Jayshree V Ullal rises on Forbes list of richest self-made women in America
summary: Jayshree V Ullal, CEO and President of Arista Networks, has significantly climbed Forbes' list of richest self-made women in America with a net worth of $1.9 billion in 2022. Her success stems from transforming Arista Networks, taking it to IPO in 2014, and a distinguished career that included generating billions for Cisco, and holding key positions at AMD, IBM, and Hitachi. Ullal's journey highlights her leadership, expertise, and entrepreneurial spirit in the technology industry.

title: The Plan for Possible: Connecting a regenerative future
summary: Cisco, a global tech leader, outlines "The Plan for Possible," its environmental sustainability strategy aimed at achieving a regenerative future. This plan focuses on transitioning to clean energy, evolving towards a circular economy for its products, and investing in resilient ecosystems. The goal is to move beyond mere sustainability to actively heal and thrive social and environmental systems, striving for net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2040 and aligning with scientific imperatives to limit global temperature rise.

title: Cisco Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ:CSCO) is favoured by institutional owners who hold 75% of the company
summary: Institutional investors hold a significant 75% stake in Cisco Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ:CSCO), granting them considerable influence over the company's share price and strategic decisions. While the Vanguard Group, BlackRock, and State Street Global Advisors are among the largest shareholders, no single entity dominates, and insiders hold less than 1% of the stock. The general public owns 24%, highlighting the importance of institutional analysis for potential investors.

title: Cultivating Collaborative Innovation: Unlocking the Power with Partners
summary: Cisco emphasizes collaborative innovation with partners to drive business value and agility in a rapidly evolving market. By co-developing technology solutions and leveraging partners' insights, Cisco plans to accelerate innovation and create more feature-rich offerings for customers. This approach transforms Cisco into a platform provider, empowering a new generation of innovators and unlocking the full potential of technology through strategic partnerships.

title: Palo Alto Networks Secures Black Hat from Itself
summary: Palo Alto Networks has been a trusted partner for Black Hat conferences for six years, securing the event's network infrastructure. This year at Black Hat USA, Palo Alto Networks is providing firewall services, collaborating on threat hunting, and exclusively offering security orchestration, automation, and response (SOAR) using its Cortex XDR, NGFWs, Panorama, and Cortex XSOAR products. Their efforts aim to protect Black Hat from both external threats and internal attempts by attendees to test attack techniques, ensuring a stable and secure learning environment.

title: Incident Response trends Q2 2023: Data theft extortion rises, while healthcare is still most-targeted vertical
summary: Cisco Talos Incident Response (Talos IR) observed a 25% increase in data theft extortion incidents in Q2 2023, making it the most-observed threat. These incidents involve threat actors stealing data and threatening to leak it without necessarily deploying ransomware. Healthcare continues to be the most targeted industry, followed by financial services, while compromised credentials remained the primary initial access method.

title: Networking startup Nile raises $175 million to challenge Cisco
summary: Nile, a networking startup co-founded by former Cisco executives John Chambers and Pankaj Patel, has raised $175 million in a new funding round to compete with Cisco in the networking market. The company, which provides network-as-a-service (NaaS) solutions, aims to challenge incumbents by offering lower costs and advanced security features. Nile plans to expand its sales and marketing team, targeting mid-sized businesses.

title: T‑Mobile Launches First‑Ever 5G Network Slicing Beta for Developers
summary: T-Mobile has launched its first-ever 5G network slicing beta program for developers, initially focusing on enhancing video calling applications. This initiative leverages T-Mobile's nationwide 5G Standalone (5G SA) network to offer customized network slices that provide optimized performance for applications requiring consistent speeds, lower latency, and increased reliability. Major companies like Dialpad, Google, Webex by Cisco, and Zoom are already participating in the beta to develop next-generation communication tools.

title: Multiple Hurdles In The Race To 6G
summary: The development and rollout of 6G technology by 2030 face numerous significant challenges, despite its potential to revolutionize industries with speeds up to one terabit per second and sub-0.1 ms latency. Key hurdles include establishing universal standards, building complex infrastructure, managing massive data, achieving terahertz waves, and ensuring robust cybersecurity and scalability. Collaborative efforts across the entire supply chain, from chipmakers to network providers, are essential to overcome these obstacles and avoid fragmentation.

title: Verizon to ‘sunset’ Blue Jeans vidconf platform
summary: Verizon is discontinuing its BlueJeans video collaboration platform, which it acquired in April 2020. The company cited "changing market conditions in a post-pandemic landscape" as the reason, shifting its focus to other priorities like 5G and mobile edge computing. The move highlights the intense competition in the video conferencing market and the impact of evolving work trends.

title: Demonstrating Transparency through Software Bill of Materials (SBOM)
summary: Cisco has announced the general availability of distributing SPDX-formatted Software Bill of Materials (SBOMs) for its supported on-premise software released after September 2022. This initiative aims to enhance transparency and resilience across the software supply chain, positioning Cisco ahead of government mandates and other large technology vendors. The article highlights Cisco's foundational commitment, standardized approach, and centralized services that have enabled this strategic move towards greater software transparency.

title: Gogo opens up about its 5G troubles
summary: Gogo is facing significant delays and challenges in launching its 5G network for business aircraft, now hoping for a mid-2024 launch. Issues stem from a glitchy 5G chip from supplier GCT and the FCC's "rip and replace" program involving Chinese equipment, which has impacted their finances. Despite setbacks and competition from SmartSky, Gogo remains optimistic about future revenue growth and shareholder returns by combining 5G with satellite services.

title: Cisco acquires telecommunications software startup Working Group Two for $150M
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is set to acquire Norwegian telecommunications software startup Working Group Two AS for $150 million. The acquisition, expected to close by October 29, will integrate Working Group Two's wgtwo platform, which helps carriers manage their mobile core networks, into Cisco's Mobility Services. This move aims to simplify connectivity, reduce costs, and accelerate the rollout of new features for carriers.

title: Intuit Names Marianna Tessel General Manager of Small Business and Self-Employed Group
summary: Intuit has appointed Marianna Tessel as the General Manager of its Small Business and Self-Employed Group, effective September 5, 2023. Tessel, previously Intuit's Chief Technology Officer, will lead the company's largest and fastest-growing business unit. Alex Balazs will succeed Tessel as the new Chief Technology Officer, having been a key architect of Intuit's technology platform.

title: ERISA Case Against Cisco Over BlackRock TDFs Dismissed
summary: A federal court in California dismissed a breach of fiduciary duty lawsuit against Cisco Systems under ERISA, concerning its selection of BlackRock's LifePath target-date funds (TDFs). The judge ruled that underperformance alone over a short period does not constitute imprudence, especially without evidence of a flawed selection process. This dismissal is one of several similar cases brought by Miller Shah against various companies, all of which have been rejected on similar grounds.

title: Cisco (CSCO) reports Q4 results: Here’s all you need to know
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. reported strong fourth-quarter 2023 results, with revenues up 16% year-over-year to $15.2 billion and earnings per share increasing to $1.14. The company's Secure, Agile Networks division significantly contributed to its top-line growth, and Cisco provided positive guidance for the first quarter of 2024. CEO Chuck Robbins highlighted solid customer demand, market share gains, and innovation in AI, security, and cloud as key drivers of confidence for future opportunities.

title: Cisco Reports Fourth Quarter And Fiscal Year 2023 Earnings
summary: Cisco reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2023, with Q4 revenue up 16% year over year to $15.2 billion and fiscal year revenue reaching $57.0 billion, an 11% increase. The company highlighted significant progress in its business model transformation, with double-digit growth in software revenue and annualized recurring revenue (ARR), and provided optimistic guidance for Q1 and full fiscal year 2024.

title: Why Cisco’s stock is rising in the face of a downbeat outlook
summary: Despite a lower-than-expected full-year revenue forecast from Cisco Systems Inc. for the upcoming fiscal year, the company's stock was observed rising. Analysts had projected higher revenue, but some believe Cisco's outlook might be conservatively set. The networking giant expects revenue between $57 billion and $58.2 billion, representing flat to 2% growth.

title: Cisco had a record-breaking year, but nobody is saying the real reason
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. achieved record revenue growth and profit in its recently completed fiscal year, which the article attributes primarily to significant price increases implemented in early 2022. While these price hikes boosted sales and profit, the forecast suggests these gains may not continue. The article highlights that the company's revenue and gross income had been stagnant for the past decade until these price adjustments took effect.

title: Cisco and Accenture Deliver SAP Operational Intelligence
summary: Cisco and Accenture have partnered to introduce SAP Operational Intelligence, a solution designed to improve how businesses measure SAP performance beyond traditional uptime. This offering combines Cisco's Full Stack Observability suite (including AppDynamics and ThousandEyes) with Accenture's SAP expertise to provide real-time diagnostics, performance insights, and correlation of SAP performance with business outcomes. The goal is to enhance collaboration between IT and business teams, reduce Mean Time to Repair (MTTR), and ensure SAP effectively supports business objectives.

title: Cisco Networking Academy fostering founders, one learner at a time
summary: Cisco Networking Academy is celebrating World Entrepreneurs’ Day by highlighting how the program fosters entrepreneurial skills alongside technical ones. The article discusses how Cisco itself was founded on an entrepreneurial spirit and how its Networking Academy helps learners like Antonius and Niels develop problem-solving and self-belief to become founders. It also introduces new courses on stakeholder engagement and entrepreneurship fundamentals to further support future innovators.

title: Cisco and Nutanix Forge Global Strategic Partnership to Simplify Hybrid Multicloud and Fuel Business Transformation
summary: Cisco and Nutanix announced a global strategic partnership aimed at accelerating hybrid multicloud adoption for customers. Their combined solution integrates Cisco's SaaS-managed compute and networking infrastructure with the Nutanix Cloud Platform, offering IT modernization and business transformation. This collaboration seeks to simplify operations, maximize resiliency, and streamline IT for global enterprises, with solution availability expected within 90 days.

title: A Roadmap for Selecting the Right CCaaS Vendor
summary: This article provides a roadmap for selecting a Contact Center as a Service (CCaaS) vendor, driven by the increasing adoption of CCaaS platforms for their flexibility and scalability. It outlines key evaluation criteria used by Metrigy in its CCaaS MetriRank report, including market share, financial strength, market momentum, customer sentiment, product mix, and customer business success scores. The article then highlights the leading CCaaS providers such as NICE, Five9, Cisco, Genesys, and 8x8, discussing their strengths and market positioning.

title: Vickie Wong
summary: Vickie Wong serves as Senior Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer at Cisco, where she leads the Controllership organization. Her responsibilities include global accounting, SEC and international statutory reporting, ESG Controllership, and financial operations. With 15 years at Cisco and previous experience as an audit Partner at KPMG, she is recognized for her expertise in reporting, financial accounting, and acquisition integrations.

title: Which AI Stocks Could Drive Digital Transformation in Latin America?
summary: Latin American economies are increasingly prioritizing digital transformation and the responsible use of AI, with countries like Brazil, Colombia, and Uruguay publishing national AI strategies. Companies such as Intel and Cisco are actively supporting digital infrastructure development in the region. Investors can gain exposure to this trend through ETFs like the Pacer Data and Digital Revolution ETF, the Digital Infrastructure and Connectivity UCITS ETF, and the Amundi MSCI Digital Economy and Metaverse ESG Screened UCITS ETF, all of which include major tech players.

title: Hyperscalers have been choosing Arista over Cisco for data center switches
summary: For the first time in years, enterprise spending, not cloud service provider spending, drove the growth of the data center switch market in Q2 2023, according to Dell'Oro Group. Cisco and Arista Networks remain the leading vendors, with Arista excelling in cloud service providers and Cisco in enterprise.
