The report also highlights that while AI investments are shifting budgets from traditional front-end networks, the Ethernet switch market continues to grow, driven by easing supply chain constraints and increased demand for higher-speed switches from hyperscalers.

title: Supply Chain Easing Creates Ethernet Switching Boom
summary: The Ethernet switching market is experiencing a significant boom, driven largely by the easing of supply chain issues that allows vendors to fulfill back orders. Major players like Cisco, Juniper Networks, and Hewlett Packard Enterprise are seeing substantial revenue growth, with Cisco's revenues rising by 55.3 percent and HPE by 78.8 percent in Q2 2023. While datacenter Ethernet port counts were down slightly, sales of Ethernet gear for the edge and campus surged by 52.5 percent, contributing to an overall 38.4 percent increase in Ethernet switching sales.

title: How Cisco Talos IR helped a healthcare company quickly resolve a Qakbot attack
summary: Cisco Talos Incident Response partnered with healthcare technology company Veradigm to quickly resolve a Qakbot attack, showcasing the benefits of proactive measures and established relationships in reducing incident resolution times. Veradigm's robust defense-in-depth strategy, including Cisco Secure Endpoint and Umbrella, along with their retainer with Talos IR, enabled them to prevent the successful deployment of Qakbot and limit potential harm. This case highlights how preparedness, expert collaboration, and an understanding of organizational environments are crucial for mitigating cyber threats, especially in highly regulated sectors like healthcare.

title: Another San Francisco Bay Area Tech Giant Will Lay Off Hundreds
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is set to lay off 350 workers across its Milpitas and San Jose offices, effective October 16, adding to previous cuts this year. The company stated these layoffs are part of a strategic transformation rather than cost-cutting, emphasizing investment prioritization and support for affected employees. These job reductions occur amidst a general slowdown in tech layoffs in the Bay Area after a peak earlier in the year.

title: Netgear Orbi 970 Series Wi-Fi 7 Mesh Router Flexes 27Gbps Of Wireless Bandwidth
summary: Netgear has introduced its new flagship Wi-Fi 7 mesh router system, the Orbi 970 series, which boasts a combined wireless bandwidth of up to 27Gbps and covers up to 10,000 square feet. This quad-band system utilizes multi-link operation (MLO) to enhance backhaul speeds between the router and satellites, offering impressive wired connectivity options as well. While representing a future-proofing investment, the Orbi 970 series is priced at a premium, with a 3-pack costing $2,299.99.

title: Cisco named a Visionary in the 2023 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Communications Platform as a Service (CPaaS)
summary: Cisco has been recognized as a Visionary in the inaugural 2023 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Communications Platform as a Service (CPaaS), highlighting its Webex Connect solution. This acknowledgment follows Cisco's move to the Leader quadrant in the IDC MarketScape for CPaaS 2023, underscoring its commitment to simplifying and automating customer journeys. The company's CPaaS platform aims to help businesses deliver seamless, personalized omnichannel experiences, which Gartner predicts will be leveraged by 90% of global enterprises by 2026.

title: Cisco to buy Splunk in $28 billion deal, boosting defenses as use of AI widens
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is set to acquire cybersecurity firm Splunk in a $28 billion deal, marking its largest acquisition to date. This move is aimed at bolstering Cisco's software and AI-powered data analysis capabilities, enhancing its defenses against security threats particularly as the use of AI expands. The acquisition, pending shareholder approval, is expected to close by the end of 2024's third quarter.

title: Cisco Buys Cybersecurity Provider Splunk for $28 Billion
summary: Cisco Systems is acquiring cybersecurity software company Splunk for $28 billion ($157 per share in cash), a move seen by analysts as an "aggressive strategic play" in the cybersecurity market. This acquisition is expected to accelerate Cisco's revenue growth and gross margins, becoming cash flow positive in the first fiscal year. The deal, which provided a significant premium to Splunk shareholders, highlights Cisco's intent to compete strongly in the AI-driven software space against major rivals.

title: AI and security headline Cisco’s acquisition of Splunk, but other synergies exist
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is set to acquire Splunk Inc. for $28 billion, a move anticipated to accelerate Cisco's growth in recurring revenue and strengthen its position in AI-enabled security and observability. The acquisition aims to create a software colossus well-positioned for the complex world of generative AI, offering synergies in observability, analytics, hybrid cloud, and sustainability, despite the integration challenges posed by its significant size.

title: Cisco to Buy Splunk for $157/Share in Cash; Wedbush Says Price Tag 'Richer' on EV/FCF Implled Multiple 2024, 2025
summary: Cisco is set to acquire Splunk for $157 per share in cash. Wedbush noted that the acquisition price is "richer" based on implied EV/FCF multiples for 2024 and 2025. The news highlights a significant transaction in the technology sector, impacting both companies involved.

title: Unpacking Cisco’s Decision to Acquire Splunk
summary: Cisco Systems, Inc. announced its intention to acquire Splunk Inc. for approximately $28 billion, aiming to become a global leader in AI, security, and observability. This strategic move is expected to transition cybersecurity from threat detection to prediction and prevention, leveraging the combined power of data and AI. The acquisition, valued at $157 per share in cash, has received unanimous board approval and is anticipated to close by the end of Q3 2024.

title: Is Datadog a Buyout Target After Cisco's Splunk Acquisition?
summary: After Cisco's $28 billion acquisition of Splunk, Datadog (DDOG) is being considered a potential buyout target, given its strong long-term growth potential and current undervaluation. Despite a recent stock dip due to a cautious near-term revenue outlook, Datadog's expanding product suite and critical role in cloud-based monitoring and analytics make it attractive to competitors or large tech companies looking to bolster their cloud software presence. The article suggests that a further drop in its stock price could increase the likelihood of takeover talks.

title: Cisco's $28 billion Splunk deal may ignite software deal frenzy
summary: Cisco's $28 billion acquisition of Splunk is expected to spark a new wave of software vendor acquisitions by other technology giants, driven by the appeal of predictable subscription revenue. Investment bankers and analysts suggest that Splunk's shift to a subscription model and the improving M&A outlook for software companies make similar firms attractive targets for major tech conglomerates like Microsoft, Adobe, and Oracle. This comes as valuations for software stocks are considered cheap by historical standards, encouraging dealmaking in a sector that has seen a recent slowdown.

title: What Recent Cybersecurity Breaches Can Teach Companies
summary: Recent cybersecurity breaches in the gaming industry highlight the critical need for robust cybersecurity measures and Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery (BCDR) plans in all organizations. The article emphasizes that no company is immune to cyber threats and outlines key steps companies can take, including prioritizing cybersecurity at all levels, creating incident response plans, investing in employee education, implementing data protection measures, and collaborating with cybersecurity experts to minimize risks and financial losses.

title: Cisco-Splunk under the microscope: Joint customers weigh in
summary: Cisco's $28 billion acquisition of Splunk is viewed positively by joint customers, particularly for strengthening Cisco's security and observability portfolios, despite some concerns about potential pricing changes. A flash survey from Enterprise Technology Research revealed that while sentiment is generally favorable, especially regarding synergies and product overlap, customers expressed apprehension about higher prices and a 'brain drain' from Splunk. The deal is seen as a strategic move for Cisco to boost its software revenue and international presence, offering Splunk a lifeline after its challenging transition to a cloud and subscription model.

title: Palantir: Implications Of The $28B Mega Cisco-Splunk Deal (NYSE:PLTR)
summary: Cisco's $28 billion acquisition of Splunk underscores the increasing value of AI capabilities in the industry, raising questions about potential takeover targets. Palantir Technologies, with its aggressive focus on AI and growth in Business Intelligence, is identified as a possible acquisition candidate. This deal highlights the broader trend of large corporations seeking to acquire advanced AI capabilities for long-term revenue growth.

title: Cisco wins US court order barring sale of counterfeit equipment
summary: Cisco Systems (CSCO.O) has won a preliminary U.S. court order preventing computer parts reseller Dexon Computer Inc. from selling counterfeit Cisco products. This decision comes as the two companies are engaged in a long-running legal dispute, with Dexon also having filed an antitrust lawsuit against Cisco in Texas. The judge cited "sufficient evidence of counterfeit sales" by Dexon and ordered Cisco to post a $500,000 bond.

title: Oakmark Fund: Third Quarter 2023 and Fiscal Year-End
summary: The Oakmark Fund reported -1.31% return in Q3 2023, outperforming the S&P 500 Total Return Index. The fund highlighted its performance despite headwinds to value, driven by top contributors ConocoPhillips and Charter Communications, and discussed new holdings in Centene, Cisco Systems, Corteva, CVS Health, Danaher Corporation, and Phillips 66, emphasizing their perceived discounts to intrinsic value.

title: Elevate efficiency in converged infrastructure | NetApp Blog
summary: The article discusses how the FlexPod converged infrastructure, integrating NetApp, Cisco, and AMD technologies, can revolutionize IT efficiency. It highlights the benefits of this trio, combining all-flash storage, fortified Cisco compute, and powerful AMD EPYC processors, to deliver enhanced performance, cost savings, and simplified management for enterprise applications and VDI solutions. The integration aims to streamline data management, provide powerful computing, and enable rapid deployment and scaling through automation.

title: Is Now a Good Time to Invest in Cybersecurity?
summary: The cybersecurity market is experiencing significant growth, driven by increasingly sophisticated cyberattacks and regulatory pressure. Despite recent stock dips due to recession concerns and large tech players entering the sector, experts remain bullish on its long-term prospects. Investors can gain exposure through ETFs or individual stocks of companies like IBM, Cisco, and Palo Alto Networks, as the industry is projected to grow substantially by 2030.

title: Unidentified attackers breach tens of thousands of Cisco devices
summary: Tens of thousands of Cisco devices running IOS XE software have been compromised due to an actively exploited vulnerability, CVE-2023-20198. Researchers found that primarily telecommunications companies have been targeted, with attackers gaining full control of devices. Cisco is working on a fix and urges customers to take immediate action, noting that initial activity related to the breach began as early as September 18.

title: Sustainability data — Are we comparing apples and oranges?
summary: This article highlights the critical challenge of lacking data standardization in sustainability efforts, making it difficult to compare and report on environmental metrics effectively. It explains how diverse data sources for sustainability initiatives require standardization to create meaningful KPIs, track progress, and build trust among stakeholders. The author emphasizes that without industry-wide consensus on data standards, organizations will struggle to achieve accurate reporting and collective progress in combating climate change.

title: Unpacking CVE-2023-20198: A Critical Weakness In Cisco IOS XE:
summary: This article discusses CVE-2023-20198, a critical Web UI Privilege Escalation Vulnerability in Cisco IOS XE with a CVSS score of 10, actively exploited in the wild. It allows unauthenticated attackers to create high-privilege accounts. The article also mentions a related new CVE-2023-20273 and highlights that thousands of internet-facing IOS XE systems have been implanted, with many subsequently used for further attacks. Recommendations include disabling the HTTP Server feature on internet-facing systems and monitoring for anomalous behavior.

title: Cisco and Bang & Olufsen Unveil New Wireless Earbuds for Secure Hybrid Work
summary: Cisco and Bang & Olufsen have launched new wireless earbuds, the Bang & Olufsen Cisco 950, designed for the hybrid workforce. These earbuds offer advanced security and device management for IT, high-fidelity audio, and are optimized for Webex Meetings and Calling. The collaboration aims to provide a high-end audio experience with robust functionality for professionals working from anywhere.

title: Driving API Security Forward: Protecting Vehicle-to-Cloud Communications
summary: This article discusses the critical importance of API security in the rapidly evolving automotive industry, where vehicles are becoming complex, interconnected systems. It highlights the challenges posed by the increasing number of ECUs, the long lifecycle of vehicles, and the need to secure communication between vehicles and the cloud. The author also outlines winning strategies for API security, including the use of cloud gateways and robust authentication methods, emphasizing that a comprehensive and integrated approach is essential.

title: Ironclad Named a Leader in 2023 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Contract Life Cycle Management
summary: Ironclad has been recognized as a Leader in the 2023 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Contract Life Cycle Management, marking its second consecutive appearance and an upgrade from its Challenger position in 2022. The company was acknowledged for its Ability to Execute and Completeness of Vision, leveraging AI-powered products to streamline the entire contract lifecycle for major clients like L'Oréal, OpenAI, and Cisco. This recognition affirms Ironclad's mission to enhance contract management through its leading digital platform.

title: Rockwell Announces New Offerings at Automation Fair
summary: Rockwell Automation is set to unveil a range of new services, systems, and solutions at Automation Fair 2023 in Boston, scheduled for November 6-9. These offerings include advancements in OT cybersecurity, enhanced training, new service agreements for spare parts and repairs, and updates to the PlantPAx system. The company will also showcase innovative hardware and software, such as AI-powered asset risk predictors and new industrial data platforms.

title: Arista's stock soared 14% after its stand-out Q3 report and Oracle customer news
summary: Arista's shares rose 14% following a strong Q3 report and the announcement that Oracle has become a major new customer. The Santa Clara networking equipment provider reported better-than-expected financial results. Arista CEO Jayshree Ullal confirmed the new Oracle partnership.

title: Fortinet expands SASE coverage with two new data centres in India
summary: Fortinet is expanding its SASE coverage by investing in two new data centers in Bengaluru and Pune, India. These centers will provide Universal SASE and cloud-based services, addressing the rising demand for cloud-delivered solutions and the need for strengthened defenses against cyber threats in India. This move complements Fortinet's existing R&D and support centers and aims to enhance security for India's hybrid and remote workforce.

title: SolarWinds hack explained: Everything you need to know
summary: The SolarWinds hack, identified as one of the largest cybersecurity breaches, involved malicious code injected into its Orion IT monitoring software, affecting thousands of organizations including the U.S. government. Suspected Russian nation-state hackers, referred to as Nobelium, gained widespread access, leading to significant investigations and legal actions against SolarWinds. The incident highlighted critical vulnerabilities in supply chain security and spurred new cybersecurity initiatives and regulations, such as the mandating of Software Bill of Materials (SBOMs).

title: Upwork Introduces AI Apps, Offers, and Educational Content
summary: Upwork has launched new AI apps, offers, and educational content to empower independent talent on its platform with generative AI tools. These initiatives include partnerships with industry leaders like Adobe and Amazon, access to training resources, and the introduction of Upwork Chat Pro, a GPT-4-powered app. The goal is to enhance productivity, automate tasks, and improve work quality for freelancers, addressing the growing demand for AI-related projects.

title: Cisco taps ex-Microsoft executive Rodney Clark to lead partner network
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. has appointed Rodney Clark, a former Microsoft executive, as its new senior vice president for Partnerships and Small & Medium Business. Clark will be responsible for leading Cisco's global partner network. His appointment was announced on November 6, 2023.

title: Commvault Cloud Unifies Data Protection, Security, AI And Recovery
summary: Commvault has launched Commvault Cloud, an AI-powered platform that unifies data protection, security, intelligence, and recovery with a focus on cyber resilience. The platform integrates new Metallic AI capabilities, including the AI copilot Arlie, and offers solutions like Cleanroom Recovery and the upcoming Platinum Resilience fully managed service. This move addresses the increasing complexity of data security in hybrid, multi-cloud environments and the market shift towards integrated suite solutions.

title: Arista partners with Zscaler to jump into zero-trust networking
summary: Arista Networks has announced a partnership with Zscaler Inc. to enhance network security through the integration of its cloud networking solutions with Zscaler's Zero Trust Exchange platform. This collaboration aims to offer businesses an improved defense against cyberthreats without requiring a complete overhaul of existing infrastructure, by implementing granular security controls and identity-driven policies at the network's edge. The partnership will focus on sharing security intelligence between their platforms and extending zero-trust principles to secure unclassified devices and legacy systems.

title: Arm Announces Appointment of Ami Badani as Chief Marketing Officer
summary: Arm Holdings plc has announced the appointment of Ami Badani as its new Chief Marketing Officer (CMO). Badani, who previously served as Vice President of Marketing and Developer Products at NVIDIA, will lead Arm's global marketing organization and report directly to CEO Rene Haas. Her expertise in AI and developer ecosystems is expected to be crucial as Arm focuses on expanding its compute platform for the AI era.

title: CSCO Earnings: Cisco reports higher revenue and profit for Q1 2024
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (NASDAQ: CSCO) announced strong Q1 2024 results, with revenues up 8% year-over-year to $14.7 billion and earnings rising to $1.11 per share. The company achieved its strongest Q1 in history for both revenue and profitability, attributing confidence in future growth to AI, Security, Cloud, and Observability. Cisco also provided guidance for the second quarter.

title: Walmart, Cisco Systems fall; Macy's Williams-Sonoma rise, Thursday, 11/16/2023
summary: On Thursday, November 16, 2023, several major companies experienced significant stock price changes. Walmart, Cisco Systems, Palo Alto Networks, Children’s Place Inc., and Shoe Carnival Inc. saw their stock prices fall following disappointing forecasts or results. Conversely, Macy's Inc., Tetra Tech Inc., and Williams-Sonoma Inc. rose due to strong financial reports or encouraging outlooks.

title: Cisco Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ:CSCO) Q1 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Cisco Systems, Inc. reported strong Q1 fiscal year 2024 results, exceeding revenue and EPS expectations, driven by solid gross margins and expense control. CEO Chuck Robbins highlighted that while product deliveries have been exceptionally strong, customers are now focused on implementation, causing a temporary slowdown in new orders. The company expects order growth to rebound in the second half of the fiscal year and is confident in its long-term growth trajectory, especially given opportunities in AI infrastructure, security, observability, and the impending acquisition of Splunk.

title: Casa Systems Appoints Former Cisco Executive Sanjay Kaul as Chief Revenue Officer; Chief Customer Officer Alfred de Cárdenas to Retire at End of Year
summary: Casa Systems (Nasdaq: CASA) has appointed former Cisco executive Sanjay Kaul as its new Chief Revenue Officer, effective November 27, 2023. This appointment comes as Chief Customer Officer Alfred de Cárdenas plans to retire at the end of the year after 36 years in the industry. Kaul brings extensive experience in telecom, IT, and enterprise sectors, with a focus on driving value creation and business transformation.

title: Cisco Launches New Business Performance Insight and Visibility for Modern Applications on AWS
summary: Cisco has introduced new business metrics for its Cloud Observability platform, powered by the Cisco Observability Platform. This enhancement aims to provide improved business context for modern applications running on Amazon Web Services (AWS), offering end-to-end visibility into application performance. The new features help bridge the gap between IT operations and business goals by correlating application performance with critical business KPIs, thereby allowing teams to protect revenue, enhance customer experiences, and manage brand reputation more effectively.

title: Online Meeting App Market is Booming Worldwide | Adobe Systems, Microsoft, Cisco WebEx, Google, Logitech International
summary: A new research publication by Advance Market Analytics predicts significant growth for the global Online Meeting App Market, driven by increasing R&D spending and the adoption of high-ability broadband services. The study, which includes major players like Google, Microsoft, and Zoom, highlights market opportunities such as strategic alliances and mobile video conferencing. Key trends identified are the emergence of cloud-based applications and the integration of advanced technologies.

title: Cisco Redefines Cybersecurity Defense with Powerful, Portfolio-Wide Artificial Intelligence Capabilities
summary: Cisco has introduced its AI Assistant for Security, integrating artificial intelligence throughout its Security Cloud platform to enhance cybersecurity defenses. This AI Assistant leverages one of the world's largest security-focused datasets, processing over 550 billion security events daily, to help organizations make informed decisions and automate complex tasks. New capabilities include an AI Assistant for Firewall Policy and an AI-powered Encrypted Visibility Engine for all firewall models, aiming to simplify policy management and detect malware in encrypted traffic without decryption.

title: Cisco Introduces AI Assistant for Security
summary: Cisco has launched its AI Assistant for Security, integrating artificial intelligence throughout its Security Cloud platform. This AI-driven tool is designed to enhance cybersecurity defenses by helping organizations make informed decisions, automate complex tasks, and augment existing capabilities. Trained on billions of security events daily, the AI Assistant aims to close the gap between cybersecurity intent and outcomes, initially focusing on firewall policy management and encrypted traffic visibility.

title: Navigating Retail Disruption: Maximize Customer Centricity and Business Performance with Observability
summary: This article discusses how Cisco Full-Stack Observability solutions help retailers enhance customer centricity and business performance in a competitive, digitally transformed retail landscape. It highlights the importance of flawless digital experiences, optimized application performance, robust security, and cost control for both online and in-store operations. The solutions offer real-time visibility, insights, and remediation steps to ensure seamless customer journeys, secure transactions, and efficient resource utilization, ultimately driving customer loyalty and revenue.

title: Two New Marvell OCTEON 10 Processors Bring Server-Class Performance to Networking Devices
summary: Marvell Technology has introduced two new OCTEON 10 data processing units (DPUs), the OCTEON 10 CN102 and CN103, designed to bring server-class performance and efficiency to networking devices. These 5 nm DPUs, featuring up to eight Arm Neoverse N2 cores, offer significant performance improvements and power reduction for applications in routers, firewalls, and 5G infrastructure. Several major networking equipment manufacturers are already integrating these new processors into their product designs.

title: Operation Blacksmith: Lazarus targets organizations worldwide using novel Telegram-based malware written in DLang
summary: Cisco Talos has uncovered "Operation Blacksmith," a new campaign by the Lazarus Group utilizing three novel DLang-based malware families, including two remote access trojans (RATs) and a downloader. One RAT, NineRAT, uniquely communicates via Telegram bots for command and control. The campaign targets manufacturing, agricultural, and physical security sectors globally, often exploiting the Log4j vulnerability (CVE-2021-44228) for initial access.

title: Cisco defeats Centripetal patent case that led to $2.75 bln award
summary: A federal judge in Virginia has rejected Centripetal Networks' $2.75 billion patent infringement claims against Cisco Systems. This decision overturns a 2020 award that was previously invalidated due to an ethics conflict involving the original judge's ownership of Cisco stock. The case, which centered on network-security technology patents, was reheard by a new judge who found no infringement by Cisco.

title: Splunk lays off 132 people from San Jose offices
summary: Splunk Inc. laid off 176 people from its dual headquarters in San Jose and San Francisco, weeks after announcing its acquisition by Cisco Systems Inc. The layoffs impact 132 individuals in its San Jose offices. This move follows Splunk's recent acquisition and affects senior workers as part of broader company adjustments.

title: New KV-Botnet Targeting Cisco, DrayTek, and Fortinet Devices for Stealthy Attacks
summary: A new botnet, dubbed KV-botnet by Lumen Technologies' Black Lotus Labs, is leveraging compromised firewalls and routers from Cisco, DrayTek, Fortinet, and NETGEAR to facilitate covert data transfer for advanced persistent threat actors, including China-linked Volt Typhoon. The botnet consists of two complementary clusters (KV and JDY) and has been active since at least February 2022, primarily targeting edge devices in enterprises. The malware operates entirely in-memory, making detection difficult but vulnerable to power cycling, though re-infection remains a risk.

title: Cisco Systems Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (CSCO) saw its stock rise by 0.74% to $50.24 on a mixed trading day, outperforming the S&P 500 which rose 0.45%, and the Dow Jones which remained mostly unchanged. This marks the second consecutive day of gains for the company's stock.

title: Cisco to Acquire Isovalent to Define the Future of Multicloud Networking and Security
summary: Cisco announced its intent to acquire Isovalent, a leader in open source cloud-native networking and security, to enhance its secure networking capabilities across public clouds. This acquisition will strengthen the Cisco Security Cloud vision by integrating Isovalent's contributions to eBPF, Cilium, and Tetragon, providing advanced protection for workloads across any cloud. The Isovalent team will join the Cisco Security Business Group, and Cisco is committed to supporting the open source communities behind these technologies.

title: Intellexa and Cytrox: From fixer-upper to Intel Agency-grade spyware
summary: Cisco Talos has conducted an in-depth analysis of Intellexa, a spyware vendor previously known as Cytrox, detailing its operational paradigms, procedures, and the evolution of its Predator spyware. The analysis reveals that Intellexa's Predator spyware continues to evade detection, offers persistence even after device reboots, and demonstrates a sophisticated business model with high-priced solutions targeting state-sponsored agencies. Despite public disclosures and the US adding Intellexa to its Entity List, the company effectively continues its operations by adapting to patched exploits and maintaining plausible deniability through its sales and delivery methods.

title: A Look Into Cisco Systems Inc's Price Over Earnings
summary: This article examines Cisco Systems Inc.'s stock performance and price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio. Cisco's stock has seen a 0.52% spike in the current session, with a 4.68% increase over the past month and 5.39% over the past year. The company's P/E ratio of 15.02 is lower than the Communications Equipment industry's aggregate P/E of 104.65, potentially indicating undervaluation or expectations of slower future growth.

title: Avoiding Low Growth Prospects: A Case Study
summary: This article discusses how to evaluate a company's growth potential using free cash flow (FCF), cautioning that not all high FCF yields indicate strong growth, as some reflect slower prospects. It highlights the VictoryShares Free Cash Flow ETF (VFLO) as an example of a fund that incorporates a growth filter to select large-cap companies with both high FCF and favorable growth outlooks. The case study uses Cisco to illustrate how a cash-rich company can still have slow growth relative to the market, emphasizing the need for forward-looking measures in FCF analysis.

title: The five biggest acquisitions of 2023 in Israeli tech
summary: This article details the five largest acquisitions of Israeli tech companies in 2023, with cybersecurity firms dominating the list. Talon Cyber Security led with a $625 million acquisition by Palo Alto Networks, followed by Perimeter 81 ($490 million by Check Point), Axis Security ($400 million by HPE), Bionic ($350 million by CrowdStrike), and Dig Security ($315 million by Palo Alto Networks). The report highlights that M&A activity remained strong despite macroeconomic challenges, particularly within the cybersecurity sector.

title: CIBR ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns NASDAQ:CIBR
summary: This article provides a detailed analysis of the First Trust NASDAQ Cybersecurity ETF (CIBR), examining its dividends, returns, and key characteristics. It highlights CIBR's focus on cybersecurity companies, including software, networking, and aerospace & defense sectors, and details its asset allocation, top holdings, and expense ratio. The article also covers CIBR's recent performance metrics and dividend payout history.

title: Report: HPE closing on $13B deal to buy Juniper Networks
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is reportedly nearing a $13 billion deal to acquire Juniper Networks, with an announcement potentially coming this week. This acquisition is seen as a strategic move for HPE to strengthen its position in the rapidly growing artificial intelligence industry and expand its networking business. Analysts suggest the deal could help HPE's Aruba Networks and provide growth drivers through Juniper's cloud and WAN businesses, despite initial skepticism from some market observers.

title: Why Would HPE Buy Juniper Networks?
summary: HPE is reportedly planning to acquire Juniper Networks for $14 billion, aiming to revive its datacenter networking presence and bolster its position in the AI era's networking demands. This acquisition follows HPE's successful integration of Aruba Networks and aligns with the increasing importance of high-performance, low-latency networking in modern AI clusters. While the financial implications and integration challenges exist, the move could significantly strengthen HPE's hardware, software, and services portfolio.

title: HPE, Juniper Deal Makes an AI Networking Giant. What It Means for Cisco Stock.
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise's acquisition of Juniper Networks will create the second-largest networking company in the U.S. market. This deal presents both opportunities and challenges for competitors like Cisco Systems and Arista Networks, particularly in the AI networking space. The offer price for Juniper was at a significant premium, highlighting the strategic importance of this merger.

title: Inside HPE’s acquisition of Juniper Networks and the battle for networking supremacy
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. has significantly expanded its networking business with the acquisition of Juniper Networks Inc. for approximately $14 billion. Industry analysts view this as a strategic move to leverage Juniper's AI advancements, particularly its Mist AI service, to strengthen HPE's position in the AI and cloud-native markets, rather than a pure innovation play. The acquisition aims to enhance HPE's edge-to-cloud capabilities and allow it to better compete against industry giants like Cisco Systems Inc.

title: How Cisco innovations drive the consumer electronic revolution
summary: Cisco plays a crucial, often unseen, role in the consumer electronics revolution by providing underlying networking and security infrastructure. The company's innovations in AI, security, transportation, and sustainability are central to enabling seamless digital experiences and supporting advanced technologies showcased at events like CES. Cisco is focused on making the connected world more efficient, secure, and sustainable for both businesses and consumers.

title: HPE To Acquire Juniper Networks: What You Need To Know
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise announced plans to acquire Juniper Networks in a $14 billion deal, aiming to significantly boost its AI networking capabilities and double its networking business. This acquisition is seen by analysts and partners as a move to challenge Cisco's dominance in the networking market, especially in the AI-powered era. The success of the deal, which brings together HPE, HPE Aruba, and Juniper, will depend heavily on effective integration to create a new AI networking giant.

title: Top 10 Video Conferencing Companies | Tech Connects Virtually
summary: The video conferencing market is experiencing significant growth, projected to reach USD 21 billion by 2032, driven by remote work trends and technological advancements. This article highlights the top 10 companies dominating this market, including Zoom, Microsoft, and Google, detailing their overviews, financial performance, geographical presence, and recent developments. The information aims to provide insights into the key players in this rapidly expanding sector.

title: The Largest Announced M&A Deals of 2023
summary: The article provides an overview of the largest M&A deals announced in 2023, highlighting significant consolidations across various sectors. Key acquisitions include ExxonMobil and Chevron in energy, Pfizer and Cisco in pharmaceuticals and technology, and major movements in telecommunications, electric vehicles, and mining. These deals reflect strategic growth, diversification, and expansion in a dynamic economic environment.

title: Live music, cheap meals to be served at Goodnight Johnny's restaurant in Burlington
summary: John Thibault, a former tech executive, is opening "Goodnight Johnny's Music Bar" in Burlington on January 30, offering affordable American comfort food and live music. After a life-changing medical event ended his tech career, Thibault decided to pursue his passion for food and music, aiming to create a community spot where families can enjoy good meals and various genres of live entertainment, with nothing on the menu exceeding $30. He leveraged his entrepreneurial experience to build a team and a concept focused on great food, service, value, and music.

title: Companies are struggling to keep private data safe from generative AI, Cisco says
summary: A new Cisco report indicates that despite corporate bans and restrictions on generative AI tools, employees are still entering private company data into them. The survey of 2,600 privacy and security professionals found that a significant number of companies have either banned or limited AI usage due to concerns about data leaks and intellectual property infringement. This highlights a struggle for companies to balance the benefits of AI with the imperative to protect sensitive information.

title: Midnight Blizzard: Guidance for responders on nation-state attack
summary: Microsoft Security detected and responded to a nation-state attack by Midnight Blizzard (NOBELIUM) on its corporate systems on January 12, 2024. The actor utilized a password spray attack on a legacy test account without MFA and leveraged malicious OAuth applications to access corporate email via Exchange Web Services, obscuring their activity with residential proxy networks. Microsoft provides guidance for other organizations to defend against similar attacks, focusing on auditing OAuth application privileges, protecting against password sprays, and utilizing XDR/SIEM alerts to detect malicious activity.

title: Network giant Cisco, reseller Dexon to end legal fight
summary: Cisco Systems and independent reseller Dexon have resolved their legal disputes in California and Texas federal courts. Dexon will drop its antitrust lawsuit against Cisco and pay an unspecified amount to resolve claims of selling counterfeit Cisco equipment. Dexon has also agreed to stop selling unauthorized Cisco products, change its business model, and apply to join Cisco’s authorized reseller program.

title: Lazard Appoints Dan Schulman and Stephen Howe Jr. to Its Board of Directors
summary: Lazard, Inc. (NYSE: LAZ) has appointed Dan Schulman, former PayPal CEO, and Stephen R. Howe Jr., former US Chairman and Managing Partner of the Americas for Ernst & Young, to its Board of Directors, effective February 1, 2024. This expansion aims to leverage their vast experience in leading and transforming public companies, financial expertise, and corporate governance to drive Lazard's future growth and ambitions. Both new board members expressed enthusiasm for joining Lazard during this phase of growth, highlighting their commitment to contributing to the firm's strategic direction.

title: Cisco Collaborates with Microsoft and Samsung to Deliver Superior Meeting Room Experiences
summary: Cisco, Microsoft, and Samsung have partnered to introduce new integrated video collaboration solutions for Cisco Room series, featuring Microsoft Teams Rooms with Front Row and Samsung's Smart Signage displays. These solutions aim to provide seamless and inclusive hybrid meeting experiences for all participants, addressing the growing need for high-quality, easily deployable, and manageable meeting technology in the era of hybrid work. The collaboration emphasizes immersive views, AI-powered collaboration, simplified management, increased scalability, and seamless interoperability.

title: Dynatrace’s latest moves and the future of observability
summary: Dynatrace has recently announced several strategic initiatives, including the acquisition of Runecast, a new AI Observability feature for LLMs, and the introduction of OpenPipeline to address data ingestion challenges. These moves are aimed at consolidating observability tools, enhancing security, and optimizing AI-powered applications, positioning Dynatrace as a key player in the evolving platform-centric observability market. The article highlights Dynatrace's competitive stance against major players like Splunk and Datadog, supported by recent Enterprise Technology Research data.

title: Zscaler COO suddenly resigns, joins cloud security unicorn Wiz
summary: Dali Rajic, former COO of Zscaler, has resigned to take the same position at cloud security competitor Wiz. Wiz aims to achieve $1 billion in ARR and plans for an initial public offering (IPO), with Rajic leading its growth strategy and operations. Rajic emphasized Wiz's rapid growth and market potential at the intersection of cloud, cybersecurity, and AI.

title: Cisco Unveils New Innovations on the Cisco Observability Platform
summary: Cisco announced new innovations for its Observability Platform, including Digital Experience Monitoring with Real User Monitoring and Session Replay, observability for Kubernetes workloads using eBPF, and a Unified Observability Experience. These enhancements aim to provide customers with better visibility, insights, and actions across their technology stack, improving overall performance and user experience, and reducing tool sprawl. The update also introduces a GenAI-powered natural language interface for troubleshooting and expanded use cases for business risk observability with Data Security Posture Management.

title: Analyzing all the security and AI news from Cisco Live EMEA 2024
summary: Cisco Live EMEA 2024 showcased significant innovations in security and AI, with a strong emphasis on addressing key IT pain points. The company introduced Cisco Identity Intelligence and AI Assistant for Secure Access to combat identity-based attacks and streamline security administration. Additionally, Cisco unveiled new Validated Designs for AI infrastructure and expanded its partnership with Nvidia to provide diverse networking solutions for AI deployments.

title: Cisco Systems Inc. stock underperforms Wednesday when compared to competitors
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (CSCO) stock underperformed on Wednesday, shedding 0.42% to close at $49.77, despite a generally favorable trading session for the broader market. The S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average both saw gains, rising 0.82% and 0.40% respectively. Cisco's stock closed $8.42 short of its 52-week high, which was reached on September 1st.

title: Exclusive: Cisco to cut thousands of jobs as it seeks to focus on high growth areas
summary: Cisco is reportedly planning to restructure its business, which will involve laying off thousands of employees to focus on high-growth areas. An announcement could come as early as next week, ahead of its earnings call on Feb. 14. This move follows a previous restructuring in late 2022 and comes as many other tech companies are also cutting jobs.

title: Cisco Delivers 800Gbps on Amitié Transatlantic Cable in Collaboration with Microsoft
summary: Cisco and Microsoft successfully conducted an 800Gbps transmission trial on the Amitié transatlantic cable, spanning 6,234 kilometers from Boston to Bordeaux. This achievement supports the escalating demand for increased subsea network capacity driven by the growth of cloud and AI services. The trial utilized Cisco’s NCS 1014 platform with Acacia’s Coherent Interconnect Module 8, setting a new performance benchmark for SDM subsea cables.

title: Illusions & Accessories LLC
summary: Illusions & Accessories LLC, owned by Shannon Storms, has evolved over twenty-five years from handling personal computers to managing business networks across Mississippi, Alabama, and Louisiana. Storms holds numerous trade certifications, and the company offers services including computer repair, network management, and security installations. They also have a division, Eyes in the Sky Surveillance, and are premium hospitality installers for Guest WiFi in various hotel brands.

title: Cisco Plans to Cut Thousands of Jobs After Sales Growth Stalls
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. plans to cut approximately 4,000 jobs, representing about 5% of its global workforce, due to a slowdown in corporate tech spending impacting sales growth. This restructuring effort is expected to cost the company around $500 million. The move comes as Cisco, the largest networking equipment maker, grapples with a challenging economic environment.

title: Cisco Systems Stock Tumbles Amid Cautious Guidance, Job Cuts—Key Indicator to Watch
summary: Cisco Systems (CSCO) shares fell sharply after the company released cautious fiscal third-quarter and full-year guidance and announced plans to cut 5% of its global workforce. The company's projections for earnings and revenue fell short of analysts' expectations, attributing the conservative outlook to macroeconomic uncertainty and weaker demand from certain sectors. Investors are advised to watch a key long-term uptrend line, as a breakdown below it could signal further declines for the stock.

title: FBI Dismantles Ubiquiti Router Botnet Controlled by Russian Cyberspies
summary: The FBI has neutralized a botnet composed of Ubiquiti Edge OS routers that was controlled by Russia's APT28 hacking group, also known as Fancy Bear. The collective of nation-state threat actors repurposed the botnet, initially built using 'Moobot' malware by other cybercriminals, into a global espionage platform. US law enforcement used a court order to disrupt the botnet by blocking remote management access to the compromised devices and collecting routing information to expose Russian attempts to thwart the operation.

title: Cisco's AI push in focus as shares fall on tepid networking gear demand
summary: Cisco Systems' shares fell over 3% after the company lowered its annual revenue forecast and announced job cuts due to sluggish demand for networking equipment. Despite the downturn, Cisco is focusing on AI and cybersecurity, including a partnership with Nvidia and the impending acquisition of Splunk, to counter the weak market. Analysts note Cisco's AI pipeline growth but acknowledge its relatively small scale compared to the company's overall operations.

title: Cisco Systems Inc. stock underperforms Friday when compared to competitors
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (CSCO) stock fell 1.26% on Friday, closing at $48.44, underperforming the broader market. This marked the second consecutive day of losses for the company, as the S&P 500 Index (SPX) dropped 0.48% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) fell 0.37%.

title: Palo Alto Shares Plunge on Strategy Shift
summary: Palo Alto Networks' shares plunged over 25% after the company announced a strategic shift towards "platformization" with aggressive promotions and incentives, leading to weaker-than-expected guidance. While the company's Q2 earnings beat estimates, investors reacted negatively to the revised full-year billings forecast. CEO Nikesh Arora believes this strategy is necessary to address "pricing fatigue" in the cybersecurity market and gain market share from competitors.

title: After Earnings, Is Arista Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar maintains that Arista Networks' stock is overvalued despite strong fourth-quarter earnings and a bullish long-term forecast driven by high-speed cloud providers and AI investments. While the company is positioned for significant market share gains, its current valuation is considered overexuberant. Morningstar assigns Arista a "wide moat" rating due to its intangible assets and customer switching costs in high-speed networking, but also a "High Uncertainty Rating" due to customer concentration and challenges in expanding into new market segments.

title: AbbVie Sells $15 Billion of Bonds to Buy ImmunoGen, Cerevel
summary: AbbVie Inc. has sold $15 billion of bonds in the US investment-grade market to help finance its acquisitions of ImmunoGen Inc. and Cerevel Therapeutics Holdings Inc. The pharmaceutical giant offered the securities in seven parts, with the longest portion, a 40-year bond, yielding 105 basis points over Treasuries. This bond sale adds to a recent trend of large buyout financings in the market.

title: Why Cisco Systems Inc Stock Is A Buy? Acquirer’s Multiple Stock Screener Analysis
summary: This article analyzes Cisco Systems Inc (CSCO) using The Acquirer's Multiple stock screener, highlighting it as an undervalued 'buy' based on key fundamentals despite a recent 4.46% share price decline. Cisco is identified as a leading provider of networking equipment and cybersecurity software, with strong market shares. The analysis provides key financial stats such as market cap, enterprise value, operating earnings, and free cash flow to support its valuation.

title: AI poised to bolster workplace efficiency and security, Cisco exec says
summary: Cisco Executive Vice President Jeetu Patel believes AI will significantly enhance workplace efficiency and security. He highlights AI's potential to address talent shortages in cybersecurity and software development by acting as a "force multiplier." Patel also foresees AI generating original insights, not just aggregating existing knowledge, which could lead to breakthroughs in areas like cancer research, while emphasizing the need for thoughtful consideration of the technology's dangers.

title: GE Vernova and Dragos team up to protect electric grids from cyber threats
summary: GE Vernova’s Grid Solutions and Dragos Inc. have partnered to provide comprehensive industrial cybersecurity solutions to enhance electric grid resilience. This collaboration will integrate their expertise in grid automation and operational technology (OT) cybersecurity, offering advanced functionalities like asset visibility, proactive threat detection, and rapid incident response to protect critical infrastructure. The partnership aims to safeguard critical infrastructure and other OT environments for GE Vernova's global grid automation customers.

title: MWC 2024’s Biggest Telecom Announcements From HPE, Palo Alto, Samsung And Verizon
summary: Mobile World Congress (MWC) 2024 in Barcelona brought forth significant telecom announcements and partnerships focused on edge networking, SASE, and 5G technologies. Key players like Cisco, Ericsson/Cradlepoint, Kyndryl with HPE, Nokia with Intel, Palo Alto Networks, Red Hat with NTT, Samsung with Juniper Networks and Wind River, and Verizon all revealed new services, collaborations, and advancements aimed at enhancing network efficiency, security, and scalability. These initiatives range from AI-ready services and private 5G deployments to energy savings in 5G networks and network-as-a-service offerings.

title: Automation at Its Best: A Look at FlexPod Intersight Workflow
summary: This article highlights the FlexPod Intersight Workflow project, an open-source initiative utilizing Cisco Intersight Cloud Orchestrator to automate and streamline data center operations. It emphasizes the benefits of automation, including increased efficiency, simplicity, and scalability in managing FlexPod infrastructure. The workflow offers various capabilities for NetApp ONTAP configurations and end-to-end management of storage virtual machines, initiator groups, and datastores.

title: HPE’s stock drops on big revenue decline, while NetApp heads upward
summary: Hewlett-Packard Enterprise (HPE) experienced a significant revenue decline in its first fiscal quarter, causing its stock to drop, despite exceeding profitability expectations. The company is struggling with its server segment and GreenLake revenues, facing increased competition and GPU procurement issues for AI-related sales. In contrast, NetApp reported increased sales driven by rising demand for AI storage and its all-flash arrays, leading to a substantial gain in its stock value.

title: Dow's nearly 125-point rally led by gains for shares of NVIDIA Corp., Cisco
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose by nearly 125 points, primarily driven by strong performances from NVIDIA Corp. and Cisco Systems Inc. NVIDIA's shares surged after an analyst upgrade, while Cisco remained relatively flat despite an analyst reiteration of an overweight rating. The overall market saw NVIDIA and Cisco among the top gainers, contrasting with losses from other Dow components.

title: NetApp Turbocharges AI Innovation with Intelligent Data Infrastructure
summary: NetApp has introduced new capabilities for its intelligent data infrastructure, aiming to enhance generative AI projects and provide a competitive advantage. These updates include AIPod for AI-optimized converged infrastructure, new FlexPod for AI reference architectures, and validation for NVIDIA OVX systems. NetApp also announced Autonomous Ransomware Protection with AI (ARP/AI) embedded in storage, leveraging machine learning to combat sophisticated cyber threats.

title: NetApp Turbocharges AI Innovation with Intelligent Data Infrastructure
summary: NetApp announced new capabilities for its intelligent data infrastructure, integrating with NVIDIA's high-performance compute, networking, and software, to maximize the potential of generative AI (Gen AI) projects. These updates include NetApp AIPod™ certifications for NVIDIA DGX BasePOD, new FlexPod for AI reference architectures, and validation for NVIDIA OVX systems. Additionally, NetApp is introducing Autonomous Ransomware Protection with AI (ARP/AI) embedded in storage to combat sophisticated cyber threats.

title: Cisco gains EU antitrust nod for $28 bln Splunk acquisition
summary: Cisco Systems (CSCO.O) has received unconditional EU antitrust approval for its $28 billion acquisition of cybersecurity firm Splunk (SPLK.O). The European Commission approved the deal after concluding it would not raise competition concerns due to its limited market impact and the presence of sufficient alternative players. This acquisition, Cisco's largest ever, aims to enhance its software business amid the artificial intelligence boom and offset a post-pandemic slowdown.

title: Cisco Closes $28 Billion Splunk Deal Without Antitrust Drama
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. successfully closed its $28 billion acquisition of Splunk in just six months, bypassing significant antitrust scrutiny. This quick approval, attributed by CEO Chuck Robbins to the differing business focuses of the two companies, stands in contrast to many other large mergers facing regulatory delays.

title: Cisco Systems Completes Its $28 Billion Purchase of Splunk
summary: Cisco Systems announced it has completed its $28 billion acquisition of cybersecurity firm Splunk, just days after European antitrust approval. This strategic move aims to expand Cisco's presence in the AI market by integrating Splunk's security and observability platforms, which Cisco views as critical for powering, developing, protecting, and managing AI in real time. The all-cash deal, valued at $157 per share, is expected to be cash flow positive and gross margin accretive for Cisco in fiscal year 2025, with an increase in earnings per share anticipated in fiscal year 2026.

title: Climb Takes On Big Disties with Emerging Vendor Focus
summary: Climb Global Solutions is challenging large distributors by focusing on incubating emerging technology brands that are underserved by broadline distribution. The company, led by CEO Dale Foster, has strategically acquired several European distributors and aims to provide value to emerging vendors that larger distributors, focused on established brands like Cisco and Microsoft, cannot. Climb plans further acquisitions and EMEA expansion, asserting that the market for emerging vendors is radically underserved.

title: AMD Showcases Networking Security Innovations, 800G Ethernet Interoperability at OFC 2024
summary: AMD will showcase its latest networking security innovations and 800G Ethernet interoperability at OFC 2024. The demonstrations include 800G Ethernet using Versal Premium devices, AI-inferencing for DDoS mitigation, and 100Gbps TLS-1.3 offload for enhanced network security. These advancements aim to address the increasing demand for network performance and security in core network infrastructures and edge nodes.

title: Cisco completes acquisition of enterprise resilience giant Splunk for $28B
summary: Cisco has successfully acquired Splunk for approximately $28 billion, integrating market-leading security and observability capabilities. This merger aims to provide organizations with real-time unified views of their digital enterprises, enhancing security, observability, networking, and AI integration. The combined entity is poised to leverage data to power and protect the AI revolution, offering comprehensive solutions for critical infrastructure defense.

title: Cisco Announces New Multifunctional Collaboration Devices for Hybrid Work
summary: Cisco introduced new multifunctional collaboration devices, the Cisco Board Pro G2 and Cisco Desk Phone 9800 Series, designed to enhance hybrid work environments. These AI-powered devices aim to provide seamless and personalized collaboration experiences across various office spaces. The offerings address the growing need for advanced technology to support meaningful collaboration as companies transition to permanent hybrid models.

title: Cisco and Ford Motor Company Rollout Webex App for Productivity on the Move
summary: Cisco and Ford Motor Company have partnered to integrate the Webex app into Ford and Lincoln vehicles equipped with the Ford and Lincoln Digital Experience, enhancing in-vehicle productivity. This collaboration allows users to join audio-only Webex meetings while driving and full video meetings with shared content when parked, transforming vehicles into mobile workspaces. The Webex app is now available for download on Google Play for select new Ford and Lincoln models, offering seamless connectivity and collaboration on the go.

title: SEC fines Arista Networks founder Bechtolsheim $1M for insider trading
summary: The SEC has fined Arista Networks founder Andreas “Andy” Bechtolsheim nearly $1 million for insider trading. He allegedly used non-public information about Cisco's planned acquisition of Acacia Communications to arrange put options for a relative and an associate in 2019, yielding significant profits. Bechtolsheim, who resigned as Arista chairman in December, settled with the SEC without admitting or denying the allegations.

title: Observe raises $115M from Sutter Hill, Snowflake Ventures to supercharge data cloud observability
summary: Observe, a San Mateo-based startup focused on data-centric application observability, has secured $115 million in Series B funding. The round was led by Sutter Hill Ventures with participation from existing investors and Snowflake Ventures, highlighting a deepening partnership as Observe leverages Snowflake's data cloud. The funding will be used to expand teams, scale its North American presence, and further develop its AI-driven observability solutions, especially for monitoring Snowflake environments.

title: Cisco Completes Acquisition of Isovalent to Define the Future of Multicloud Networking and Security
summary: Cisco has announced the completion of its acquisition of Isovalent, a leader in open-source cloud-native networking and security. This acquisition aims to strengthen Cisco's multicloud networking and security offerings, integrating Isovalent's technologies into the Cisco Security Cloud vision. Cisco also committed to supporting the open-source communities behind eBPF, Cilium, and Tetragon.

title: Juniper Networks CEO: ‘We’re Just Scratching The Surface’ With AI-Powered Networking
summary: Juniper Networks CEO Rami Rahim emphasizes focusing on real AI use cases and deployments to cut through hype in the networking space. He highlights Juniper's market-leading Mist AI offering, its expansion across the portfolio, and the value it delivers to customers through reduced deployment times, fewer trouble tickets, and proactive issue resolution. Rahim also details Juniper's efforts in empowering partners with AI specializations and new managed services programs.

title: Juniper to cut 500 roles in India
summary: Juniper Networks, following its merger with Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), plans to reduce its India workforce by 10-15 percent, affecting approximately 500 employees. This strategic move, reported by Economic Times, is a result of regulatory approvals for HPE's $14 billion acquisition of Juniper. The layoffs are expected across sales, marketing, shared services, product development, and R&D functions due to anticipated overlapping roles.

title: Cisco Hypershield: Reimagining Security
summary: Cisco Hypershield introduces a new security infrastructure designed to autonomously create defenses and produce measured responses to cyber-attacks across diverse IT environments. This fabric leverages hyper-distributed enforcement, centralized policy management, hitless upgrades via a dual dataplane, and continuous learning to provide complete visibility and graduated responses to anomalous behavior. Hypershield aims to address challenges like network segmentation and distributed exploit protection by offering a robust and precise tool for security teams.

title: Cisco Reimagines Security for Data Centers and Clouds in Era of AI
summary: Cisco has unveiled Hypershield, a new security architecture designed to protect data centers and clouds in the era of AI. This innovation allows for security placement anywhere it's needed, providing AI-native security capabilities such as autonomous network segmentation and distributed exploit protection. Cisco Hypershield aims to tip the scales in favor of defenders against sophisticated AI-driven cyber threats, leveraging hardware acceleration and cloud-native technologies like eBPF.

title: Earth Day: How we’re helping our social and environmental systems heal and thrive
summary: This article details how Cisco and the Cisco Foundation are addressing social and environmental challenges by investing in innovative digital solutions. It highlights three strategic priorities: clean energy transitions, circular transformation, and investing in resilient ecosystems, showcasing projects like Jaza Energy in Tanzania, Green Wolf Costa Rica, and Human-I-T in the US. These initiatives aim to promote a healthy planet, advance community well-being, and combat issues like energy poverty, waste, and the digital divide.

title: Cisco joins Rome Call as AI ethics debate widens
summary: Cisco has joined the Rome Call for AI Ethics, a Vatican-initiated document promoting transparent, accountable, and socially beneficial AI development. The move highlights a growing consensus among technology leaders, religious figures, and civil society organizations on the need for ethical guidelines in AI. Cisco's participation underscores its commitment to principles like transparency, fairness, and accountability, aligning with the Call's push for responsible AI to avoid societal problems seen in past industrial revolutions.

title: Cisco signs the "Rome Call for AI Ethics"
summary: Cisco, a multinational digital communications technology company, has signed the Pontifical Academy for Life’s “Rome Call for AI Ethics”. This commitment signifies Cisco's pledge to an ethical approach to artificial intelligence, focusing on Ethics, Education, and Rights. The move was welcomed by Archbishop Vincenzo Paglia, highlighting the increasing urgency of ethical AI development as this marks a significant step towards ensuring AI respects human dignity and aligns with values of transparency and accountability.

title: CEOs of Microsoft, Nvidia and other tech giants join federal AI advisory board
summary: A federal AI advisory board has been established, comprising prominent tech executives, academics, and civil rights leaders, including CEOs from Microsoft, Nvidia, and OpenAI. Led by Secretary of Homeland Security Alejandro Mayorkas, the board will advise the Department of Homeland Security on the safe application of AI in critical infrastructure and issue recommendations for organizations like power grid operators and manufacturers. This initiative stems from President Biden's executive order on AI, which also mandates data sharing on new AI models' safety with the government.

title: Midtown's Coda building lands new office for insurance giant
summary: The Travelers Companies Inc. has opened a new Midtown Atlanta office in the Coda building. This tower is also home to major companies like Walmart Inc. and Cisco Systems Inc., further establishing its importance as a commercial hub.

title: Seven Corporate Buyers Enter Into Aggregation Deal To Purchase 180 MW of Solar Energy Through the Net Zero Consortium for Buyers
summary: Seven corporations, including Cisco, Juniper Networks, Bio-Rad Laboratories, Cadence Design Systems, IDEXX Laboratories, Inc., PTC, and a large healthcare company, have entered into an aggregated virtual power purchase agreement (VPPA) to collectively buy 180 MW of solar energy from the Millers Branch Solar Facility in Texas. This deal, facilitated by Sustainability Roundtable, Inc.'s Net Zero Consortium for Buyers (NZCB), allows companies with modest energy loads to access utility-scale renewable energy and helps advance their individual net-zero goals. The aggregation is one of the largest peer-buyer organized aggregations globally and brings the NZCB close to its goal of causing a gigawatt of new renewable energy capacity.

title: Richmond Agrees to Divest Money Away From Israel
summary: The Richmond City Council voted 5-0 to divest from companies doing business in Israel, impacting approximately 6.9% of the city's $600 million portfolio. The policy creates a predetermined list of companies involved in Israel's actions concerning Palestinian rights and also restricts investment in companies involved in arms, border/mass surveillance, and mass incarceration/detention industries. Mayor Eduardo Martinez emphasized ethical investing and not profiting from violence, citing historical divestment efforts by the city.

title: Strengthening our U.S. Public Sector Leadership Team with the Promotion of two Industry Veterans
summary: Cisco has announced the promotions of Gary DePreta to Senior Vice President of U.S. Public Sector Sales and Mike Witzman to Senior Director of U.S. Public Sector Solutions Engineering. These strategic appointments aim to reinforce Cisco's commitment to its Public Sector customers. Both individuals bring extensive experience and leadership to their new roles, with backgrounds in technology, government, and military service, positioning them to drive digital transformation for public sector clients.

title: At RSAC, Cisco launches bolsters in Security Cloud, built on Hypershield and Splunk
summary: Cisco announced significant updates to its Security Cloud at the RSA Conference, leveraging Splunk and its Hypershield technology. These enhancements are designed to protect AI-scale data centers, improve threat prevention with AI-driven analytics, and simplify identity management. The move aims to accelerate Cisco's growth in the highly fragmented security market by consolidating offerings and capitalizing on its data advantage.

title: Cisco Systems CEO says his company is ready to leverage artificial intelligence
summary: Cisco Systems CEO Chuck Robbins emphasizes the company's readiness to capitalize on artificial intelligence, learning from past missed opportunities in cloud computing. The company is shifting towards software offerings, including a major acquisition of Splunk, to achieve more stable revenue and compete effectively in the AI-driven cybersecurity market, despite some near-term challenges like inventory digestion and workforce reductions.

title: Cisco Unlocks AI-Powered Intelligence for Self-Hosted Observability
summary: Cisco has introduced new AI-powered capabilities for its self-hosted AppDynamics On-Premises observability solution, designed to enhance anomaly detection, accelerate root cause analysis, and secure business-critical applications. These innovations address needs for data residency and sensitive data protection, particularly for industries sensitive to data governance. Additionally, Cisco announced AppDynamics Flex licensing, offering flexibility for customers to transition from self-hosted to SaaS observability solutions.

title: Cisco adds AI features to AppDynamics On-Premises
summary: Cisco has introduced new AI-driven capabilities to its AppDynamics On-Premises observability platform, including a new virtual appliance designed for anomaly detection, root cause analysis, application security, and SAP monitoring. These enhancements aim to help customers more quickly identify and resolve performance and security issues across their application stack. The platform now also supports deployment on AWS and Microsoft Azure, offering greater flexibility for managing observability solutions.

title: A New Day for Cisco’s Go-to-Market
summary: Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins announced the creation of a new President, Go-to-Market role, filled by former Splunk CEO Gary Steele. Steele will lead Cisco's sales, partner, and global marketing teams to evolve their Go-to-Market strategies, focusing on customer needs and driving growth following the Splunk acquisition. The article also mentions the departure of Jeff Sharritts, EVP and Chief Customer and Partner Officer, and emphasizes Cisco's confidence in its future as a strategic partner to customers in AI, security, observability, and data.

title: CSCO Earnings: All you need to know about Cisco’s Q3 2024 earnings results
summary: Cisco Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ: CSCO) reported its Q3 2024 earnings, with revenues at $12.7 billion, a 13% year-over-year decrease, but still beating estimates. Net income decreased 41% to $1.9 billion, while adjusted EPS of $0.88 surpassed projections. The company provided Q4 2024 and full fiscal year 2024 guidance, and its stock rose over 7% post-earnings.

title: Cisco and Nutanix: Taking Hyperconverged Infrastructure to the .NEXT Level
summary: This article details how the strategic partnership between Cisco and Nutanix is enhancing hyperconverged infrastructure (HCI) solutions to address modern IT challenges. It highlights three core pillars: complete simplicity through innovations like Cisco Intersight Standalone Mode and "GPT-in-a-Box," complete flexibility allowing reuse of Cisco UCS servers and integrated networking choices, and complete resiliency with built-in security and a joint support model. The collaboration aims to provide operational ease, adaptability, and robust infrastructure for customers.

title: Feeling the Heat: The Evolution of Data Centre Cooling
summary: This article traces the evolution of data center cooling systems, from early raised-floor cooling and perimeter fan units to modern water, liquid, and immersion cooling technologies. It highlights the constant challenge of managing heat generated by data centers, which consume significant amounts of electricity, and emphasizes the need for flexibility in design to accommodate future cooling methods. The author discusses various approaches and their trade-offs, underscoring that no single solution fits all needs.

title: Down 15% This Year Is Verisign Stock A Better Pick Over F5 Networks?
summary: Trefis analysts argue that Verisign (VRSN) is a better investment than F5 Networks (FFIV) despite F5's slightly better revenue growth, due to Verisign's superior profitability, stronger financial position, and more attractive valuation relative to its historical averages. The analysis projects Verisign to offer an 18% return over the next three years, compared to 8% for F5. Both stocks have underperformed the broader market, though Verisign has seen a 20% decline while F5 has remained relatively flat since early 2021.

title: Judge dismisses 401(k) suit against Cisco about BlackRock target-date series
summary: A judge has dismissed a 401(k) lawsuit filed against Cisco Systems regarding its use of BlackRock target-date series. The lawsuit was defeated, as reported by Pensions & Investments Lawsuits & Legal.

title: Datadog Stock Begins To Climb Back After Slumping On Q1 Results
summary: Datadog's stock is recovering after an 11.5% slump post-Q1 earnings, despite strong results and raised 2024 revenue outlook. The company's Q1 revenue of $611.2 million beat estimates, with robust RPO growth and increasing product adoption among customers. Analysts remain largely optimistic, pointing to dissipating cloud cost optimization headwinds, improving usage trends, and Datadog's strong positioning in the growing IT operations management and cloud services markets, bolstered by AI adoption and vendor consolidation.

title: Lenovo and Cisco Announce Strategic Partnership to Simplify Path to AI Innovation
summary: Lenovo and Cisco have announced a global strategic partnership to deliver fully integrated infrastructure and networking solutions aimed at accelerating digital transformation and AI innovation for businesses. This partnership involves designing, engineering, and delivering integrated solutions, including the Cisco Nexus networking ecosystem into Lenovo's portfolio, to offer enhanced network performance, security, and scalability. The collaboration also focuses on improving generative AI capabilities and digital workplace productivity through new technologies and a streamlined go-to-market strategy.

title: 23 Tech Stocks Now Yield Higher Dividends Than The S&P 500
summary: The article highlights a significant shift in the tech sector, with 23 tech stocks now offering higher dividend yields than the S&P 500. It discusses how traditional dividend ETFs are underexposed to technology, prompting investors to seek specialized tech dividend ETFs. The piece also notes that while some high-yielding tech stocks like IBM have seen modest share price growth, others such as Qualcomm offer both strong dividends and price appreciation.

title: Sielen Namdar
summary: This article features Sielen Namdar, Cisco's Global Head of Sustainability for Industries, highlighting her role in accelerating impactful sustainability progress across various sectors. It details her background, her passion for driving sustainability with technology, and her commitment to fostering positive change and innovation at a global scale. Namdar's work involves supporting customers in 11 industries with Cisco technology and contributing to a more sustainable future.

title: Cisco Takes $150M Stake in VMware
summary: Cisco Systems has acquired a $150 million equity stake in VMware, reinforcing its "Data Center 3.0" virtualization strategy. This investment makes Cisco the second major tech company, after Intel Capital, to invest in VMware before its anticipated IPO. The move aims to strengthen collaboration between Cisco and VMware to accelerate customer adoption of virtualization products with Cisco networking infrastructure.

title: MSP Update: N-able Reportedly Exploring Sale, More
summary: This article details several significant developments in the MSP sector, including N-able reportedly exploring a sale after attracting acquisition interest, and Kaseya adding a new executive to its C-suite, possibly hinting at an IPO. It also covers Lumifi Cyber's acquisition of Netsurion to bolster its MDR capabilities, Evergreen's unique non-integration model for MSP consolidation, and a new partnership between Lenovo and Cisco for AI-powered digital transformation. Additionally, the article highlights challenges MSPs face in cybersecurity and Sonar's focus on the channel with a new executive to promote clean code.

title: Building a fortress of ‘never trust, always verify’: The power of zero trust architecture
summary: Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA) is presented as a crucial paradigm shift in cybersecurity, moving away from traditional perimeter-based security models. The article details ZTA's core principles—identity verification, context-based connections, and least privilege—and explains how it enhances security, supports remote work, and streamlines cloud adoption. It also outlines steps for implementing ZTA, emphasizing the importance of strong Identity and Access Management and microsegmentation.

title: Cisco Live Day 1 news brings together networking and security
summary: Cisco Live's 2024 event highlights the company's focus on integrating networking and security, especially in the context of AI's growing influence. Cisco announced innovations like Nexus HyperFabric with Nvidia and Nutanix, and advancements in its Security Cloud, aiming to simplify operations and enhance threat protection across its installed base. The company emphasizes a strategy of "melting security into the network" to leverage its heritage and address the evolving tech landscape.

title: Cisco leaps forward in Fortune 500—’I see us clearly as a leader in AI,’ CFO says
summary: Cisco Systems advanced eight spots to No. 74 on this year's Fortune 500 list, driven by its strategic focus on AI and substantial R&D investments. CFO Scott Herren highlights Cisco's strong position in AI, particularly after the $28 billion acquisition of Splunk, emphasizing its capabilities in data, networking, and security. The company is also integrating AI into various products like Webex and security solutions, despite recent staff reductions, while maintaining strong ESG commitments.

title: Cisco Ventures into AI with $1 Billion Investment Fund
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (NASDAQ: CSCO) has launched a $1 billion investment fund aimed at supporting startups that create safe and dependable artificial intelligence services. The company has already invested $200 million into companies like Mistral AI, Scale AI Inc., and Cohere Inc., demonstrating its commitment to enhancing AI innovation and building a robust AI ecosystem. This strategic move positions Cisco as a key player in the rapidly growing AI market, which is projected to exceed $500 billion within the next three years.

title: Cisco Investments launches CVC’s first $1bn AI fund
summary: Cisco Investments has launched a $1 billion AI fund, making it the largest corporate venture capital AI fund to date, surpassing similar initiatives by Salesforce Ventures and IBM. The fund aims to advance innovation in the AI industry and has already committed nearly $200 million to generative AI startups like Scale AI, Mistral, and Cohere. This initiative underscores Cisco's commitment to investing in the broader AI ecosystem and supporting its customers in the AI era.

title: ANET Stock – A Pure Play on Data Center Growth
summary: Arista Networks (ANET) is presented as a pure-play investment in data center growth, having significantly outperformed the Nasdaq since its IPO by selling ethernet hardware. The article discusses Arista's competition with NVIDIA in the data center networking space, particularly concerning InfiniBand versus Ethernet solutions, and examines ANET's valuation compared to peers like NVIDIA, Cisco, and Broadcom. It concludes by highlighting Arista's attractive pure-play exposure to data center expansion but also raises questions about future growth and customer concentration risk as NVIDIA intensifies its presence in the market.

title: Five thoughts from Cisco Live 2024
summary: Cisco Live 2024 showcased the company's aggressive push into AI and the integration of Splunk across its portfolio.
