Key takeaways include Splunk's expansion beyond security and observability, Cisco's emerging comprehensive AI infrastructure story, significant advancements in its security offerings, and the growing importance of ThousandEyes for network visibility. The event highlighted Cisco's transition towards a cloud-centric strategy and the need for more cross-cloud solutions to create integrated customer experiences.

title: Does Fortinet's (FTNT) Cybersecurity Strength Make It a Buy?
summary: Fortinet (FTNT) is a leading cybersecurity company with a comprehensive suite of solutions, poised for growth in a rapidly expanding market. Its Security Fabric platform, advanced threat detection, and multi-cloud support differentiate it, highlighted by strong Q1 2024 financial performance and strategic acquisitions like Lacework. Despite underperforming the Zacks Computer and Technology sector year-to-date and having a stretched valuation, its robust portfolio and innovation make it a key player, currently holding a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

title: Cisco Appoints Ekta Singh-Bushell to Board of Directors
summary: Cisco (NASDAQ: CSCO) announced the appointment of Ekta Singh-Bushell to its board of directors, effective June 14, 2024. Singh-Bushell brings extensive experience in data, security, and services, having previously served at the Federal Reserve Bank of New York and Ernst & Young. She also serves on the public company boards of ChargePoint Holdings, Inc., Huron Consulting Group Inc., Lesaka Technologies, Inc., and TTEC Holdings, Inc.

title: Mastering Nutanix Hyperconverged Infrastructure on Cisco’s Black Belt Academy
summary: This article details the Cisco and Nutanix strategic partnership, focusing on their hyperconverged infrastructure solution and its integration into Cisco's Black Belt Academy. It outlines the comprehensive training tracks available for partners, covering everything from solution overview and architecture to deployment, migration from HyperFlex, and competitive positioning. The partnership aims to streamline hybrid multi-cloud adoption and redefine data center modernization for customers.

title: Advanced Authentication Market size is set to grow by USD 55.87 billion from 2024-2028, increasing volume of online transactions boost the market, Technavio
summary: The global advanced authentication market is projected to grow by USD 55.87 billion from 2024-2028, driven by the increasing volume of online transactions and the adoption of out-of-band (OOB) authentication, according to Technavio. Despite the challenge of rising OTP token costs, the market is set to accelerate at a CAGR of 19.31%, with North America contributing significantly. The growth is fueled by the need for robust security amidst rising cyber threats and the transition from traditional passwords to multi-factor and biometric authentication solutions.

title: U.K. Regulator to Scrutinize Hewlett Packard Enterprise-Juniper Networks Merger
summary: The U.K.'s Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) will investigate the proposed $14 billion merger between Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) and Juniper Networks. The antitrust watchdog seeks to determine if the deal, announced in January, would reduce competition in the market. The CMA has set an initial review deadline of August 14.

title: Life science company LB Pharmaceuticals relocates to PENN 1
summary: JLL announced that LB Pharmaceuticals Inc, a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, has signed a new lease to relocate and expand its administrative and executive offices to PENN 1 in Midtown Manhattan. The move to the 10th floor of PENN 1, a 55-story tower redeveloped by Vornado Realty Trust, will take place in the third quarter of 2024. This relocation positions LB Pharma within THE PENN DISTRICT, an area rapidly transforming with new transportation, public spaces, and diverse businesses, including a growing life science sector.

title: Progress Software elevates severity of new MOVEit bug to ‘critical’ as exploit attempts jump
summary: Progress Software has elevated a new MOVEit vulnerability (CVE-2024-5806) to "critical" severity, urging customers to patch the issue due to increased exploitation attempts. The bug, resembling a previous vulnerability, allows hackers to access and manipulate data on MOVEit Transfer servers, leading to widespread concern among cybersecurity researchers and government agencies. This comes after a 2023 incident involving a similar MOVEit vulnerability resulted in massive data theft affecting millions and numerous lawsuits against Progress Software.

title: What Exactly Does NVIDIA Do, and Why Should You Care?
summary: This article provides an introduction to NVIDIA, highlighting its transformation from a GPU manufacturer to a systems company offering hardware and an ecosystem for developers. It delves into the factors driving NVIDIA's success, its significant impact on the customer experience market through AI, and key partnerships with companies like Cisco, SAP, and ServiceNow. The piece emphasizes NVIDIA's role in advancing AI for personalized and intelligent customer interactions across various sectors.

title: 12 Common Concerns of the Permeable IoT Attack Surface
summary: This article outlines 12 common security concerns associated with the expanding Internet of Things (IoT) attack surface. It highlights issues such as limited device updates, lack of standardization, data privacy, and supply chain vulnerabilities, emphasizing the unique challenges IoT presents compared to traditional systems. The author advocates for a DevSecOps approach to embed security from the initial stages of development and operations, using automation to address the rapidly growing and diverse IoT ecosystem.

title: GoDaddy Elects New Board Member
summary: GoDaddy Inc. announced the unanimous election of Graham Smith as an independent director to its board, effective June 26, 2024. Smith brings 30 years of experience in leadership, operational finance, and board service from leading technology companies, including Salesforce.com, Inc. and Splunk Inc. He will serve as a Class I director and join the board's Audit and Finance Committee.

title: Cisco Nexus devices zero day raises alarms despite CVSS score
summary: A suspected Chinese state-sponsored actor, dubbed Velvet Ant, is actively exploiting a zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2024-20399) in Cisco NX-OS software despite its moderate CVSS score of 6.0. The actor is deploying custom malware to gain root access and persistent control over Cisco Nexus devices, which are crucial backbone switches for data centers. Cisco has begun releasing software updates, and the vulnerability has been added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

title: Korn Ferry: 'perfect storm' creating 'new unemployables'
summary: A Korn Ferry report warns that a "perfect storm" of AI, recession-proofing strategies, and talent hoarding is creating a new class of "unemployable" young, skilled individuals. Many Gen Zers, despite being well-qualified and eager to work, struggle to find jobs due to firms halting hiring during the pandemic and now adopting a "wait and watch" approach concerning AI's impact on workloads. The report suggests employers are retaining existing talent and expecting increased efficiency rather than hiring new staff, highlighting the difficult job market for recent graduates.

title: Navigating the Digital Transformation in India's Construction Industry
summary: India's construction industry is experiencing rapid growth, projected to increase by 17% in the next year, driven by significant investment in digital technologies. Indian businesses are leading in technology adoption, outperforming other markets, and actively upskilling their workforce to overcome digital skills gaps, particularly in AI and machine learning. Despite challenges like high costs and lack of digital skills, the industry is embracing digital transformation, with AI poised to become a foundational technology, promising substantial returns for those who implement comprehensive solutions.

title: Cisco and HTX sign MOU to Pilot 5G and AI Technologies to Enhance Homeland Security
summary: Cisco and Singapore’s HTX (Home Team Science and Technology Agency) have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to collaborate on piloting 5G and AI technologies. This partnership aims to digitally transform public safety, security, and network operations in Singapore. The collaboration will leverage Cisco’s 5G-as-a-Service solution and involve building a customized AI-driven Security and Network Operations (AISecOps) platform for HTX.

title: In Hiroshima, a call for peaceful, ethical AI
summary: Cisco participated in the AI Ethics for Peace conference in Hiroshima, advocating for the responsible and ethical development of artificial intelligence. Dave West, Cisco's president for APJC, highlighted Cisco's commitment to building AI infrastructure, security, and data insights on a foundation of trust and responsible AI principles. The conference aimed to extend the Rome Call for AI Ethics, emphasizing collective effort to ensure AI serves positive human advancement.

title: OpenAI, Nvidia, Google and others launch AI cybersecurity consortium
summary: A new industry group, the Coalition for Secure AI (CoSAI), has been launched by major tech firms including OpenAI, Nvidia, and Google, under the umbrella of OASIS. Its mission is to enhance the security of AI applications through the development of tools, technical guidance, and the sharing of cybersecurity best practices. CoSAI will initially focus on three open-source workstreams: scanning for AI cybersecurity risks, mitigating those risks, and addressing software supply chain vulnerabilities related to AI.

title: How to neutralise a web DDoS tsunami attack
summary: Organisations face a new, aggressive type of DDoS attack called the Web DDoS Tsunami, which targets Layer 7 (L7) and can reach millions of requests per second, mimicking legitimate traffic. Traditional defense tools like network-based DDoS protection and WAFs are proving ineffective. A proper defense requires AI-driven, behavioral-based L7 solutions that can adapt in real-time, accurately distinguish legitimate from malicious traffic, and protect against advanced threats.

title: Data Center Market size is set to grow by USD 434.8 billionn from 2024-2028, Rise in adoption of multi-cloud and network upgrades to support 5G to boost the market growth, Technavio
summary: The global data center market is projected to grow by USD 434.8 billion from 2024-2028, driven by the increasing adoption of multi-cloud strategies and network upgrades for 5G support. This growth is also fueled by the implementation of AI for enhanced energy efficiency and automation in data centers, despite challenges such as cybersecurity issues. Technavio's report highlights key segments including IT infrastructure, power management, mechanical construction, general construction, and security solutions, with significant growth expected across North America, APAC, Europe, South America, and the Middle East and Africa.

title: New standards to head off quantum cyberthreats
summary: The IT industry is proactively addressing the potential threat of quantum computers cracking current encryption methods by developing new standards. The U.S. Department of Commerce's National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) is set to release standards in July 2024 to protect against quantum cyberthreats, even though a quantum computer capable of this doesn't yet exist. Companies like Cisco are already preparing solutions, such as Postquantum Preshared Keys (PPKs), to secure data in anticipation of this future risk.

title: AI and the Workforce: Industry Report Calls for Reskilling and Upskilling as 92 Percent of Technology Roles Evolve
summary: A report by the AI-Enabled ICT Workforce Consortium, led by Cisco and analyzed by Accenture, reveals that 92% of technology roles will see significant transformation due to AI advancements. The report highlights the critical need for reskilling and upskilling, especially for entry and mid-level professionals, by focusing on essential skills like AI literacy, data analytics, and prompt engineering. Consortium members have committed to training over 95 million people globally in digital and AI skills over the next decade to foster an inclusive, AI-powered workforce.

title: Fenwick Represents Cisco Investments in Groq’s $640M Series D Funding
summary: Fenwick represented Cisco Investments in Groq's $640 million Series D funding round. This significant investment, led by BlackRock Private Equity Partners and including participation from Cisco Investments, will enable Groq to expand its Language Processing Unit deployment and talent pool. The Fenwick transaction team comprised corporate partner Will Black and associates Stephanie Hong and John Clancy.

title: What’s up with… Singtel, DT, HPE/Juniper
summary: This article provides an industry news roundup, highlighting Singtel's launch of commercial quantum-safe networking services in Singapore for enterprises, Deutsche Telekom's strong Q2 financial results, and the UK's CMA clearance for HPE's acquisition of Juniper Networks. It also touches on Amdocs' record quarterly revenues and Vodafone Business's plan to expand its team in Germany to bolster enterprise offerings.

title: Cisco (CSCO) to Cut Thousands of Jobs in New Layoff Round, Reuters Says
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (CSCO) plans a second round of layoffs this year, eliminating thousands more jobs, according to Reuters. This move is part of the networking equipment maker's strategy to shift focus towards faster-growing sectors like cybersecurity and artificial intelligence. This follows previous job cuts earlier this year due to a slowdown in corporate tech spending.

title: Cisco could reportedly let go 4,000+ workers in new round of job cuts
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is reportedly preparing for a new round of layoffs that could affect over 4,000 employees, six months after a similar restructuring cut. These job cuts are attributed to slow demand for networking equipment and macroeconomic uncertainty. Despite a recent drop in networking revenue, Cisco has raised its full-year sales forecast and anticipates growth in fiscal 2025, partly due to its acquisition of Splunk Inc.

title: Bay Area tech giant Cisco to cut nearly 6,000 jobs in shift to AI, cybersecurity
summary: Cisco Systems announced its second major round of layoffs for the year, intending to cut an additional 7% of its global workforce, potentially impacting around 5,900 employees. This move is part of the San Jose-based tech company's strategic shift towards artificial intelligence and cybersecurity. The announcement coincided with their fourth-quarter and fiscal year earnings report, which showed a decline in revenue compared to the previous year.

title: Cisco Systems Inc. Q4 FY2024 Earnings: EPS of $0.54 Beats Estimates, Revenue Hits $13.6 Billion
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. reported Q4 FY2024 revenues of $13.6 billion, exceeding analyst estimates, although it marked a 10% year-over-year decrease. The company's GAAP EPS of $0.54 also surpassed expectations, despite a 44% decline from the previous year. For the full fiscal year 2024, revenue reached $53.8 billion, a 6% decrease, with GAAP EPS at $2.54, down 17% year-over-year.

title: Cisco to Layoff 7% of Global Workforce to Focus on AI and Security
summary: Cisco Systems, Inc. announced a layoff of approximately 7% of its global workforce, impacting about 5,000 employees and costing up to $1 billion. These cuts follow a 10% decrease in fourth-quarter revenues but are reallocating funds to fast-growing areas like AI, cloud, and cybersecurity. The company's recent acquisition of Splunk for $28 billion is set to enhance its product portfolio with AI-powered security tools.

title: Cisco plans to cut 7% of workforce amid AI push
summary: Cisco plans to lay off 7% of its global workforce, approximately 5,600 employees, as part of a restructuring to invest in key growth areas like artificial intelligence, cloud, and cybersecurity. CFO Scott Herren stated this is a reallocation of resources rather than a cost-cutting measure. The company anticipates up to $1 billion in restructuring costs and reported a 10% decline in revenues for its fiscal 2024 fourth quarter.

title: Pinnacle is One of America's Top Companies That Care
summary: For the fifth time, Pinnacle Financial Partners has been named one of PEOPLE magazine’s 100 Companies That Care, an honor awarded jointly by Great Place to Work® and PEOPLE. This recognition highlights Pinnacle's commitment to associate well-being, community impact, and generous company benefits, placing it alongside major international brands. The article details key impact data from 2023, including high associate retention, significant equity compensation, and substantial investments in low-to-moderate-income housing and nonprofits.

title: Cyberattack Hits Major Oil Company Halliburton, Forcing Some Systems Offline
summary: A cyberattack has impacted Halliburton, a major US oil services company, prompting concerns about potential ransomware involvement. Halliburton confirmed the incident in an SEC filing, stating that certain systems were proactively taken offline and an investigation is underway. The incident is drawing comparisons to the 2021 Colonial Pipeline attack, highlighting ongoing cybersecurity vulnerabilities in critical infrastructure.

title: Halliburton cyber-attack forces shutdown of its systems
summary: Halliburton, an energy industry service provider, suffered a cyberattack on August 21, forcing it to shut down its normal systems and programs. The company confirmed it is investigating the unauthorized activity and restoring systems, while the U.S. Department of Energy stated there's no indication of impact on national energy services. This incident highlights the increasing cybersecurity risks for large corporations in critical sectors.

title: Fortifying the future of Security for AI: Cisco Announces intent to acquire Robust Intelligence
summary: Cisco has announced its intent to acquire Robust Intelligence, a company specializing in AI security solutions, to enhance its Cisco Security Cloud offerings. This acquisition aims to strengthen AI application and model protection against various threats like prompt injection and data poisoning. The integration of Robust Intelligence's technology will provide Cisco with unparalleled visibility into AI traffic, driving innovation and simplifying operations for securing AI-driven enterprises.

title: Cisco's Robust Intelligence acquisition boosts AI security
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is acquiring Robust Intelligence Inc., an AI-focused security startup, for an undisclosed sum. This acquisition aims to enhance Cisco's AI security offerings by integrating Robust Intelligence's platform, which protects AI models and data throughout their lifecycle, into Cisco's existing security and networking products. The move aligns with Cisco's strategy to diversify revenue streams into security amid declining revenue from its core networking hardware business and growing enterprise demand for AI investment protection.

title: What is NetApp?
summary: NetApp is a multinational corporation specializing in data storage hardware, software, and cloud services, known for its unified storage offerings and hybrid cloud solutions. The article details NetApp's history, financial performance, product portfolio including prominent storage systems, and key acquisitions. It also touches on a past Department of Justice probe and leadership changes throughout its existence.

title: Cisco owes nearly $66 million in audio-conferencing patent case, jury says
summary: A Texas federal jury ruled that Cisco Systems must pay Paltalk $65.7 million for infringing a patent related to audio conferencing technology used in Cisco's WebEx products. Cisco plans to challenge the verdict. Despite the win, Paltalk's stock was down 37%, as the company expects to receive no more than a third of the gross proceeds from the award.

title: Ninth Circuit paves way for Falun Gong members to sue Cisco for aiding and abetting torture in China
summary: A Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals panel denied an en banc rehearing for a class action lawsuit against Cisco Systems by Falun Gong members. The plaintiffs allege Cisco developed surveillance software, Golden Shield, for the Chinese Communist Party, which was used to track and persecute Falun Gong members, thus aiding and abetting human rights violations. The panel upheld a precedent allowing the suit under the Alien Tort Statute, rejecting arguments about foreign policy concerns and judicial overreach raised by dissenting judges.

title: Cisco Networking Academy unveils new unified teaching and learning experience on NetAcad.com
summary: Cisco Networking Academy has launched a new unified teaching and learning experience on NetAcad.com, integrating the offerings of NetAcad.com and SkillsForAll.com. This new platform provides a modern digital experience with over 50 courses, available in multiple languages and designed for mobile-first, instructor-led, self-paced, and hybrid learning options. The initiative aims to scale access to quality IT education in key areas like cybersecurity, AI, and programming, fostering an inclusive future by empowering learners with in-demand skills.

title: Exclusive: Vista and Blackstone in talks to acquire software maker Smartsheet, sources say
summary: A private equity consortium, including Vista Equity Partners and Blackstone, is in talks to acquire Smartsheet, a U.S. workplace collaboration software maker with a market value of about $7 billion. A deal could be signed in the coming weeks, and Smartsheet's shares jumped nearly 10% on the news. This potential acquisition highlights a resurgence in private equity dealmaking, especially in technology, following high interest rates in 2023.

title: 3 AI Stocks to Scoop Up Instead of Nvidia
summary: JPMorgan analysts have identified three AI stocks—Coherent (COHR), Lumentum (LITE), and Fabrinet (FN)—that are poised to benefit from the surging demand for AI products, despite Nvidia's recent flat performance. These companies are key component suppliers to Nvidia and other major tech firms, playing crucial roles in optical instruments, silicon carbide materials, and advanced photonic products. The article highlights their strong financial performance, strategic positioning within the AI supply chain, and positive analyst ratings, suggesting them as compelling alternatives for investors interested in the AI sector.

title: Apple supplier Jabil to set up Rs 2,000 crore mfg unit in Tiruchirapalli
summary: Apple supplier Jabil Inc. is investing Rs 2,000 crore to establish an electronics manufacturing facility in Tiruchirappalli, India, creating 5,000 jobs. This move signifies the expanding Apple supplier ecosystem in India, with Tamil Nadu emerging as a prominent electronics manufacturing hub. The investment is part of India's "China Plus One" strategy, drawing global players to diversify their manufacturing bases.

title: Apple's supplier Jabil to invest ₹2,000 cr in Tamil Nadu, aims to create 5,000 jobs
summary: Apple's contract manufacturer Jabil Inc. is set to invest ₹2,000 crore in a new electronics manufacturing facility in Tiruchirapalli, Tamil Nadu, expecting to create 5,000 jobs. This makes Jabil the fourth Apple supplier in the state, joining Foxconn, Pegatron, and Tata Electronics, and was finalized during Tamil Nadu CM M.K. Stalin's U.S. visit. The investment contributes to Tamil Nadu's growing electronics exports, largely driven by iPhone shipments, positioning the state as India's electronics hub.

title: Jabil Inc to invest Rs 2,000 crore in Trichy
summary: Jabil Inc, a key supplier to Apple and others, is set to invest Rs 2,000 crore in Trichy, Tamil Nadu. This significant investment is expected to create 5,000 jobs and foster a new electronics manufacturing cluster in the region, as announced by Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin.

title: Cisco Networking Academy Unveils New Unified Teaching and Learning Experience on NetAcad.com
summary: Cisco Networking Academy has launched a new unified teaching and learning platform on NetAcad.com, integrating content from both NetAcad.com and SkillsForAll.com. This new web experience offers over 50 courses in up to 18 languages, covering key technology topics with flexible learning options and a mobile-first design. The initiative aims to scale access to quality IT education globally and empower learners with in-demand skills.

title: Jabil Inc to pour Rs 2,000 Crore into new electronics manufacturing plant in Tiruchi
summary: Jabil Inc is committing Rs 2,000 crore to establish a new electronics manufacturing facility in Tiruchi, Tamil Nadu, a strategic investment expected to create approximately 5,000 jobs. This move is part of broader industrial development efforts in the region, with Jabil Inc. and Rockwell Automation collectively investing Rs 2,666 crore. The expansion reinforces Tamil Nadu's growing prominence as an electronics manufacturing hub.

title: Nokia Gains Big with CoreWeave’s AI Networking Deal
summary: Nokia has secured a significant deal with AI-focused cloud provider CoreWeave for its global backbone's optical and IP transport networking. This win strengthens Nokia's position in the competitive AI networking market, highlighting its ability to provide high performance and energy efficiency. The partnership is crucial for Nokia's credibility given CoreWeave's leadership in GPU-driven AI workloads and substantial funding.

title: USA: Court of Appeals confirms Cisco Systems can face liability for providing surveillance technology used to facilitate human rights abuses
summary: A U.S. Court of Appeals has affirmed its 2023 decision that Cisco Systems can face liability for allegedly providing surveillance technology used to facilitate human rights abuses, specifically referencing its role in China's "Great Firewall" and targeting of the Falun Gong. This decision allows claims of aiding and abetting against technology companies to move forward, though Cisco plans to appeal to the U.S. Supreme Court. The ruling has implications for other tech companies like Google and Amazon regarding their potential liability for the misuse of their services.

title: IBM, Cisco layoffs to cut nearly 200 San Francisco workers | Technology
summary: Cisco and IBM are together laying off nearly 200 workers in San Francisco. Cisco is cutting 134 people as part of a larger layoff of 7% of its workforce, while IBM is eliminating 58 positions in San Francisco and 54 in San Jose. These cuts are part of a broader trend of tech industry layoffs that began in late 2022.

title: Cisco to lay off hundreds of Bay Area employees in second round of cuts this year
summary: Cisco Systems has announced a second round of layoffs affecting over 800 Bay Area employees, part of a larger global workforce reduction of 5,900 as the company shifts focus to AI and cybersecurity. These cuts, effective November 15, follow an earlier round of 4,200 layoffs this year. The announcement coincided with a fourth-quarter revenue decrease of 10% and an annual yield drop of 6%.

title: Cisco layoffs to impact hundreds of Bay Area workers
summary: Cisco Systems, Inc. is set to lay off hundreds of employees across its Bay Area offices in San Francisco, Milpitas, and San Jose, with the job cuts totaling 842 people. These permanent layoffs will take effect on November 15. IBM also announced permanent layoffs this week, impacting 112 employees in its San Francisco and San Jose facilities, effective November 18.

title: Cisco to crunch five Bay Area offices into San Jose’s Santana Row
summary: Cisco Systems is consolidating its Bay Area office footprint by closing five offices, including four in North San Jose and Splunk's San Francisco headquarters. Workers will be moved to a Splunk office at 3098 Olsen Drive in San Jose's Santana Row, a move intended to enhance collaboration, improve cost efficiencies, and drive innovation. This consolidation follows Cisco's recent decision to lay off 7% of its global workforce.

title: 5 Things to Know About ConnectWise’s New CEO Manny Rivelo
summary: ConnectWise has appointed Manny Rivelo as its new CEO, succeeding Jason Magee, following the company's acquisition by Thoma Bravo. This appointment comes as ConnectWise approaches a potential recapitalization or sale by Thoma Bravo. Rivelo's background, particularly his experience as CEO of cybersecurity vendor Forcepoint and his ties to Francisco Partners, suggests a possible strategic focus on cybersecurity for ConnectWise's future.

title: Safe and trustworthy AI is a shared responsibility
summary: Cisco Executive Vice President Dev Stahlkopf emphasizes that safe and trustworthy AI is a shared responsibility between public and private sectors. Cisco supports initiatives like the EU AI Pact, highlighting their commitment to responsible AI through established principles, a robust impact assessment process, and active participation in global AI frameworks. The company also focuses on AI literacy and workforce development, aiming to train millions in digital skills by 2032.

title: Talos discovers denial-of-service vulnerability in Microsoft Audio Bus; Potential remote code execution in popular open-source PLC
summary: Cisco Talos' Vulnerability Research team recently disclosed critical vulnerabilities, including two in Microsoft products and three in OpenPLC. The Microsoft vulnerabilities involve a denial-of-service in the High-Definition Audio Bus Driver and a memory corruption issue in the Pragmatic General Multicast server, both of which have been patched. The OpenPLC vulnerabilities, one of which could lead to remote code execution, also resulted in denial-of-service risks through crafted Ethernet/IP requests.

title: The 6 Hottest AI Networking Tools On The Market Right Now
summary: This article highlights six trending AI networking tools designed to simplify network operations, enhance efficiency, and reduce costs for IT teams and channel partners. It details offerings from major players like Arista, Cisco, HPE Aruba, Juniper, Nile, and Riverbed, showcasing their AI-driven platforms for network design, monitoring, and management. These tools aim to improve network performance, automate tasks, and provide deeper insights across various IT environments.

title: Shipping zero trust to global ports
summary: Given the critical role global ports play in the economy and their increasing vulnerability to cyberattacks, security for their IT infrastructure, especially IoT and OT devices, is paramount. Recent incidents like the Australian port breach and the discovery of unexpected communications equipment in cranes highlight the urgent need for enhanced security measures. Adopting zero trust principles can help secure these crucial systems by improving device visibility, enabling secure communication, and managing third-party access, thereby safeguarding against disruptions and espionage.

title: Ingram Micro IPO, Revenue Details Go Live
summary: Ingram Micro has filed its registration statement with the SEC for a proposed initial public offering, with its common stock set to trade under the symbol "INGM" on the New York Stock Exchange. The filing reveals that Ingram Micro's net sales have declined over the past three years, from $54.4 billion in 2021 to $48 billion in 2023. Platinum Equity, which owns Ingram, will sell the common stock through its entity Imola JV Holdings, with the number of available shares and price range yet to be determined.

title: Patent troll Sable pays up, dedicates all its patents to the public!
summary: Cloudflare successfully defeated patent troll Sable IP and Sable Networks in a three-year legal battle, culminating in a jury verdict finding Cloudflare did not infringe and Sable's asserted patent claim was invalid. As a result, Sable agreed to pay Cloudflare $225,000, grant a royalty-free license to its entire patent portfolio, and dedicate all its patents to the public, preventing future assertions. This outcome validates Cloudflare's Project Jengo initiative, which encourages public participation in finding prior art to fight patent trolls, with additional prizes to be awarded.

title: Cisco Nears Investment in CoreWeave at $23 Billion Valuation
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is set to invest in CoreWeave, a prominent cloud-computing provider in the AI sector, as part of a transaction valuing CoreWeave at $23 billion. This investment is tied to a secondary transaction that involves existing shareholders, such as employees, tendering $400 million to $500 million of their holdings. The deal is reportedly nearing completion.

title: Cisco is reportedly close to investing in Nvidia-backed AI startup CoreWeave
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is reportedly nearing an investment in the AI startup CoreWeave, a deal that would value the cloud-computing provider at $23 billion. CoreWeave, already backed by Nvidia and other significant investors, is known for its early adoption of Nvidia's graphics chips for data centers. This investment highlights Cisco's strategic interest in the burgeoning artificial intelligence sector.

title: Opinion: Why Cisco is looking at investing in CoreWeave, a hot AI startup
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is reportedly planning a significant investment in CoreWeave, a prominent AI startup. This move aims to strengthen Cisco's position in the AI data center market. CoreWeave specializes in cloud services tailored for artificial intelligence and machine learning.

title: Ciena Stock Powers the AI Cloud Boom—Don’t Miss Out
summary: Ciena is positioned as a key player in the AI and cloud infrastructure boom due to its high-bandwidth and low-latency connectivity solutions, essential for AI deployment. Despite a weak Q3 2024, underlying signs of recovery are evident, driven by cloud providers upgrading their networks. The company also announced a significant $1 billion stock buyback program, indicating confidence in future performance.

title: Paltalk awarded $65.7 million in Cisco patent case
summary: Paltalk has been awarded $65.7 million in a patent infringement lawsuit against Cisco Systems, Inc. by a District Court in Texas. While this represents a significant financial victory for Paltalk, the net proceeds will be substantially reduced by litigation expenses, potentially to less than one-third of the gross award. The company, known for its various multimedia communication applications, also reported a significant revenue decrease and net losses in Q2 but is pursuing strategic acquisitions and exploring other opportunities.

title: Intel Broker Claims Cisco Breach, Selling Stolen Data from Major Firms
summary: Notorious hacker Intel Broker claims to have breached Cisco Systems, Inc., stealing source codes, confidential documents, and credentials from various global firms including Verizon, AT&T, and Microsoft. The stolen data is reportedly for sale on Breach Forums using Monero (XMR). This alleged breach, if confirmed, highlights significant cybersecurity risks for major organizations, following Intel Broker's history of high-profile data breaches.

title: Innovating an energy future that’s smart, secure, and more sustainable
summary: Cisco is helping utilities modernize the energy grid to be smarter, more secure, and sustainable, addressing challenges like aging infrastructure, cyberthreats, and the complexities of renewable energy. The article highlights how digital technologies, networking, IoT, and AI are crucial for grid resilience, security, and the transition to clean energy, showcasing examples from CPFL Energia and ScottishPower Renewables. Cisco's end-to-end portfolio and security expertise are positioned as key differentiators in enabling this transformation.

title: Data Breach Investigation on Cisco
summary: CYFIRMA's investigation revealed a significant data leak at Cisco involving the threat actor "IntelBroker," who claimed responsibility for compromising sensitive data including GitHub and GitLab projects, source code, hardcoded credentials, and customer information. This incident poses substantial security, reputational, and supply chain risks to Cisco and its B2B clients, as evidence suggests continued access despite Cisco's security measures. The breach also impacted numerous B2B clients, with potential exposure of their production source codes, and highlights the ongoing threat from the CyberNiggers hacking group.

title: Cisco Confirms Security Incident After Hacker Offers to Sell Data
summary: Cisco has confirmed a security incident after a hacker, 'IntelBroker', offered to sell data allegedly stolen from the company. The networking giant stated that a small number of unauthorized files were published from a public-facing DevHub environment, but its core systems were not breached. The company is investigating but has not found sensitive PII or financial data among the stolen files.

title: IntelBroker’s Alleged Cisco Breach: A Deep Dive into the Claims and Responses
summary: This article details IntelBroker's alleged breach of Cisco Systems, tracking the claims made by the threat actor and Cisco's responses. It covers IntelBroker's assertions of extensive data exfiltration, Cisco's initial denials and subsequent confirmations of a data leak from a public-facing DevHub, and the ongoing dispute over the scope and sensitivity of the compromised data. The article also provides background on IntelBroker's history as a prominent cybercriminal and BreachForums administrator.

title: Huawei, Juniper stick out of Gartner’s dwindling SD-WAN competitor pool
summary: Gartner's latest SD-WAN Magic Quadrant report reveals a consolidating market, with a consistent top six leaders and fewer tertiary players. Huawei and Juniper Networks are highlighted for their unique positions outside the leader quadrant; Huawei for its strong sales and market share despite geopolitical limitations, and Juniper for its visionary AI-powered SD-WAN services despite a smaller customer base. The report also notes the increasing importance of SASE strategies and the continued consolidation in the market.

title: 3 Key Reasons to Buy Fortinet Stock Beyond the 45% Surge in 3 Months
summary: Fortinet (FTNT) has seen a 45% stock surge in three months due to its strong market position, financial performance, and innovation in cybersecurity, including AI integration. The company's efforts to address the cybersecurity skills gap through training programs also contribute to its attractive investment profile. Despite a premium valuation and competition from companies like Palo Alto Networks and Cisco Systems, Fortinet is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for cybersecurity solutions.

title: Cisco Unveils New AI Innovations to Amplify the Employee Experience
summary: Cisco has introduced new AI-powered solutions at WebexOne to enhance collaboration and boost employee productivity. These innovations include Cisco Spatial Meetings for immersive 3D experiences, Ceiling Microphone Pro with AI-driven adjustments, and new Cisco AI Assistant features for workflow automation and content generation. The goal is to create equitable and exceptional experiences in hybrid work environments and simplify management for IT teams.

title: From the Golden Shield to the Sword: The Expansion of the Alien Tort Statute to Reach U.S. Companies as Aiders and Abettors of Foreign Wrongs
summary: This article discusses the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit's denial of rehearing en banc in Doe v. Cisco Systems, a case with significant implications for U.S. companies under the Alien Tort Statute (ATS). The case involves claims that Cisco Systems aided and abetted human rights abuses by the Chinese government, and the article analyzes the original panel opinion, the denial of rehearing, and the potential for a Supreme Court appeal. It also offers best practices for U.S. companies operating with foreign nations in light of these developments.

title: How AI Will Help Empower SMB Cybersecurity
summary: This article discusses how Artificial Intelligence (AI) can significantly enhance cybersecurity for Small and Medium Businesses (SMBs). It highlights AI's ability to identify vulnerabilities, predict and prevent cyberattacks using behavioral analytics, accelerate incident response, improve authentication methods, and detect advanced malware more effectively than traditional methods. The author emphasizes AI's affordability and efficiency, making it an ideal solution for SMBs often targeted by cyberattacks.

title: Cisco Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ:CSCO) Investors Are Less Pessimistic Than Expected
summary: Cisco Systems' current P/E ratio of 21.6x is higher than the market average in the U.S., despite the company's recent sluggish earnings performance, which includes a 17% decline in EPS last year. Analysts predict a 7.2% annual earnings growth for Cisco over the next three years, which is weaker than the market's expected 10% growth. This high P/E, coupled with a less optimistic growth outlook, suggests that investors may be overly optimistic, potentially setting themselves up for disappointment if the P/E adjusts to align with future growth prospects.

title: Activist Investor Legion Pushes for Changes at Five9
summary: Activist investor Legion Partners has increased its stake in Five9 and is pushing for a board seat and cost-cutting measures, following a similar move by Anson Funds Management. This comes after Five9's stock dropped 60 percent since the beginning of the year, despite previously achieving strong year-over-year revenue growth. Five9 had already announced layoffs impacting seven percent of its workforce in July to drive shareholder value, but now faces pressure from new investors amidst other Five9 developments, including the introduction of its "Genius AI."

title: Ultra Accelerator Link Consortium Incorporates; Announces Membership Opportunity
summary: The Ultra Accelerator Link (UALink) Consortium has officially incorporated and is inviting companies to join as Contributor members. Formed by major tech companies like AMD, AWS, Intel, and Microsoft, the consortium aims to define a high-speed, low-latency interconnect for AI accelerators in data centers. The UALink 1.0 specification, enabling up to 200Gbps per lane for up to 1024 accelerators, will be made available to members this year and released for general review in Q1 2025.

title: Networks for AI, with AI
summary: This article discusses the critical role of robust communication networks in enabling AI applications and the transformative impact of AI on network design, architecture, and operations. It highlights how AI-powered networks can meet the demands of AI applications by providing low latency, high reliability, and enhanced security, while also simplifying operations, optimizing performance, and reducing energy consumption. Nokia emphasizes its leadership in providing end-to-end network technology to build AI-optimized and autonomous networks.

title: Sify partners with Cisco for enterprise digital transformation
summary: Sify Technologies has partnered with Cisco ThousandEyes to enhance digital experience for enterprises through improved network observability and performance. This collaboration combines Sify's managed services with Cisco's advanced networking capabilities, providing detailed insights into application performance and user experience. Sify also announced significant investments in data center expansion and previous partnerships with NVIDIA and Ciena to bolster its infrastructure.

title: The ISAC advantage for collective threat intelligence
summary: Ben Corll argues that Information Sharing and Analysis Centers (ISACs) are crucial for cybersecurity in an era of increasing cyberthreat sophistication. He highlights the advantages of ISACs, such as real-time industry-specific threat intelligence, confidential information sharing, and augmented regulatory compliance. Despite challenges like information overload and cost, active participation in ISACs strengthens collective defenses across various sectors.

summary: A recent CIO Cyber Cashe livestream event discussed the significant impact of technology, particularly AI tools, on investment management operations. Experts highlighted how these tools can automate low-level tasks, freeing up analysts for deeper insights, while also addressing the growing threat of cyber incidents like ransomware. The discussion covered both the benefits of AI in portfolio management and the importance of due diligence when working with tech providers, especially regarding data security.

title: 12 advantages and disadvantages of video conferencing
summary: Video conferencing offers numerous benefits, such as increased savings by reducing travel, enhanced employee productivity through remote work flexibility, and valuable features like meeting transcription and translation services. However, it also presents challenges including a lack of personal interaction, potential network connectivity issues, security concerns, and complexities in complying with data governance and regulatory requirements for newly generated content. Organizations must weigh these pros and cons to effectively integrate video conferencing into their operations while addressing privacy and compliance issues.

title: Observability Wars: Datadog and Dynatrace Vie for Data Dominance
summary: The data observability market is experiencing rapid growth due to the demand for AI and cloud products, with Datadog and Dynatrace emerging as key competitors. Both companies show strong growth potential, with Datadog excelling in customer loyalty and guidance, while Dynatrace offers a more favorable valuation. Despite their differences, both firms operate in a fast-growing industry with significant future prospects.

title: Robust Intelligence, Now Part of Cisco, Recognized as a 2024 Gartner® Cool Vendor™ for AI Security
summary: Robust Intelligence, a recently acquired AI security startup by Cisco, has been recognized in the first-ever 2024 Gartner Cool Vendors for AI Security report. This recognition highlights Robust Intelligence's innovative work, including the creation of the AI Firewall and its comprehensive AI security platform. The article emphasizes the importance of AI security in addressing modern enterprise challenges and showcases Robust Intelligence's offerings integrated into the Cisco Security Cloud.

title: HPE Issues Critical Security Patches for Aruba Access Point Vulnerabilities
summary: HPE has released critical security patches for multiple vulnerabilities affecting Aruba Networking Access Point products. These updates address two critical unauthenticated command injection flaws (CVE-2024-42509 and CVE-2024-47460) with CVSS scores of 9.8 and 9.0 respectively, which could lead to arbitrary code execution. The company advises users to apply patches and implement workarounds like restricting access to management interfaces and enabling cluster security to mitigate risks.

title: Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) Has A Pretty Healthy Balance Sheet
summary: Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) maintains a healthy balance sheet despite an increase in debt to US$31.0b from US$8.43b a year ago, offset by US$18.6b in cash, resulting in net debt of US$12.4b. The company's low debt to EBITDA ratio of 0.81 and strong free cash flow coverage (98% of EBIT) indicate its ability to manage its debt obligations comfortably. While its EBIT declined by 16% over the last year, Cisco Systems is positioned to handle current debt levels effectively.

(NASDAQ: PALT) reported its Q3 2024 earnings, highlighting a strategic merger agreement to acquire New Tech Technology Solutions, which is expected to significantly boost revenue and allow entry into the growing cloud infrastructure and cybersecurity sectors. The company also emphasized its growing involvement in AI infrastructure orders and acquisitions of AI-focused security startups, though analysts remain skeptical about long-term growth strategies.

This performance comes as Cisco shifts focus to cybersecurity and AI-driven products, exemplified by its acquisition of Splunk, to offset a slowdown in its traditional networking business.

title: Cisco Beats Earnings Estimates and Boosts Outlook. Why the Stock Is Falling.

title: Green Data Center (GDC) Market to grow by USD 202.4 Billion from 2024-2028, driven by rising electricity costs and AI redefining the market landscape - Technavio
summary: The global Green Data Center (GDC) market is projected to grow by USD 202.4 billion from 2024-2028, largely driven by increasing electricity costs and the transformative impact of AI. Despite challenges related to the cost of building these centers, the demand for GDCs is accelerating due to digital transformation, environmental regulations, and corporate social responsibility initiatives across various industries.

The piece emphasizes the economic and operational benefits of a standards-based, unified Ethernet infrastructure for AI.

The piece also covers new cybersecurity threats, the near elimination of network downtime due to misconfigurations, and the growing challenge of balancing AI's energy consumption with sustainability goals.

The report highlights a robust cyclical resurgence in critical hardware and networking sectors, with companies like Arista Networks, Cisco, and Amphenol also strategically positioned to capitalize on emerging opportunities.

summary: Cisco Systems announced its intent to acquire SnapAttack, a threat detection and engineering platform provider, to enhance its Splunk business. The acquisition aims to accelerate threat detection strategies, improve enterprise security operations, and attract new security customers hesitant about Splunk Enterprise Security. This marks Cisco's fourth security-related acquisition this year, strengthening its security profile and TDIR solutions.

Cisco highlights its contributions and commitments in each area, advocating for intentional action and collaboration to foster a sustainable future.

The piece concludes by outlining what customers can expect from doing business with Cisco, emphasizing its commitment to innovation, customer support, and environmental responsibility.

The Chief Minister also highlighted the first semiconductor project in Mysuru as a significant milestone for the state.

summary: Jim Cramer has advised investors against aggressively buying Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO) stock if it's trading above $50, despite recent positive earnings and a 30% gain since its Q4 earnings report. Cramer also expressed skepticism about the recent surge in quantum computing stocks, drawing parallels to non-fungible tokens, and criticized the Federal Reserve's recent interest rate cut given the underlying economic data. He suggests that 2025 might be a year to focus on "cult stocks" that defy conventional valuation methods.

His leadership transformed Tenable into a global leader in exposure management, and his contributions to the cybersecurity field are described as indelible.

title: How Akamai regained control of its runaway cloud bills
summary: Akamai, a technology company, significantly reduced its cloud spending by 40% in the first year of "Project Cirrus" after realizing its cloud bills had ballooned to hundreds of millions due to organic growth and acquisitions. The project involved repatriating some workloads to Akamai's acquired Linode cloud platform, reconfiguring others, and addressing inefficiencies like overprovisioning and a lack of oversight. Akamai's CIO, Kate Prouty, emphasized the importance of a strategic overhaul, educating engineers on cost stewardship, and making more deliberate choices for cloud workloads based on business outcomes.

summary: Marquis Who's Who has recognized Prasanth Kalapatapu, a senior product leader at Cisco Systems Inc., for his significant contributions to information technology. His extensive career, education, and commitment to continuous learning, including recent AI certifications, highlight his expertise in the tech industry.

title: Fortinet Warns of New Zero-Day Used in Attacks on Firewalls with Exposed Interfaces
summary: Fortinet has issued a warning about a new zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2024-55591) actively being exploited in attacks targeting FortiGate firewalls with management interfaces exposed to the public internet. The campaign, which began in mid-November 2024, allows attackers to gain super-admin privileges, create new accounts, and extract credentials using DCSync via crafted requests to the Node.js websocket module. Organizations are urged to apply patches, avoid exposing firewalls to the internet, and monitor networks for unusual activity.

title: Cisco has a plan to stop AI from going rogue

This software aims to automatically detect when staff use AI applications, test machine learning models for vulnerabilities, and plug security gaps. Cisco believes its security expertise positions it uniquely to address the reluctance of businesses to adopt generative AI due to safety concerns.

title: New Cisco AI Defense solution safeguards AI applications and data

The service addresses the growing security challenges of AI adoption, particularly with in-house model training and proprietary data, by providing tools for secure AI application development and access control. Available in March, AI Defense aims to offer a unified layer of safety across multi-model environments, adapting to evolving threats using machine learning and Cisco Talos threat intelligence.

title: HPE Investigates After Alleged Data Breach
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is investigating claims by the cyberattack group IntelBroker of a data breach, which allegedly involved access to HPE's API, GitHub repositories, and source code. This is not the first time HPE has faced such incidents, with previous breaches in 2018 and 2021. While HPE has activated its cyber-response protocols and says there's no evidence of customer information involvement or operational impact, the validity and scope of the claims are still under investigation.
