The author also outlines future blog posts that will delve into best practices and Cisco's role in assisting customers with their IPv6 transitions.

title: U.S. Sues to Block Tech Deal in First Antitrust Action of Trump Term (Published 2025)
summary: The Justice Department has moved to block Hewlett Packard Enterprise's (HPE) $14 billion acquisition of Juniper Networks, marking the first antitrust challenge of President Trump's second term. The lawsuit argues that the deal would substantially lessen competition in the wireless networking industry, despite expectations of a lighter regulatory touch from the new administration. This action suggests a potential continuity in antitrust enforcement despite the change in presidential administrations.

title: US DOJ sues to block Hewlett Packard Enterprise's $14 billion Juniper deal
summary: The US Department of Justice has filed a lawsuit to block Hewlett Packard Enterprise's (HPE) proposed $14 billion acquisition of Juniper Networks, citing concerns about stifling competition in the networking equipment market. The DOJ argues that the deal would reduce competition to essentially two major players, Cisco Systems and HPE. Both HPE and Juniper Networks stated they would defend the deal, asserting that it would enhance competition against incumbent global players.

title: DOJ Sues To Block HPE’s $14B Acquisition Of Juniper Networks: Five Things To Know
summary: The U.S. Department of Justice has filed a lawsuit to block Hewlett Packard Enterprise's $14 billion acquisition of Juniper Networks, alleging it would reduce competition and innovation in the WLAN market. HPE and Juniper Networks have vowed to fight the lawsuit, calling the DOJ's analysis "fundamentally flawed" and stating the deal would enhance competition. The outcome of this litigation has significant implications for the networking industry, particularly for market leader Cisco.

title: Breakingviews - Trump trustbusters flash a clear M&A signal
summary: Despite expectations for a relaxed merger enforcement environment under a new Trump administration, the Department of Justice has sued to block Hewlett Packard Enterprise's (HPE) $14 billion acquisition of Juniper Networks. This move signals a surprising continuity in antitrust scrutiny, particularly as the case involves an incumbent potentially stifling a growing competitor. The lawsuit suggests that while political leadership changes, the underlying staff and a degree of enforcement consistency may persist, challenging bankers' hopes for an unrestrained M&A landscape.

title: C-RAN Market Size to Hit USD 293.84 Billion by 2035
summary: The global C-RAN market is projected to grow from USD 26.22 billion in 2025 to USD 293.84 billion by 2035, exhibiting a CAGR of 27.34%. This significant growth is driven by the increasing demand for 5G networks, cloud computing, IoT, and smart devices, alongside government initiatives for digital transformation. Asia Pacific currently dominates the market, with North America expected to show the highest CAGR, propelled by advancements in AI and network optimization.

title: Intel Announces Another 58 Layoffs For Folsom Campus
summary: Intel has announced an additional 58 layoffs at its Folsom campus, continuing its cost-cutting measures due to stagnant sales and increased competition. This new round of job cuts, expected by March 31, follows previous significant reductions, with the Folsom workforce decreasing from over 6,000 in 2018 to under 4,000. The company is also planning to sell its 150-acre Folsom campus in a sale-leaseback deal as part of its broader restructuring efforts and aims to cut approximately 15,000 jobs globally.

title: Industrial laser sales aid Fabrinet push
summary: Fabrinet, a contract manufacturer of optical components, reported record sales and earnings, exceeding expectations for the quarter ending December 27. The growth was primarily driven by strong demand for data center optical interconnects and a significant rebound in industrial laser sales. The company is expanding its manufacturing capacity with a new 2 million square-foot facility to support future growth.

title: Dynatrace ups AIOps ante with log analytics refresh
summary: Dynatrace has introduced significant updates to its log analytics and AIOps capabilities, prompting some users to consider consolidating away from competing observability vendors. Key enhancements include an overhauled Logs app, deeper integration with the Problems app for automated root cause analysis, and generative AI features for remediation suggestions within its Davis CoPilot. While these updates offer benefits like faster troubleshooting and potential cost savings, challenges such as migration efforts and concerns around log data ingestion costs and organizational adjustment remain.

title: After Earnings, Is Apple Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar maintains a $200 fair value estimate for Apple, rating its stock as "overvalued" with two stars, despite a "wide" economic moat. The Q1 fiscal earnings showed a 4% revenue rise year-over-year, but iPhone revenue declined 1% while services grew 14%. Morningstar anticipates slowing long-term iPhone growth due to China headwinds and a mature smartphone market, and believes the current valuation reflects overly optimistic future growth expectations.

title: Intermedia COO On Kicking Off 'Record Year' In Sales And The Massive Microsoft Teams Opportunity For Partners
summary: Intermedia COO Jonathan McCormick describes 2025 as another record year for UCaaS product sales, driven by strategic initiatives in 2024. The company is capitalizing on a significant opportunity to integrate its advanced voice capabilities directly into Microsoft Teams, targeting businesses not using Microsoft's PBX for voice. Intermedia is also actively incorporating AI into its platform to offer practical use cases that boost efficiency and improve customer experience for partners and their clients.

title: Cisco Study: CEOs Embrace AI, But Knowledge Gaps Threaten Strategic Decisions and Growth
summary: A new Cisco study reveals that while 97% of CEOs plan to integrate AI and 80% recognize its potential, only 1.7% feel fully prepared. Many CEOs fear that gaps in their understanding of AI and an underinvestment in technology and infrastructure could lead to missed opportunities, competitive losses, and hinder strategic decision-making. To address these concerns, CEOs plan to invest in their people, modernize infrastructure, and strengthen cybersecurity, often relying on trusted partners to future-proof their networks for AI.

title: Cisco Empowers Service Providers to Reimagine Networks for AI
summary: Cisco has introduced new solutions to help service providers deliver AI connectivity by evolving their networks to manage increased data volumes and varieties from AI applications. The company's "Agile Services Networking" architecture provides a blueprint for secure, energy-efficient, high-bandwidth connectivity, leveraging new Silicon One platforms, coherent pluggable optics, and software enhancements. These innovations aim to simplify network complexity, assure service experiences with AI-powered automation, and enable service providers to monetize AI-driven services.

title: XR8 Technology Services add new IT partnerships to its growing portfolio of vendors
summary: Northampton-based XR8 Technology Services has announced new partnerships with global technology leaders Cisco and Zebra Technologies, expanding its vendor portfolio. These collaborations aim to provide local Northamptonshire businesses with critical IT solutions, including Cisco's cybersecurity and Zebra's equipment for manufacturing and logistics. This move reinforces XR8's commitment to delivering world-class IT services and expertise to the local business community.

title: Cisco delivers solid earnings and revenue beat, boosting its stock
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. reported a strong second quarter, exceeding earnings and revenue expectations, driven by demand for AI and the acquisition of Splunk Inc. Despite a decline in net income and challenges in its core networking business, the company provided optimistic guidance for the third quarter and increased its quarterly dividend and stock buyback authorization. Concerns remain about rising costs and the long-term impact of Splunk on overall growth.

title: Data Center Networking Software Market: USD 18.2 billion
summary: The global Data Center Networking Software market is projected to reach USD 18.2 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 7.9% from 2025 to 2031, driven by technological advancements, expanding applications across end-use sectors, favorable government policies, and increased investment in R&D. Key players include Cisco Systems Inc., Juniper Networks Inc., and Arista Networks Inc. The market is segmented by software type, deployment type, end-user industry, and application, with Asia-Pacific expected to show the highest growth.

title: Home Wi-Fi Router Market to Grow by USD 3.34 Billion (2025-2029), Driven by E-Learning Growth, with AI Driving Market Transformation - Technavio
summary: The global home Wi-Fi router market is projected to grow by USD 3.34 billion between 2025 and 2029, with a CAGR of 12.5%. This growth is primarily fueled by the expansion of e-learning and strategic partnerships between telecom providers and router manufacturers. However, challenges such as inadequate network infrastructure in developing countries, particularly the lack of 5G technology, could hinder market expansion. AI is also expected to play a key role in transforming this market.

title: Cisco shares climb as network gearmaker raises forecast, eases tariff worries
summary: Cisco Systems' shares rose 6% after the company increased its annual revenue forecast, driven by strong AI-related demand for its networking gear. The company also alleviated investor concerns about the impact of U.S. tariffs, citing a "negligible impact" due to its diversified global supply chain. Despite a forecasted slight decline in gross margins due to tariff costs, analysts raised price targets for the stock, though potential budget cuts from the U.S. government pose future uncertainty.

title: Five thoughts from Cisco’s second-quarter earnings
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. reported a strong fiscal second quarter, with its stock trading at an all-time high. Key takeaways include the significant growth in security product orders, the successful implementation of a "platform effect" strategy championed by CPO Jeetu Patel, and the increasing role of AI as a catalyst for growth within Cisco's product lines. The acquisition of Splunk is proving valuable beyond just revenue, integrating deeply across Cisco's offerings, though the company's valuation still reflects its hardware roots despite strong software metrics.

title: HPE hits back at DOJ move to block Juniper deal, says Huawei could benefit
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is challenging the U.S. Department of Justice's (DOJ) attempt to block its $14 billion acquisition of Juniper Networks. HPE argues that blocking the deal would inadvertently benefit both Cisco and Huawei, potentially harming U.S. efforts to reduce reliance on Chinese technology. The company asserts that the DOJ's complaint overlooks the acquisition's benefits and the competitive nature of the wireless networking market.

title: Check Point Software Announces Plans to Expand Global R&D Footprint with First Asia-Pacific Research ...
summary: Check Point Software is expanding its global R&D footprint by establishing its first Asia-Pacific Research and Development Centre in Bengaluru, India. This strategic move aims to leverage India's tech talent to drive innovation in cybersecurity solutions, focusing on core product lines like Quantum network security and Secure Access Service Edge (SASE). The new center will complement Check Point's primary R&D hub in Tel Aviv and will initially house over 100 engineers, with Rajeev Koripalli appointed as Vice President of Research & Development, India.

title: Arista Networks’ growth accelerates, driven by big AI investments
summary: Arista Networks Inc. reported a significant increase in profit and revenue for its fourth quarter, driven by strong demand for advanced networking infrastructure supporting AI workloads. The company beat Wall Street estimates for both profit and revenue, with CEO Jayshree Ullal highlighting fiscal 2024 as a "remarkable year." Despite a conservative forecast for the current quarter which caused a temporary stock dip, analysts remain optimistic about Arista's long-term prospects due to continued AI investments from major hyperscale customers and effective cost management.

title: Cisco unveils software-driven manufacturing, partners with Rockwell Automation
summary: Cisco has launched software-defined manufacturing and partnered with Rockwell Automation to introduce a 'Digital Skills for Industry' skilling program in India. This initiative aims to address the significant skills gap in the Indian manufacturing sector by providing essential digital skills in areas like cybersecurity, IoT, AI, and automation. The program is crucial for India to achieve its goal of exporting $1 trillion worth of goods by 2030 and to maintain global competitiveness.

title: Top 10 Tech Companies to Work for in San Jose in 2025
summary: San Jose is identified as a leading tech hub in 2025, boasting over 6,600 tech companies and an annual tech GDP of $275 billion, with competitive salaries ranging from $140,000 to $200,000. The article highlights top employers like Adobe, Cisco, and Zoom, emphasizing their innovation in AI, green technology, and cybersecurity, alongside strong company cultures. It details how these companies foster career growth, employee satisfaction, and significant industry impact, making San Jose an attractive destination for tech professionals.

title: Live Nation Entertainment Reports Full Year And Fourth Quarter 2024 Results
summary: Live Nation Entertainment reported robust financial results for its full year and fourth quarter 2024, driven by record fan demand for live music. The company highlighted significant growth in concerts, ticketing, and sponsorship segments, alongside strategic investments in venue expansion and artist support, projecting continued double-digit operating income growth for 2025.

title: Cisco Wins “Industrial IoT Company of the Year 2025” Award from IoT Breakthrough Organization
summary: Cisco was named "Industrial IoT Company of the Year 2025" by IoT Breakthrough for its contributions in industrial cybersecurity, AI readiness, and validated design guides. The award recognizes Cisco's long-standing impact in digitizing industrial operations through robust security measures akin to enterprise solutions, and its commitment to enabling AI/ML applications in industrial settings. Cisco's partnerships and comprehensive design guides further support successful networking and security deployments across various industries.

title: Ooma vs Google Voice: Which VoIP System is Superior?
summary: This article compares Ooma and Google Voice, concluding that Ooma is generally superior due to its features, customer support, and integrations, although Google Voice is more affordable for small teams and offers better security. The comparison details pricing, features, integrations, security, customer support, and international calling rates to help businesses choose the best VoIP system for their needs.

title: PLDT Expands International Capacity with Apricot Cable System
summary: PLDT, Inc. has completed the landing of the Apricot submarine cable system in Baler, Aurora, and Digos, Davao, significantly increasing its international data capacity by 33% to over 140 Tbps. This new 12,000-kilometer cable system enhances network resilience and supports the growing demand for digital services across the Asia Pacific and the U.S., strategically positioning the Philippines as a digital hub. The system's routing avoids earthquake-prone areas and establishes direct connections, making it a critical intra-Asia hub for OTT service providers and vital for supporting next-generation technologies.

title: LVHD: Seeking Low Volatility In High Dividends With Average Result
summary: The Franklin U.S. Low Volatility High Dividend Index ETF (LVHD) invests in 111 stocks based on yield, price volatility, and earnings volatility, focusing on utilities and consumer staples. Despite its goal of low volatility, the ETF has underperformed the Russell 3000 and competitors, and its lower volatility doesn't mitigate drawdown risk effectively. This article updates a previous review of LVHD, analyzing its current holdings and recent performance.

title: Cisco Expands Partnership with NVIDIA to Accelerate AI Adoption in the Enterprise
summary: Cisco and NVIDIA announced an expanded partnership to accelerate AI adoption in enterprises by creating a cross-portfolio unified architecture for AI-ready data center networks. This collaboration will integrate Cisco Silicon One with NVIDIA Spectrum-X, making Cisco the only partner silicon included in NVIDIA Spectrum-X. The aim is to provide enterprises with flexibility and choice in AI infrastructure, simplifying deployment and optimizing performance for AI workloads.

title: Cisco, Rockwell partner to bridge India’s manufacturing skills gap with ‘Digital Skills for Industry’ program
summary: Cisco and Rockwell Automation have partnered to launch the ‘Digital Skills for Industry’ program in India, aiming to address the significant skills gap in the country's manufacturing sector. This initiative will empower future workers with essential digital skills across IT and OT, including cybersecurity, networking, IoT, data science, AI, programming, and automation. The program seeks to enhance digital literacy, attract new talent, and boost workforce productivity to help India achieve its goal of exporting USD 1 trillion in goods by 2030.

title: NVIDIA Announces Financial Results for Fourth Quarter and Fiscal 2025
summary: NVIDIA reported record financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025, driven by strong demand for its Data Center products, particularly for Blackwell AI supercomputers. The company announced record quarterly revenue of $39.3 billion and full-year revenue of $130.5 billion, with significant growth in Data Center revenue. NVIDIA also provided an optimistic outlook for the first quarter of fiscal 2026, highlighting continued advancements and partnerships across various sectors including AI, Gaming, Professional Visualization, Automotive, and Robotics.

title: Israel second most targeted country for cyberattacks
summary: Israel was the second most targeted country for cyberattacks in 2024, experiencing 1,550 attacks, according to Radware's annual cyber report. This marks an increase from fourth place in 2023, with a 550% rise in global Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks. Geopolitical conflicts and cloud migration are cited as key drivers behind this "revolution in the threat landscape."

title: Leaked ransomware chat logs reveal Black Basta’s targeted CVEs
summary: Researchers analyzed leaked chat logs from the Black Basta ransomware gang, identifying 62 unique CVEs, predominantly in widely adopted enterprise technologies like Microsoft products, Citrix Netscaler, and Atlassian Confluence. The findings highlight Black Basta's preference for known vulnerabilities with publicly available exploits and their strategy of targeting high-revenue companies in legal, financial, healthcare, and industrial sectors. This underscores the critical importance of prompt patching and mitigations for widely used applications and devices.

title: NetApp Stock Sinks After Data Storage Firm's Sales Miss Estimates
summary: NetApp (NTAP) stock declined after the company reported mixed fiscal third-quarter results. Earnings met expectations, but sales of $1.64 billion were below analyst estimates, and the company's guidance also fell short of Wall Street's consensus view.

title: Cisco provides a dose of service provider innovation at MWC 2025
summary: Cisco made significant announcements at MWC 2025, introducing innovations aimed at enhancing service provider capabilities through AI and automation. Key solutions include ThousandEyes Connected Devices for deeper network visibility into the "last mile," and an AI-enabled Provider Connectivity Assurance suite for real-time network analysis. Additionally, Cisco expanded its Mobility Services Platform with 5G Advanced Services readiness and a programmable core, fostering new monetization opportunities for telecommunication companies.

title: Data security in AI: Challenges and future strategies
summary: Jeetu Patel, Cisco Systems Inc.'s EVP and chief product officer, highlighted the critical need for robust data security in the rapidly evolving fields of AI, automation, and data analytics. He emphasized that companies must move beyond simply being 10% better to achieving a 10x improvement to stand out, especially in leveraging generative AI. The article underscores that collaboration between technology developers, businesses, and policymakers is essential for ethical and effective implementation of these advancements.

title: HPE boss optimistic Juniper deal will happen, Wi-Fi and all
summary: HPE CEO Antonio Neri remains optimistic about the company's acquisition of Juniper Networks, despite a lawsuit from the US Department of Justice to block the deal. Neri believes the DoJ has misunderstood the acquisition by narrowly focusing on the wireless local area network (WLAN) market, and he insists that divestment of Juniper's Mist Wi-Fi assets is "unacceptable." He argues the merger will create a strong competitor to Huawei outside the US and is confident in HPE's legal case, with a trial set for July 9.

title: Extreme Networks CEO On Win Rates Against Cisco And HPE And Using AI To Help Admins 'Cheat On Their Papers'
summary: Extreme Networks CEO Ed Meyercord discusses the company's "next-generation" AI strategy, including the new Extreme Platform One and MSP program, designed to simplify networking management and enhance partner support. He highlights improving win rates against competitors like Cisco and HPE, attributing this success to Extreme's differentiated AI-focused cloud networking approach and user-friendly platforms. Meyercord emphasizes how AI will accelerate partner training and enable greater automation for network administration, positioning Extreme for significant growth in 2025.

title: GM names its first-ever chief AI officer
summary: General Motors has appointed Barak Turovsky, a former Cisco and Google executive, as its first-ever Chief AI Officer. Turovsky will lead GM's AI strategy, reporting to Dave Richardson, SVP of software and services engineering. His role is to accelerate the integration of AI across GM's operations, particularly in EV, ICE, and autonomous vehicle development, with the aim of enhancing products, optimizing operations, and improving customer experience.

title: HPE cuts 2,500 jobs, remains committed to Juniper buy, faces tariff issues
summary: HPE announced it will lay off 2,500 employees over the next 18 months as part of a cost-cutting program, despite reporting a 16% increase in Q1 revenue for fiscal year 2025. The company also faces challenges including a higher-than-normal AI server inventory, a U.S. Justice Department lawsuit delaying its $14 billion acquisition of Juniper Networks, and potential impacts from U.S. government tariffs. HPE remains committed to the Juniper acquisition and plans to mitigate tariff effects through supply chain adjustments and pricing.

title: Extreme Networks scores next-gen Wi-Fi deal with United Soccer League
summary: Extreme Networks has partnered with the United Soccer League (USL) to provide next-generation Wi-Fi solutions and analytics, enhancing fan experiences and streamlining operations across its stadiums. The agreement includes the deployment of Extreme's Wi-Fi 7 solutions and ExtremeCloud Business Insights for Venues, offering advanced connectivity and actionable data. This collaboration aims to meet the evolving demands of modern sports environments and fan engagement.

title: Ciena isn’t too worried about tariffs right now
summary: Ciena is currently unconcerned about potential tariffs impacting its business, especially given its diversified supply chain across Canada, Mexico, India, and Thailand, though it acknowledges the fluid situation. The company is optimistic about continued growth driven by AI-related bandwidth demand, with CEO Gary Smith highlighting strong performance from service providers and cloud segments. Ciena's leadership believes the issues of excess equipment inventory are largely resolved, setting the stage for optical fiber's comeback.

title: Five9 appoints new president, Andy Dignan
summary: Five9, Inc. has promoted Andy Dignan to President, effective immediately. Dignan, who has been with Five9 since 2018 in various leadership roles, will receive a substantial compensation package including a base salary, annual bonus, and equity incentives. The company has recently reported strong financial results for Q4 2024, exceeding analyst expectations and demonstrating significant growth in AI adoption and subscription revenue.

title: X-ELIO closes six US PPAs with Cisco, Biogen, IDEXX, and Waters Corporation
summary: X-ELIO, a global renewable energy leader, has signed six virtual Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs) totaling 172 MW in the United States with major companies including Cisco Systems, Biogen, IDEXX Laboratories, and Waters Corporation. These 12-year agreements, facilitated by Sustainability Roundtable, Inc.'s Net Zero Consortium for Buyers (NZCB), are tied to X-ELIO's Star Dairy and Rosebud projects in Texas, which are expected to begin operations in 2027 and provide 367,000 MWh of clean energy annually. The PPAs reinforce the participating companies' sustainability goals and expand X-ELIO's presence in the US solar market.

title: X-Elio inks VPPAs with Cisco, Biogen, others for 172 MW of US solar
summary: X-Elio has signed multiple virtual power purchase agreements (VPPAs) with companies including Cisco and Biogen. These agreements will allocate 172 MW of solar power from X-Elio's projects in the United States. The deals aim to support corporate sustainability goals by providing renewable energy.

title: Former Splunk CEO, Cisco President Gary Steele To Take Helm Of Defense Upstart Shield AI
summary: Gary Steele, former CEO of Splunk and Cisco's president of go-to-market, is leaving Cisco to become the new CEO of Shield AI, a drone and defense technology startup. He will take over from co-founder Ryan Tseng, who will transition to a president role. Steele's appointment follows a $240 million funding round for Shield AI, increasing its valuation to $5.3 billion.

title: Judge rejects challenge to BlackRock target-date series telling plaintiffs 3 strikes and you’re out
summary: A judge has rejected a challenge against a BlackRock target-date series within the Cisco Systems 401(k) plan. This decision marks the third time the plaintiffs' arguments have been dismissed, effectively telling them "three strikes and you're out." The article highlights the judge's ruling concerning the BlackRock offerings, likely affirming their suitability or compliance.

title: LOS ANGELES 2028: Design and engineering giant AECOM joins LA28 as “Official Venue Infrastructure Partner”
summary: AECOM, a design and engineering firm, has been named the "Official Venue Infrastructure Partner" for the LA28 Olympic & Paralympic Games. This partnership formalizes AECOM's existing involvement with LA28, which dated back to the bid phase, and leverages their expertise in stadium design and construction, as exemplified by their work on the Intuit Dome. The agreement is a cash-and-services program, providing significant financial and technical benefits to LA28 without requiring the construction of new permanent venues.

title: Silicon Photonics Modules Market: Driving Innovation in High-Speed Communication with Intel, Cisco, IBM
summary: HTF Market Intelligence has published a comprehensive study on the Global Silicon Photonics Modules Market, forecasting its growth from USD 1.29 billion in 2022 at a CAGR of 25.8% from 2024 to 2032. The market, driven by high data demand and energy consumption reduction, focuses on integrating photonic devices onto silicon chips for high-speed data transmission in telecommunications and data centers. Key players include Cisco Systems, Intel Corporation, and IBM.

title: FatPipe Inc (FATN) IPO: Everything Investors Need to Know
summary: FatPipe Inc. (FATN), an enterprise networking technology company specializing in SD-WAN and hybrid WAN solutions, is preparing for its IPO on the Nasdaq Capital Market. The company plans to offer 740,740 shares at $5.75 to $7.75 per share, aiming to raise $4.26 million to $5.74 million to fund product development, sales expansion, and general corporate purposes. While FatPipe is not yet profitable, it operates in a growing market and aims to differentiate itself against larger competitors.

title: Cisco Paves the Way with Agentic AI Collaboration
summary: Cisco is enhancing its collaboration portfolio with new AI-powered solutions, including the Webex AI Agent, designed to transform traditional contact centers into dynamic customer experience centers. These innovations aim to improve customer and employee experiences by anticipating needs, automating tasks, and increasing productivity. Key features include the Webex AI Agent for 24/7 self-service and new functionalities within Cisco AI Assistant for Webex Contact Center, alongside tools for enhanced employee efficiency and IT management.

title: Cisco, Nvidia Partner On Joint Architecture For The Creation Of AI Data Centers
summary: Cisco and Nvidia have expanded their partnership by introducing "Cisco Secure AI Factory with Nvidia," an architecture designed to help enterprises build and secure data centers for AI workloads. This collaboration, building on a February announcement, aims to simplify AI data center development with security at its core. The offering will feature two deployment options—vertically integrated and modular—and incorporate Cisco's security solutions from its Isovalent acquisition.

title: Cisco to Deliver Secure AI Infrastructure with NVIDIA
summary: Cisco and NVIDIA are collaborating to create the "Cisco Secure AI Factory with NVIDIA," an architecture designed to simplify and secure the deployment and management of AI infrastructure for enterprises. This initiative integrates networking and security solutions, including Cisco Hypershield and AI Defense, to protect AI workloads, models, and applications. The partnership aims to address complex security challenges in AI factories, offering flexible deployment options with validated reference architectures.

title: DOJ’s Baseless Lawsuit Against HPE and Juniper Hurts Consumers
summary: This article argues that the Department of Justice's lawsuit to block the merger between Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) and Juniper Networks is baseless and harmful to consumers. The author, Mark Jamison, contends that the merger would enhance competition against market leader Cisco and foster innovation, rather than consolidate the market. He believes the lawsuit, initiated late in the Biden administration, is politically motivated and urges the new Trump administration's DOJ to withdraw it to support pro-growth and pro-competition policies.

title: Nokia Strengthens Home Broadband Offerings With New 5G Gateway, Wi-Fi 7 Support
summary: Nokia Corporation launched the FastMile Gateway 4, a compact 5G indoor gateway with Wi-Fi 7, designed to provide high-speed home connectivity. This new gateway optimizes radio capacity, enhances coverage, and maximizes throughput with features like high-gain antennas and 4-carrier aggregation. Nokia now offers four Wi-Fi 7 gateway models, aiming to enhance 5G FWA services and provide operators with a seamless 5G and Wi-Fi 7 experience for homes.

title: Chart Industries turns to NaaS to solve multicloud merger challenge
summary: After acquiring UK-based Howden, Chart Industries expanded its global multicloud sites from 40 to over 130. To efficiently manage this growth and simplify cloud deployment, Chart implemented Alkira's networking-as-a-service (NaaS) solution, which facilitated rapid integration of the acquired company's diverse cloud environments without requiring extensive staff upskilling. This approach allowed Chart's IT team to focus on strategic networking initiatives rather than the complexities of managing disparate cloud networking architectures across Azure, AWS, and Google Cloud.

title: Is Now The Time To Look At Buying Arista Networks Inc (NYSE:ANET)?
summary: Arista Networks Inc (NYSE:ANET) has experienced significant price movements, leading investors to question if its current trading price of US$83.13 reflects its true value. The stock appears fairly valued, trading slightly below its intrinsic value, with a positive outlook for profit growth of 62% over the next couple of years. While the future seems bright, investors are advised to consider other factors and potential market volatility.

title: Cisco Co-Authors Update to the NIST Adversarial Machine Learning Taxonomy
summary: Cisco, through its acquisition of Robust Intelligence, and the UK AI Security Institute collaborated with NIST to update the Adversarial Machine Learning Taxonomy. This update addresses the increasing sophistication of attacks on AI systems, particularly focusing on generative AI, and aims to standardize an approach to understanding and mitigating AI-related risks. The revised taxonomy includes new categories like misuse violations for generative AI and supply chain attacks, providing a more practical resource for AI security practitioners.

title: Data Center Hardware Market Size, Share & Trends Chart by 2033
summary: The global data center hardware market was valued at USD 74.6 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 157.5 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 8.7%. This growth is driven by the increasing demand for high-speed, low-latency processing, the rise of AI and machine learning, and the expansion of hyperscale data centers. North America currently leads the market, while Asia Pacific is expected to be the fastest-growing region.

title: Application Metrics & Monitoring Tools Market Trends 2025-2035
summary: The application metrics and monitoring tools market is projected to grow significantly from USD 12.88 billion in 2025 to USD 37.78 billion by 2035, driven by the adoption of cloud-native applications, AI-based observability solutions, and real-time performance monitoring. Key growth factors include the increasing complexity of IT environments, the explosion of IoT, and the rising demand for full-stack observability. The market will see a shift towards autonomous monitoring, self-healing infrastructure, and AI-powered predictive analytics, as companies like Datadog, New Relic, and Dynatrace continue to lead innovation.

title: Building Automation Systems Market Size | CAGR at 9.10%
summary: The global Building Automation Systems (BAS) market is projected to reach USD 183.2 billion by 2034, growing at a CAGR of 9.10% from USD 76.7 billion in 2024. This growth is driven by increasing emphasis on energy efficiency, sustainability, and the integration of IoT and AI technologies. Hardware currently dominates the market by product type, HVAC systems by application, and the commercial sector by end-user, with Europe holding the largest regional market share.

title: How Sustainable Is US Exceptionalism?
summary: US stocks have significantly outperformed global markets for 17 years, but an analysis suggests that much of this "exceptionalism" is due to expanding valuations and a strengthening dollar, rather than superior earnings growth. European policy changes, including fiscal stimulus and regulatory reforms, along with a declining share of the USD in global reserves, could shift this balance and favor international equities in the future. Investors are advised to consider international diversification to mitigate risk and capture potential opportunities.

title: Former Arista COO launches NextHop AI for customized networking infrastructure
summary: Anshul Sadana, former Arista COO, has launched NextHop AI, securing $110 million in funding to address the specialized networking demands of AI infrastructure at hyperscale. NextHop AI aims to provide highly customized networking solutions for the world’s largest cloud providers, compressing product development cycles, exploring multiple technological alternatives, and achieving crucial efficiency gains. The company is developing specialized network switches and custom software stacks beyond traditional networking to support massive AI compute clusters.

title: Cisco Talos report finds identity-based attacks drove majority of cyber incidents in 2024
summary: A new report from Cisco Talos indicates that identity-based attacks, leveraging valid credentials and old vulnerabilities, were responsible for 60% of cyber incidents in 2024. These attacks often exploited weaknesses in multi-factor authentication (MFA) and targeted Active Directory, with motivations ranging from ransomware to espionage. The report also noted that while AI use by threat actors was limited in 2024, it is expected to increase in 2025 across various attack phases and potentially target AI systems themselves.

title: Should You Buy the Invesco QQQ ETF During the Nasdaq Correction? History Offers a Clear Answer.
summary: The article analyzes whether investors should buy the Invesco QQQ ETF during a Nasdaq correction, arguing that historical trends suggest corrections are great buying opportunities for long-term growth. It highlights the ETF's exposure to top AI and tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, and Nvidia, and several less-noticed AI powerhouses. The author points out that despite current economic uncertainties and tariffs contributing to the correction, the Nasdaq-100 has historically recovered to new highs after every downturn since 1985.

title: Cisco’s 2025 Data Privacy Benchmark Study: Privacy landscape grows increasingly complex in the age of AI
summary: Cisco's 2025 Data Privacy Benchmark Study indicates that the privacy landscape is becoming increasingly complex, particularly with the rise of AI. While 86% of respondents support privacy legislation and 96% affirm the positive ROI of privacy investments, there's a growing internal conflict between the perceived safety of local data storage and the trust placed in global providers for data protection. title: $24.8 Bn Digital Circular Economy Market Opportunities and Strategies to 2034: SaaS Innovations Set to Revolutionize Waste Management and Boost Sustainability
summary: The global digital circular economy market is projected to grow from $2.9 billion in 2024 to $24.8 billion by 2034, driven by smart city initiatives and AI adoption. The report also highlights the market's fragmentation, with SAP SE holding the largest share among numerous small players, and outlines strategies for future growth, including SaaS solutions for waste management and leveraging AI and machine learning.

title: Here's How Much You'd Have If You Invested $1000 in Palo Alto Networks a Decade Ago
summary: This article examines the hypothetical return on a $1000 investment in Palo Alto Networks (PANW) made a decade ago, revealing it would be worth $6,554.46 today, a 555.45% gain. It details Palo Alto Networks' business model, growth drivers, competitive landscape, and future outlook, highlighting their strong performance against the S&P 500 and gold. The company benefits from continuous deal wins and increased adoption of its next-gen security platforms.

title: Pure//Accelerate: Pure Storage Introduces Enterprise Data Cloud
summary: Pure Storage introduced its Enterprise Data Cloud (EDC) at Pure//Accelerate, aiming to simplify data and storage management by enabling organizations to focus on business outcomes rather than infrastructure. The EDC architecture, fueled by AI, provides a unified control plane for managing data across on-premises, public cloud, and hybrid environments. Key innovations include Pure Fusion with workload automation, orchestrated workflows, and enhanced security integrations with Rubrik and CrowdStrike, alongside a new AI Copilot for personalized insights.

title: Arista Networks (ANET) Names Todd Nightingale as New President and Chief Operating Officer
summary: Arista Networks (ANET) has appointed Todd Nightingale as its new President and Chief Operating Officer, effective July 1, 2025. Nightingale, previously CEO of Fastly and a leader at Cisco, will receive a $350,000 annual salary along with significant stock compensation, including $30 million in RSUs and $2 million in PSUs. This strategic leadership addition is expected to boost Arista's innovation and operational efficiency as the company continues to report strong revenue growth.

title: Telecom Giant Viasat Is the Latest Salt Typhoon Victim
summary: Viasat, a satellite communications company, has become the latest victim of the cyber-espionage group Salt Typhoon. The breach, discovered earlier this year, is believed to be linked to the group's targeting of various telecom companies during the 2024 presidential campaign. Viasat asserts that its investigation found no evidence of customer impact and that the incident has been remediated.

title: Top Stock Reports for Amazon.com, Home Depot & Cisco Systems
summary: Zacks Investment Research has released new reports on 16 major stocks, including Amazon.com (AMZN), Home Depot (HD), and Cisco Systems (CSCO), highlighting their performance and outlook. The article also touches on smaller companies like United Homes Group and The Eastern Company, detailing their challenges and strategic efforts.

title: Direxion’s New ETFs Focus on Qualcomm and Cisco

summary: The private 5G market is projected to reach $17.55 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 35.4% from $3.86 billion in 2025. This growth is driven by the demand for high-speed, low-latency, and secure connectivity in various industries, with Radio Access Network (RAN) hardware holding the largest market share and the energy & utilities sector being the fastest-growing segment. The US is expected to dominate the North American market due to significant investments in digital infrastructure and Industry 4.0 solutions.

summary: Direxion has launched four new single-stock leveraged and inverse ETFs for Cisco Systems (NASDAQ: CSCO) and Qualcomm (NASDAQ: QCOM), designed for short-term trading. title: Time To Buy F5 Stock?
summary: F5 (FFIV) has recently introduced new post-quantum cryptography readiness solutions, integrating them into its Application Delivery and Security Platform, addressing the rising demand for cybersecurity. Despite a nearly 70% surge in its stock over the past twelve months, the article argues that FFIV remains an attractive purchase due to its moderate valuation, strong operating performance, and robust financial condition. While it appears slightly expensive compared to the broader market by some metrics, its improving profitability and strong balance sheet suggest potential for further growth.

title: Securing the Future: Data Security in Hyperscale AI-Ready Data Centers
summary: This article discusses the critical importance of data security in hyperscale AI-ready data centers, highlighting the unique challenges posed by their complexity, data sovereignty concerns, and AI-specific risks. It details innovations in real-time threat detection, including AI-driven monitoring, behavioral analytics, Zero Trust architecture, and predictive analytics. The piece also outlines Sify's comprehensive 10-tiered physical and electronic security framework, emphasizing their intelligence-driven approach to ensure data sovereignty and compliance.

title: HPE can finally take over Juniper after settling with the US government
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is now cleared to acquire Juniper Networks for $14 billion after reaching a settlement with the US Department of Justice. The lawsuit, which argued the acquisition would reduce market competition, was resolved less than two weeks before its scheduled start. HPE made concessions, including divesting its Instant On business and restricting access to Juniper's Mist AIOps technology, to assuage government concerns.

title: HPE finally gets green light to buy Juniper and take on Cisco in AI networking
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. has received approval for its $14 billion acquisition of Juniper Networks Inc. after settling a lawsuit with the U.S. Department of Justice. The agreement requires HPE to license Juniper's Mist AI Ops software source code and divest HPE’s Instant On wireless networking business. This strategic acquisition aims to bolster HPE's position in the AI networking market against rivals like Cisco Systems Inc.

title: HPE soars after DOJ clears Juniper Networks acquisition
summary: Shares of HP Enterprise surged 13% after the Department of Justice approved its $14 billion acquisition of Juniper Networks, ending a lengthy antitrust lawsuit. This merger will consolidate the enterprise wireless equipment market, giving the combined entity control of about 70%. Analysts anticipate the deal could boost HPE's earnings by 8% to 14% in 2026.

title: The HPE-Juniper Networks U.S. DOJ Settlement: Five Things You Need To Know
summary: The U.S. Department of Justice has settled the lawsuit regarding Hewlett Packard Enterprise's (HPE) $14 billion acquisition of Juniper Networks, paving the way for HPE to challenge Cisco in the AI networking market. The settlement requires HPE to license Juniper's Mist AI software to two bidders and divest its Instant On wireless networking business, concessions partners view as minor given the combined entity's strengthened portfolio. This acquisition is seen as a significant victory for HPE, its partners, and customers, offering a comprehensive AI networking solution from edge to data center.

title: The Top Session Border Controller Vendors in 2025: Securing Your Enterprise Voice
summary: This article identifies and reviews the top Session Border Controller (SBC) vendors for 2025, emphasizing their role in securing enterprise voice communications. It highlights how these solutions ensure call encryption, protocol translation across diverse UC platforms, and protection against suspicious traffic in hybrid and multi-platform environments. The article provides detailed insights into various vendors like AudioCodes, Ribbon, Oracle, Cisco, and Avaya, discussing their key offerings, deployment flexibility, security features, and integrations to help enterprises select the best SBC for their needs.

title: HPE Closes $13.4B Acquisition of Juniper Networks
summary: HPE has successfully completed its $13.4 billion acquisition of Juniper Networks, significantly doubling the size of its networking business. This acquisition positions HPE to better compete with Cisco and Nvidia in the data center and AI factory markets by offering an AI-native and cloud-driven IT portfolio. The deal, which was approved by Juniper shareholders in April 2024, aims to accelerate innovation, expand market reach, and provide customers with an end-to-end AI-native networking foundation.

title: Arista buys VeloCloud from Broadcom
summary: Arista Networks has acquired VeloCloud from Broadcom, which analysts say fills a significant gap in Arista's portfolio by adding software-defined WAN (SD-WAN) capabilities. This move allows Arista to offer a more comprehensive networking solution and prepares it to support AI workloads, particularly at the edge. The deal also benefits Broadcom by divesting VeloCloud, signaling a strategic focus on VMware Cloud Foundation and private cloud infrastructure over a broad networking solutions portfolio.

title: Better Dividend ETF to Buy for Passive Income: SCHD or GCOW
summary: This article compares two dividend ETFs, SCHD and GCOW, for investors seeking passive income. While both focus on high-yielding dividend stocks, they employ different selection strategies, leading to varied holdings, fees, and return profiles. The analysis concludes that SCHD is generally the better option due to its focus on dividend sustainability and growth, combined with a significantly lower expense ratio.

title: HPE closes on $14 billion Juniper acquisition
summary: HPE has successfully acquired Juniper Networks for $14 billion, following an agreement with the U.S. Department of Justice's Antitrust Division. This acquisition aims to position the combined entity as a leader in the AI and hybrid cloud market, significantly expanding HPE's networking business. The deal includes specific conditions from the DoJ, such as the divestment of HPE's Instant On WLAN business and the licensing of certain Juniper software to competitors, ensuring continued market competition.

title: Is It Time To Consider Buying F5, Inc. (NASDAQ:FFIV)?
summary: F5, Inc. (NASDAQ:FFIV) has seen a significant price increase recently, trading near its 52-week high, but is still considered fairly priced at 18% below its intrinsic value. The company's future outlook is optimistic, with earnings expected to grow by 38%, indicating strong cash flow and potential for higher share value. While the market seems to have priced in the positive outlook, investors are advised to consider other factors like management track record and balance sheet strength before making a decision, especially given the stock's high volatility.

title: GlobalFoundries buys MIPS for edge AI network dominion
summary: GlobalFoundries has announced its acquisition of chip design company MIPS, a move aimed at strengthening its position in AI-enabled network infrastructure, particularly for edge AI processing. MIPS' focus on RISC-V architecture and its Atlas portfolio for real-time, low-latency processing make it an attractive asset as the edge AI market is projected to reach $380 billion by 2028. The acquisition is expected to allow GlobalFoundries to offer custom chip solutions for smart routers, switches, and edge-network devices, with the deal anticipated to close in the second half of 2025.

title: Keymous+ blurs hacktivism & commerce amid 700 DDoS attacks
summary: Keymous+, a group identifying as "North African hackers," has claimed responsibility for over 700 DDoS attacks since late 2023 across various regions. While presenting itself as politically motivated, evidence suggests its "Beta Team" might be operating a commercial DDoS-for-hire service called EliteStress, blurring the lines between hacktivism and profit-driven operations. The group's diverse and seemingly random targeting, coupled with its strategic marketing and collaborations with other hacktivist entities, highlights evolving motivations in the cyber-activism landscape.

title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company (HPE): A Bull Case Theory
summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Hewlett Packard Enterprise Company (HPE) by Stock Region Research. The thesis highlights HPE's strategic focus on edge computing and hybrid cloud, particularly through its GreenLake platform, which integrates AI and machine learning capabilities. HPE is seen as well-positioned to capitalize on increasing enterprise IT budgets for AI and cloud initiatives.

title: Is Extreme Networks (NASDAQ:EXTR) Using Debt Sensibly?
summary: Extreme Networks (NASDAQ:EXTR) has a debt of US$180.3m, which is offset by US$185.5m in cash, resulting in a net cash position of US$5.18m. While the company recorded an EBIT loss and an 11% revenue drop to US$1.1b over the past year, its positive free cash flow of US$63m suggests manageable short-term balance sheet risk despite notable liabilities. The article concludes that given its net cash, Extreme Networks appears capable of managing its debt safely.

title: Is SCHD Good For Retirees?
summary: The Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (SCHD) is assessed for retirees, highlighting its pros such as a high dividend yield, impressive dividend growth, and low costs. Conversely, cons include less diversification than some retirees might prefer and returns that have lagged behind the S&P 500. Despite these drawbacks, the article concludes that SCHD is a good choice for retirees primarily focused on steady, reliable income, suggesting it can serve as a strong cornerstone investment when diversified within a broader retirement portfolio.

title: SA enterprises still vulnerable to DDOS attacks
summary: South African organizations remain highly vulnerable to sophisticated cyberattacks, particularly DDoS attacks, despite the country being a frequent target. Experts from Boston IT Solutions SA and Radware highlight that many companies rely solely on traditional firewalls, which are insufficient against modern application-layer threats. They emphasize the need for advanced solutions like web application firewalls and AI-powered security to protect critical sectors and customer data.

title: LVHD Can Provide Investors With Stability And Income (NASDAQ:LVHD)
summary: The Franklin U.S. Low Volatility High Dividend Index ETF (LVHD) offers a differentiated strategy focused on profitable U.S. companies with durable dividends, featuring diversification caps and a preference for defensive sectors. It has low fees, a 3.47% yield, and has outperformed peers over 1- and 5-year periods. The article recommends LVHD as a Buy with a 3-6% portfolio allocation due to its income potential, volatility management, and diversification.

title: Minimizing healthcare cyberattacks with network segmentation
summary: Network segmentation is an architectural strategy that divides a computer network into smaller, independent sections to enhance security, performance, and compliance, particularly crucial for healthcare organizations facing complex IT environments and high cyber risk. This approach helps contain cyberattacks, reduces the attack surface, and allows for risk-based security controls, supporting compliance with regulations like HIPAA. Implementing network segmentation requires careful consideration of budget, workflow implications, and potential challenges for resource-constrained entities, emphasizing the importance of risk analysis.

title: Splunk Named a Leader in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant for Observability Platforms for the Third Consecutive Year
summary: Splunk, a Cisco company, has been recognized as a Leader in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant for Observability Platforms for the third consecutive year. This recognition is attributed to Splunk's global presence, dedicated customer base, and continuous innovation in AI. The company, including its Splunk AppDynamics unit, emphasizes its commitment to helping customers achieve digital resilience and leading observability practices.

summary: Cisco is positioned to benefit significantly from the "sovereign AI" megatrend, which involves countries building out their own comprehensive AI infrastructures. This megatrend represents a massive growth opportunity, with the AI infrastructure market projected to grow nearly tenfold in less than a decade. Cisco's broad product offerings, including routers, switches, security, and data center solutions, make it a pivotal systems provider for countries aiming to develop their sovereign AI capabilities.

summary: Cisco is positioned to significantly benefit from the megatrend of sovereign artificial intelligence (AI), which involves countries building out their own AI infrastructure. (AOI) has confirmed its partnership with Charter Communications, supplying 1.8GHz amps, line extenders, and LoRaWAN-based remote management software for Charter's HFC network upgrades. The article highlights that businesses are cautious about fully adopting AI, preferring internal deployment before customer-facing applications, and emphasizes that differentiation through specialized vertical solutions remains an opportunity for new entrants.

title: Should iShares MSCI USA Value Factor ETF (VLUE) Be on Your Investing Radar?
summary: This article analyzes the iShares MSCI USA Value Factor ETF (VLUE), highlighting its structure as a passively managed large-cap value fund with assets over $6.79 billion. title: Lumentum Holdings Insiders Sell US$2.8m Of Stock, Possibly Signalling Caution
summary: Over the past year, insiders at Lumentum Holdings Inc. have sold US$2.8 million worth of stock, with no insider purchases reported. While insider selling can raise concerns, the company maintains a modest insider ownership of 1.1%, suggesting some alignment between management and shareholders.

summary: CyberArk's shares surged after The Wall Street Journal reported that Palo Alto Networks is in discussions to acquire the company for over $20 billion. The move highlights the increasing importance of cloud security amidst rising AI threats and ransomware attacks.

title: Rakuten Mobile names Cisco, Nokia, and F5 as key 5G SA partners
summary: Rakuten Mobile has partnered with Cisco, Nokia, and F5 to deploy its fully virtualized 5G Standalone (SA) network, leveraging a cloud-native, AI-automated core built on Rakuten Symphony's platform. Cisco will provide the mobile packet core, Nokia will contribute cloud-native network functions, and F5 will enhance API security and traffic management. This collaboration aims to boost Rakuten Mobile's network capabilities, simplify operations with AI, and drive innovation across Japan, aligning with its commitment to Open RAN and software-defined infrastructure.

title: Rakuten Mobile keeps Cisco with Nokia, F5 for long-awaited 5G SA network
summary: Rakuten Mobile has selected Cisco, Nokia, and F5 as partners for its 5G standalone (5G SA) mobile network deployment in Japan, running on its Symphony cloud-native platform. This decision surprised some analysts as previous indications suggested Rakuten might replace Cisco, but the company opted to retain its relationship with Cisco, along with long-standing partners Nokia and F5. While the choice of partners continues Rakuten's multivendor strategy, analysts noted the relatively slow pace of Rakuten's 5G SA rollout compared to competitors like SoftBank and NTT DoCoMo.

title: CL Earnings: All you need to know about Colgate-Palmolive’s Q2 2025 earnings results
summary: Colgate-Palmolive Company reported its Q2 2025 earnings, with net sales increasing 1% year-over-year to $5.1 billion and organic sales growth of 1.8%. Net income rose to $743 million, and adjusted EPS grew 1% to $0.92. For the full year 2025, the company expects low single-digit increases in net sales, organic sales growth (2-4% range), and both GAAP and adjusted EPS.

This strategic approach includes using autonomous drones for real-time insights and enhancing operational excellence.

title: SNIA Announces Storage.AI
summary: SNIA, a global non-profit organization specializing in data technologies, has launched Storage.AI, an open standards project aimed at creating efficient data services for AI workloads. This initiative brings together industry leaders such as AMD, Cisco, Dell, IBM, Intel, and others to address critical challenges like latency, power, and cost in AI data pipelines through vendor-neutral solutions. Storage.AI seeks to develop an open ecosystem that optimizes performance and cost-effectiveness for AI, leveraging SNIA's extensive experience in industry standards development.

title: Web Real-Time Communication Market Forecast 2025 to 2035
summary: The Web Real-Time Communication (WebRTC) solution market is projected to grow significantly from USD 19.39 billion in 2025 to USD 755.48 billion by 2035, at a CAGR of 44.2%. This growth is driven by the increasing adoption of AI-based communication, seamless video calling demand, and cloud-based collaboration software. Large enterprises and the IT & telecom sector are expected to dominate this market, with South Korea showing the fastest regional growth.

title: Cisco Reinvents Digital Resilience for the Agentic Era at Splunk .conf25
summary: Cisco announced it will host Splunk's annual .conf25 user conference from September 8–11, 2025, in Boston, focusing on how Splunk's integration empowers customers to leverage agentic AI for actionable insights from machine data. The event will feature new innovations in security and observability, product demonstrations, and customer success stories, with keynotes from Cisco and Splunk leaders on their AI-centered product vision. Attendees will have opportunities to engage in breakout sessions, explore services, network with peers and partners, and experience Splunk's solutions in action.

title: Extreme Networks CEO sees competitor disruption and its new AI platform driving growth
summary: Extreme Networks Inc. is experiencing significant growth driven by market recovery, competitor issues, and the launch of its new AI-driven networking platform, Extreme Platform One. The company plans aggressive enhancements to Platform One and foresees a fundamental shift in networking paradigms with AI.

title: The Week Ahead: Earnings from miners, U.S. inflation data due
summary: This article outlines the key economic data releases and corporate earnings reports scheduled for the week of August 11-15. It highlights upcoming earnings from several mining companies and other sectors, alongside important U.S. and Canadian economic indicators like the U.S. Consumer Price Index and Canadian retail sales.

title: Aligning our prices and packaging with the problems we help customers solve
summary: Cloudflare is introducing two new product bundles, Externa and Interna, to simplify its offerings and align pricing with customer value. Externa focuses on connecting and protecting public-facing infrastructure, offering fair and predictable pricing without "attack traffic tax." Interna addresses internal private systems, providing a single per-seat price that covers users anywhere and contributes to a shared bandwidth pool for corporate WANs.

title: Cisco’s growing AI demand sparks earnings beat, but the stock is falling
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. beat revenue and earnings expectations for its fiscal 2025 fourth quarter, driven by stronger-than-anticipated AI infrastructure orders. Despite this performance and positive outlook on AI opportunities, the company's stock initially fell about 2% in after-hours trading before recovering. The full-year forecast provided was consistent with analyst models.

title: Cisco Reports Fourth Quarter Earnings
summary: Cisco reported strong fourth-quarter and fiscal year 2025 results, with Q4 revenue of $14.7 billion (up 8% year over year) and FY 2025 revenue of $56.7 billion (up 5% year over year). The company saw significant demand for AI infrastructure, exceeding $800 million in Q4 AI orders from webscale customers and over $2 billion for the fiscal year. Cisco also provided guidance for Q1 FY 2026 and full-year FY 2026, anticipating continued revenue growth and profitability.

title: Cisco rides AI wave to upgrade guidance
summary: Cisco Systems Inc. (NASDAQ:CSCO) has raised its first-quarter revenue outlook, surpassing market expectations due to a significant increase in demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure. The company reported AI-related orders exceeding $800 million in the final quarter, bringing the fiscal 2025 total to over $2 billion, more than double its original target. This growth is driven by major tech players expanding capacity to meet AI demand and new partnerships for sovereign AI projects.

title: Cisco Earnings: We Raise Our AI Forecast After Strong Results and Guidance
summary: Morningstar has raised its fair value estimate for Cisco stock to $61 from $56 after the company delivered strong fourth-quarter results and positive fiscal 2026 guidance. The revision is primarily driven by an increased five-year AI forecast, as AI revenue reached $1 billion in fiscal 2025 and is expected to grow significantly, becoming a substantial contributor to Cisco's networking business. While AI is a key growth driver, Morningstar notes that valuation is challenging at current levels.

title: Wait for a Dip to Buy Monday.com Stock
summary: Monday.com (MNDY) shares recently plunged 30% despite an earnings beat, due to conservative guidance and volatility warnings. Investors are advised to wait for a better entry point, and alternatively, consider Trefis's diversified portfolios as a less volatile option.

title: I Like Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO) Because Of Its AI Business, Says Jim Cramer
summary: Jim Cramer discussed Cisco Systems, Inc. (CSCO), highlighting its strong AI business and refresh cycle as positives, despite recent stock performance declines linked to security segment issues. Cramer interviewed Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins, who addressed concerns about AI's impact on employment, emphasizing agentic (non-human) job functions. The company also reportedly surpassed its AI infrastructure orders target, indicating strong growth in that area.

title: Should You Buy, Sell or Hold CyberArk Stock After a 27.2% YTD Rise?

Despite its strong prospects and projected earnings growth, CyberArk faces stiff competition and trades at a premium valuation, leading to a "Hold" recommendation for current investors.

title: AOI boosts cable amp sales as it prepares to jump into the node biz
summary: Applied Optoelectronics (AOI) reported a significant increase in 1.8GHz amplifier sales in Q2 2025, primarily driven by strong demand from Charter Communications, and is on track to achieve $300 million to $350 million in cable-related revenues for the full year. The company is also preparing to enter the cable node business in Q4 2025 with products based on Broadcom's "unified" DOCSIS 4.0 silicon, positioning itself as a direct competitor to other cable technology providers. This strategic shift follows AOI's transition from an original design manufacturer to directly supplying cable operators with its own branded products.

title: Fabrinet tanks after warning of supply chain issues in its Nvidia-linked business

title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Septerna, Inc. (SEPN) and Cisco Systems (CSCO)

This acquisition aims to provide real-time insights into encrypted network traffic without performance compromise, using MantisNet's Containerized Visibility Fabric. The integration will enable F5 customers to gain deeper understanding of encrypted sessions and automate policy enforcement for improved security and compliance.

Palermo brings over two decades of IT leadership experience, including a significant tenure at Red Hat where he championed SaaS model transformation and advanced hybrid cloud initiatives. His role at Trimble will focus on connecting technology and data to enable faster insights and scalable solutions across the enterprise.

title: Cisco to Participate in August 2025 Deutsche Bank Technology Conference
summary: Cisco Systems, Inc. announced its participation in the 2025 Deutsche Bank Technology Conference. The event will feature a fireside chat with Cisco's EVP & Chief Financial Officer and President & Chief Product Officer, and management will also hold investor meetings. No new financial information will be discussed during the conference.

title: Broadcasting Equipment Market Size Worth USD 10.48 Billion by 2033, Rising at 8.29% CAGR from 2026, Driven by Technological Advancements, Digital Media Expansion - Market Research Intellect
